questions.title: 2018 Q1 — RCP Shaft Shear at 25% Power
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 1 is at 25% Reactor Power and rising due to a plant startup

At time 17:00:00

• The 13 RCP shaft shears

At 17:00:30, which ONE of the following completes the statements below?

The Reactor is _(1)_.

Normal full PZR spray capability _(2)_ available.

choices.label: A
choices.html: (1) tripped — (2) is NOT

choices.label: B
choices.html: (1) tripped — (2) is

choices.label: C
choices.html: (1) at power — (2) is NOT

choices.label: D
choices.html: (1) at power — (2) is

questions.title: 2018 Q2 — Mixed Bed Demineralizer Malfunction
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is at 95% Reactor Power and stable

• The Rod Bank Selector Switch is in MANUAL

• The 21 MIXED BED DEMINERALIZER is in service

At time 20:00:00

• 2CC71 (Letdown Heat Exchanger Component Cooling Water Outlet Control Valve) malfunctions causing the letdown temperature to rise and stabilize at 130 °F

Which ONE of the following completes the statements below?

When in service, the mixed bed demineralizer is used to _(1)_.

As the 21 MIXED BED DEMINERALIZER heats up, RCS TAVG will INITIALLY _(2)_.

choices.label: A
choices.text: (1) provide Lithium control and help maintain RCS pH — (2) rise
choices.html: (1) provide Lithium control and help maintain RCS pH — (2) rise

choices.label: B
choices.text: (1) provide Lithium control and help maintain RCS pH — (2) lower
choices.html: (1) provide Lithium control and help maintain RCS pH — (2) lower

choices.label: C
choices.text: (1) remove chemical impurities from the RCS — (2) rise
choices.html: (1) remove chemical impurities from the RCS — (2) rise

choices.label: D
choices.text: (1) remove chemical impurities from the RCS — (2) lower
choices.html: (1) remove chemical impurities from the RCS — (2) lower

questions.title: 2018 Q3 — RHR Suction and Discharge Alignment
questions.stemText:
Given:

Which ONE of the following completes the statement below?

The Residual Heat Removal System Pumps take suction from the _(1)_ Hot Leg and can be aligned to discharge into the _(2)_ Hot Legs.

choices.label: A
choices.html: (1) 21 — (2) 21 and 22

choices.label: B
choices.html: (1) 21 — (2) 23 and 24

choices.label: C
choices.html: (1) 22 — (2) 21 and 22

choices.label: D
choices.html: (1) 22 — (2) 23 and 24

questions.title: 2018 Q4 — Cold Leg Recirculation with SJ44 Failure
questions.stemText:
Given:

• A LBLOCA has occurred

• The crew is performing 2-EOP-LOCA-3 (Transfer To Cold Leg Recirculation)

• 21SJ44 (Containment Sump Suction Valve) can NOT be opened

Which ONE of the following completes the statements below?

The crew was required to realign ECCS valves for cold leg recirculation as soon as the RWST Level _(1)_ alarm was FIRST validated.

With 21SJ44 closed, _(2)_ RHR Pump(s) will be able to draw suction from the Containment Sump and discharge ECCS flow into the RCS cold legs and to the suctions of the SI Pumps and Centrifugal Charging Pumps.

choices.label: A
choices.html: (1) LO — (2) ONLY 22

choices.label: B
choices.html: (1) LO — (2) BOTH

choices.label: C
choices.html: (1) LO-LO — (2) ONLY 22

choices.label: D
choices.html: (1) LO-LO — (2) BOTH

questions.title: 2018 Q5 — SI Actuation Timing and PZR Heater De-energization
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is at 80% Reactor Power and stable

• 21 Charging Pump is in service

• PZR Pressure and Level are lowering

• Containment Pressure is rising

At time 11:00

• PZR Pressure is 1864 psig and lowering

• Containment Pressure is 4.1 psig

At time 11:08

• PZR Pressure is 1764 psig and lowering

• Containment Pressure is 5.2 psig

Which ONE of the following completes the statements below?

_(1)_ is the earliest time that an AUTOMATIC Safety Injection occurred.

The PZR Heaters will de-energize _(2)_.

choices.label: A
choices.html: (1) 11:00 — (2) based PZR low level

choices.label: B
choices.html: (1) 11:00 — (2) when Safety Injection is actuated

choices.label: C
choices.html: (1) 11:08 — (2) based PZR low level

choices.label: D
choices.html: (1) 11:08 — (2) when Safety Injection is actuated

questions.stemText:

• Cooling of the PRT is required

Which ONE of the following completes the statements below?

Cooling of the PRT can be initiated MANUALLY _(1)_.

During PRT Cooling, the PRT is drained directly to the _(2)_.

choices.label: A

choices.label: B
choices.text: (1) or AUTOMATICALLY — (2) RCDT pump suction header
choices.html: (1) or AUTOMATICALLY — (2) RCDT pump suction header

choices.label: C
choices.html: (1) ONLY — (2) RCDT

choices.label: D
choices.html: (1) ONLY — (2) RCDT pump suction header

questions.title: 2018 Q7 — CCW Radiation Monitor and Surge Tank Overflow
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is in MODE 3

At time 15:00:00

• BOTH 2R17A/B (Component Cooling Process Monitors) have just come into HI alarm

At time 15:01:00

• The crew has also initiated S2.OP-AB.CC-0001 (Component Cooling Abnormality) and S2.OP-AB.RAD-0001 (Abnormal Radiation)

At time 15:15:00

• Chemistry has verified a rise in Component Cooling activity

Which ONE of the following completes the statements below?

At 15:00:30, 2CC149 (Surge Tank Vent Valve) is _(1)_.

If CCW Surge Tank overflows, then the in-service Waste Holdup Tank _(2)_ will become contaminated.

choices.label: A
choices.text: (1) open — (2) and the 22 ABV Exhaust Filter Unit
choices.html: (1) open — (2) and the 22 ABV Exhaust Filter Unit

choices.label: B
choices.html: (1) open — (2) ONLY

choices.label: C
choices.text: (1) closed — (2) and the 22 ABV Exhaust Filter Unit
choices.html: (1) closed — (2) and the 22 ABV Exhaust Filter Unit

choices.label: D
choices.html: (1) closed — (2) ONLY

questions.title: 2018 Q8 — PZR Saturation and RCP Restart [EDITED]
questions.stemText:
Given:

• The crew is attempting to maintain the PZR water saturated so 13 RCP can be restarted in accordance with 1-EOP-TRIP-6 (Natural Circulation Rapid Cooldown Without RVLIS) Step 1

• PZR Pressure is 2005 psig

• PZR Liquid Temperature is 635 °F

Which ONE of the following completes the statements below?

The PZR is _(1)_.

In accordance with the 1-EOP-TRIP-6, the reason for establishing and maintaining saturation conditions in the PZR is to limit the PZR pressure _(2)_ upon RCP restart.

choices.label: A
choices.html: (1) subcooled — (2) rise

choices.label: B
choices.html: (1) subcooled — (2) reduction

choices.label: C
choices.html: (1) water saturated — (2) rise

choices.label: D
choices.html: (1) water saturated — (2) reduction

At 12:00:30, PZR PORVs (2PR1 and 2PR2) are _(2)_.

choices.label: B

choices.label: C
choices.html: (1) fully open — (2) open

choices.label: D
choices.html: (1) fully open — (2) closed

CH ICH IICH IIICH IV

2235 PSIG0 PSIG2235 PSIG2235 PSIG2235 PSIG

With 2PT-456 failed low, the coincidence for the PZR Pressure LOW Reactor Trip (based on the remaining functional PZR Pressure Channels) is now ___

choices.label: B

questions.stemText:
Given:

Consider the following Containment Cooling configurations with a DBA occurring inside containment:

2. ONLY ONE CS Pump operating plus ONLY 3 CFCUs operating

3. ONLY ONE CS Pump operating plus ONLY 2 CFCUs operating

4. ONLY 5 CFCUs operating

Which ONE of the following completes the statements below?

___ are the MINIMUM Containment Cooling components required to be in operation that will maintain Containment Pressure, Temperature and Humidity within design limits with a DBA occurring inside containment.

choices.label: A
choices.html: 1 or 3

choices.label: B
choices.html: 1 or 4

choices.label: C
choices.html: 2 or 4

choices.label: D
choices.html: 2 or 3

questions.title: 2018 Q16 — Main Steam Warmup MSIV Opening
questions.stemText:
Given:

- The crew is performing S2.OP-SO.MS-0001 (Main, Reheat, Turbine Bypass Steam Warmup) and is preparing to open 21-24MS167 (Main Steam Isolation Valves)

In accordance with S2.OP-SO.MS-0001, which ONE of the following completes the statements below?

The reason that 21-24MS7 (Main Steam Loop Drain Valves) are opened before 21-24MS167 is to _(1)_.

The crew will open 21-24MS167 when there is less than a MAXIMUM of _(2)_ psid across each valve.

choices.label: A
choices.text: (1) prevent pressurized steam from forcing residual water in the piping to cause water hammer on downstream components    (2) 15
choices.html: (1) prevent pressurized steam from forcing residual water in the piping to cause water hammer on downstream components    (2) 15

choices.label: B
choices.text: (1) prevent pressurized steam from forcing residual water in the piping to cause water hammer on downstream components    (2) 50
choices.html: (1) prevent pressurized steam from forcing residual water in the piping to cause water hammer on downstream components    (2) 50

choices.label: C
choices.text: (1) remove any collected corrosion products or impurities to ensure Main Turbine blading is not impinged    (2) 15
choices.html: (1) remove any collected corrosion products or impurities to ensure Main Turbine blading is not impinged    (2) 15

choices.label: D
choices.text: (1) remove any collected corrosion products or impurities to ensure Main Turbine blading is not impinged    (2) 50
choices.html: (1) remove any collected corrosion products or impurities to ensure Main Turbine blading is not impinged    (2) 50

choices.label: A

choices.html: (1) IMMEDIATELY    (2) the pumps to rotate backward

choices.label: B
choices.text: (1) IMMEDIATELY    (2) vapor binding in the pumps
choices.html: (1) IMMEDIATELY    (2) vapor binding in the pumps

choices.label: C
choices.text: (1) between 30 to 60 minutes    (2) the pumps to rotate backward
choices.html: (1) between 30 to 60 minutes    (2) the pumps to rotate backward

choices.label: D
choices.text: (1) between 30 to 60 minutes    (2) vapor binding in the pumps
choices.html: (1) between 30 to 60 minutes    (2) vapor binding in the pumps

questions.title: 2018 Q87 — Loss of RHR in Mode 5 / RCS Inventory Loss
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is in MODE 5

• 21 RHR Loop is in service

At time 17:00

At time 17:05 (5 minutes later)

• PZR level is 9% and lowering

• 2R11A (Containment Particulate Monitor) reading is rising

In accordance with S2.OP-AB.RHR-0001, which ONE of the following completes the statement below?

At 17:05, the crew will _(1)_.

choices.html: IMMEDIATELY GO TO S2.OP-AB.LOCA-0001 (Shutdown LOCA)

choices.label: B
choices.text: perform S2.OP-AB.RHR-0001 Attachment 9 (Steam Generator Reflux Cooling)
choices.html: perform S2.OP-AB.RHR-0001 Attachment 9 (Steam Generator Reflux Cooling)

choices.label: C
choices.text: IMMEDIATELY GO TO S2.OP-AB.RHR-0002 (Loss of RHR at Reduced Inventory)
choices.html: IMMEDIATELY GO TO S2.OP-AB.RHR-0002 (Loss of RHR at Reduced Inventory)

choices.label: D
choices.text: START Safety Injection and Charging Pumps as required and CONTROL Pressurizer level between 5% and 50% while maintaining RHR System in service
choices.html: START Safety Injection and Charging Pumps as required and CONTROL Pressurizer level between 5% and 50% while maintaining RHR System in service

Starting 23 RCP when the start criteria are NOT met will result in _(2)_.

choices.text: (1) at least one of the (2) degradation of the RCP motor due to a larger motor starting current

choices.label: D
choices.text: (1) at least one of the (2) an RCS pressure transient caused by energy addition from the secondary system

questions.title: 2019 Q69 — SG Tube Leak Subcooling Calculation
questions.stemText:

questions.stemText:
Given:

___ is the MINIMUM load reduction rate at which a reactor trip is required.

questions.stemText:
Given:

In accordance with S2.OP-AB.CR-0001, which ONE of the following completes the statements below?

After the Control Room has been evacuated and in accordance with S2.OP-AB.CR-0001 Attachment 6 (Plant Operator), RCS pressure will be maintained by cycling PZR Heaters _(2)_.

choices.label: A

choices.label: B

choices.label: C
choices.text: (1) THROTTLE 21 and 22 AFW Pump Steam Generator Inlet Valves (2) and PZR Spray Valves

choices.label: D

choices.html: (1) THROTTLE 21 and 22 AFW Pump Steam Generator Inlet Valves (2) ONLY

choices.label: C

questions.title: 2019 Q79 — LOOP / EDG Trip / TS 3.8.1.1 LCO 3.0.4.b
questions.stemText:

At time 02:00

At time 02:10

At time 04:00

• Offsite power has been restored

• ALL independent AC circuits between the offsite transmission network and the onsite Class 1E distribution system are OPERABLE

• Maintenance estimates that the 2A EDG will be repaired within the next 24 hours

Which ONE of the following completes the statement below?

At 02:00, 2A EDG tripped when Engine Lube Oil Header Low Pressure FIRST lowered below _(1)_ psig.

In accordance with Technical Specifications, Unit 2 _(2)_.

choices.label: B

choices.html: (1) 40 (2) can NOT go to MODE 3 until 2A EDG is restored to OPERABLE status

choices.label: C
choices.text: (1) 60 (2) can go to MODE 3 ONLY if a risk assessment is performed
choices.html: (1) 60 (2) can go to MODE 3 ONLY if a risk assessment is performed

choices.label: D

choices.html: (1) 60 (2) can NOT go to MODE 3 until 2A EDG is restored to OPERABLE status

questions.stemText:

At time 10:00 on January 7th

The crew will transition to _(1)_ if the required RCS Cooldown Rate is greater than a MINIMUM of _(2)_ °F / Hour.

NOTE:

• 2-EOP-TRIP-5 (NATURAL CIRCULATION RAPID COOLDOWN WITH RVLIS)

• 2-EOP-TRIP-6 (NATURAL CIRCULATION RAPID COOLDOWN WITHOUT RVLIS)

choices.label: A
choices.text: START ONLY ONE Hydrogen Recombiner by performing S1.OP-SO.CAN-0001 (HYDROGEN RECOMBINER OPERATION) CONCURRENTLY with 1-EOP-LOCA-1
choices.html: START ONLY ONE Hydrogen Recombiner by performing S1.OP-SO.CAN-0001 (HYDROGEN RECOMBINER OPERATION) CONCURRENTLY with 1-EOP-LOCA-1

choices.label: B
choices.text: START BOTH Hydrogen Recombiners by performing S1.OP-SO.CAN-0001 (HYDROGEN RECOMBINER OPERATION) CONCURRENTLY with 1-EOP-LOCA-1
choices.html: START BOTH Hydrogen Recombiners by performing S1.OP-SO.CAN-0001 (HYDROGEN RECOMBINER OPERATION) CONCURRENTLY with 1-EOP-LOCA-1

choices.label: C
choices.text: CONTINUE in 1-EOP-LOCA-1 until Containment Hydrogen concentration reaches 4.0%
choices.html: CONTINUE in 1-EOP-LOCA-1 until Containment Hydrogen concentration reaches 4.0%

choices.label: D
choices.text: CONSULT TSC for additional recovery actions and REMAIN in 1-EOP-LOCA-1
choices.html: CONSULT TSC for additional recovery actions and REMAIN in 1-EOP-LOCA-1

questions.title: 2019 Q92 — Loss of Condenser Vacuum Turbine Trip
questions.stemText:
Given:

• The crew is performing S2.OP-AB.COND-0001 (LOSS OF CONDENSER VACUUM) due to a malfunction of the Condenser Air Removal system

• With Condenser Backpressure degrading, Unit 2 Turbine Load is being lowered in an attempt maintain condenser backpressure within the Allowable Operating Region of S2.OP-AB.COND-0001 Attachment 4, Condenser Absolute Pressure Limits

At time 19:00

• Condenser Backpressure has just exceeded the limit of S2.OP-AB.COND-0001 Attachment 4

• Unit 2 is at 45% Reactor Power

At 19:00 and in accordance with S2.OP-AB.COND-0001, which ONE of the following completes the statement below?

The crew will TRIP the _(1)_.

After the Reactor/Turbine is tripped, the crew will _(2)_.

choices.label: A
choices.text: (1) Reactor (2) NOT perform S2.OP-AB.COND-0001 concurrently with 2-EOP-TRIP-1, Reactor Trip Or Safety Injection
choices.html: (1) Reactor (2) NOT perform S2.OP-AB.COND-0001 concurrently with 2-EOP-TRIP-1, Reactor Trip Or Safety Injection

choices.label: B
choices.text: (1) Reactor (2) perform S2.OP-AB.COND-0001 concurrently with 2-EOP-TRIP-1, Reactor Trip Or Safety Injection
choices.html: (1) Reactor (2) perform S2.OP-AB.COND-0001 concurrently with 2-EOP-TRIP-1, Reactor Trip Or Safety Injection

choices.label: C
choices.text: (1) Turbine (2) NOT perform S2.OP-AB.COND-0001 concurrently with S2.OP-AB.TRB-0001, Turbine Trip Below P-9
choices.html: (1) Turbine (2) NOT perform S2.OP-AB.COND-0001 concurrently with S2.OP-AB.TRB-0001, Turbine Trip Below P-9

choices.label: D
choices.text: (1) Turbine (2) perform S2.OP-AB.COND-0001 concurrently with S2.OP-AB.TRB-0001, Turbine Trip Below P-9
choices.html: (1) Turbine (2) perform S2.OP-AB.COND-0001 concurrently with S2.OP-AB.TRB-0001, Turbine Trip Below P-9

questions.title: 2019 Q93 — Loss of Control Air Reactor Trip
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 1 is at 25% Power and stable

• The crew is performing S1.OP-AB.CA-0001 (Loss Of Control Air) due to a leak on the cross connect line between Station Air and the Unit 1 Control Air

• 1A Control Air Header Pressure is 79 psig

• 1B Control Air Header Pressure is 77 psig

In accordance with S1.OP-AB.CA-0001, which ONE of the following completes the statements below?

The crew's NEXT action will be to

choices.label: A
choices.text: CHECK ANY Excess Flow Check Valve closed per S1.OP-AB.CA-0001 Attachment 5 (EXCESS FLOW CHECK VALVE TABLE)
choices.html: CHECK ANY Excess Flow Check Valve closed per S1.OP-AB.CA-0001 Attachment 5 (EXCESS FLOW CHECK VALVE TABLE)

choices.label: B
choices.text: TRIP the Reactor and perform 1-EOP-TRIP-1 (Reactor Trip Or Safety Injection) CONCURRENTLY with S1.OP-AB.CA-0001 Attachment 6 (ABNORMAL COOLING WATER LINEUPS)
choices.html: TRIP the Reactor and perform 1-EOP-TRIP-1 (Reactor Trip Or Safety Injection) CONCURRENTLY with S1.OP-AB.CA-0001 Attachment 6 (ABNORMAL COOLING WATER LINEUPS)

choices.label: C
choices.text: INITIATE a plant shutdown per S1.OP-IO.ZZ-0004 (POWER OPERATION) while CONCURRENTLY performing S1.OP-AB.CA-0001 Attachment 12 (LOCAL CONTROL OF SG PRESSURE AND LEVEL)
choices.html: INITIATE a plant shutdown per S1.OP-IO.ZZ-0004 (POWER OPERATION) while CONCURRENTLY performing S1.OP-AB.CA-0001 Attachment 12 (LOCAL CONTROL OF SG PRESSURE AND LEVEL)

choices.label: D
choices.text: TRIP the Reactor and perform 1-EOP-TRIP-1 (Reactor Trip Or Safety Injection) CONCURRENTLY with S1.OP-AB.CA-0001 Attachment 12 (LOCAL CONTROL OF SG PRESSURE AND LEVEL)
choices.html: TRIP the Reactor and perform 1-EOP-TRIP-1 (Reactor Trip Or Safety Injection) CONCURRENTLY with S1.OP-AB.CA-0001 Attachment 12 (LOCAL CONTROL OF SG PRESSURE AND LEVEL)

questions.title: 2019 Q94 — STA Staffing Requirements
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Units 1 and 2 are at 100% Power

• John Doe is a licensed SRO who is also a qualified STA

In accordance with Technical Specifications, which ONE of the following completes the statements below?

John Doe _(1)_ fill the STA minimum staffing requirement on Unit 1 and Unit 2 concurrently.

John Doe _(2)_ fill the STA minimum staffing requirement on Unit 2 while concurrently filling the SRO minimum staffing requirement on Unit 2.

(1)(2)

choices.label: A
choices.html: (1) may NOT (2) may

choices.label: B
choices.html: (1) may NOT (2) may NOT

choices.label: C
choices.html: (1) may (2) may

choices.label: D
choices.html: (1) may (2) may NOT

questions.title: 2019 Q95 — PORV Block Valve Configuration Control
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is at 100% Power

At time 17:55

• An LCO 3.4.5 (RELIEF VALVES) REQUIRED ACTION has been entered due to a failure of the 2PR2 (PZR PORV) control circuitry.

• 2PR7 (PZR PORV BLOCK VALVE) will be closed to comply with LCO 3.4.5 (RELIEF VALVES) REQUIRED ACTION B

Which ONE of the following completes the statement below?

In accordance with LCO 3.4.5 and after 2PR7 has been closed, power will be _(1)_ to 2PR7 and in accordance with OP-AA-108-101-1002 (COMPONENT CONFIGURATION CONTROL), configuration control will be INITIALLY maintained with a(n) _(2)_.

choices.label: A
choices.html: (1) maintained (2) Work Clearance Document (WCD)

choices.label: B
choices.html: (1) maintained (2) Abnormal Component Position Sheet (ACPS)

choices.label: C
choices.html: (1) removed (2) Work Clearance Document (WCD)

choices.label: D
choices.html: (1) removed (2) Abnormal Component Position Sheet (ACPS)

questions.title: 2019 Q96 — Radiation Monitoring LCO 3.3.3.1
questions.stemText:
Given:

REFERENCE PROVIDED

• Unit 2 is at 100% Reactor Power and stable

• The crew is performing S2.OP-SO.CBV-0002 (Containment Pressure - Vacuum Relief System Operation) Section 5.3, "Performing a Containment Vacuum Relief"

• 2R12A (CNTMT NOBLE GAS) and 2R41D (PLANT VENT NOBLE GAS RELEASE RATE) have BOTH just failed

In accordance with Technical Specifications, which ONE of the following completes the statement below?

LCO 3.3.3.1 (RADIATION MONITORING INSTRUMENTATION) is __

choices.label: A
choices.text: NOT met and the crew must perform Action 24 ONLY
choices.html: NOT met and the crew must perform Action 24 ONLY

choices.label: B
choices.text: NOT met and the crew must perform Action 26 ONLY
choices.html: NOT met and the crew must perform Action 26 ONLY

choices.label: C
choices.text: NOT met and the crew must perform Actions 24 and 26
choices.html: NOT met and the crew must perform Actions 24 and 26

choices.label: D
choices.text: met because redundant radiation monitors are OPERABLE
choices.html: met because redundant radiation monitors are OPERABLE

questions.title: 2019 Q97 — Emergency Exposure PEEL
questions.stemText:
Given:

• A General Emergency exists at Unit 2

• The SM has turned over Emergency Coordinator duties to the EDO

In accordance with NC.EP-EP.ZZ-0304 (OPERATIONAL SUPPORT CENTER (OSC) RADIATION PROTECTION RESPONSE), which ONE of the following completes the statements below?

The Planned Emergency Exposure Limit (PEEL) for life saving is _(1)_ REM.

The EDO _(2)_ can approve the emergency exposure.

choices.label: A
choices.html: (1) 25 (2) ONLY

choices.label: B
choices.html: (1) 25 (2) or SM

choices.label: C
choices.html: (1) 75 (2) ONLY

choices.label: D
choices.html: (1) 75 (2) or SM

questions.title: 2019 Q98 — Locked High Radiation Area IV Waiver
questions.stemText:
Given:

• An NEO has signed onto a RWP to perform an Independent Verification (IV) on a manual gate valve located inside a Locked High Radiation Area (LHRA)

• RP estimates that the operator would receive a dose of 15 mrem while performing the IV

Which ONE of the following completes the statements below?

In accordance with RP-AA-460 (CONTROLS FOR HIGH AND VERY HIGH RADIATION AREAS), an LHRA has a MINIMUM dose rate of _(1)_ mrem/hr @ 30 cm.

In accordance with OP-AA-108-101-1002 (COMPONENT CONFIGURATION CONTROL) and based on the estimated dose of 15 mrem to perform the IV, the Shift Manager is _(2)_ to waive the "Hands On" IV of the manual gate valve.

choices.label: A
choices.html: (1) 100 (2) NOT permitted

choices.label: B
choices.html: (1) 100 (2) permitted

choices.label: C
choices.html: (1) 1000 (2) NOT permitted

choices.label: D
choices.html: (1) 1000 (2) permitted

questions.title: 2019 Q99 — CR Evacuation SI Defeat
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is at 100% Reactor Power and stable

At time 02:00:00

• The crew enters S2.OP-AB.CR-0001 (Control Room Evacuation)

At time 02:01:00

• The Control Room has been evacuated and ALL of the Immediate Actions of S2.OP-AB.CR-0001 were successfully completed

• There is NO damage to the plant

In accordance with S2.OP-AB.CR-0001, which ONE of the following completes the statements below?

The SM will direct Safety Injection to be defeated by ___

choices.label: A
choices.text: inserting a jumper to P-11 and, before leaving the control room, placing SEC Block switches in Block per S2.OP-AB.CR-0001 Attachment 5 (REACTOR OPERATOR)
choices.html: inserting a jumper to P-11 and, before leaving the control room, placing SEC Block switches in Block per S2.OP-AB.CR-0001 Attachment 5 (REACTOR OPERATOR)

choices.label: B
choices.text: opening vital instrument bus breakers to de-energize SSPS and the SEC cabinets per S2.OP-AB.CR-0001 Attachment 7 (#1 NEO)
choices.html: opening vital instrument bus breakers to de-energize SSPS and the SEC cabinets per S2.OP-AB.CR-0001 Attachment 7 (#1 NEO)

choices.label: C
choices.text: inserting a jumper for P-11 and opening vital instrument bus breakers to de-energize SECs S2.OP-AB.CR-0001 Attachment 5 (REACTOR OPERATOR)
choices.html: inserting a jumper for P-11 and opening vital instrument bus breakers to de-energize SECs S2.OP-AB.CR-0001 Attachment 5 (REACTOR OPERATOR)

choices.label: D
choices.text: opening vital instrument bus breakers to de-energize SSPS and before leaving the control room, placing the SEC Block switches in Block per S2.OP-AB.CR-0001 Attachment 7 (#1 NEO)
choices.html: opening vital instrument bus breakers to de-energize SSPS and before leaving the control room, placing the SEC Block switches in Block per S2.OP-AB.CR-0001 Attachment 7 (#1 NEO)

questions.title: 2019 Q100 — Emergency PAR Default
questions.stemText:
Given:

REFERENCE PROVIDED

At time 08:00

• The Shift Manager has declared a SITE AREA EMERGENCY per FB1.L and RB1.L, "Loss of the Fuel Clad Barrier and Loss of Reactor Coolant System Barrier"

At time 08:45

• Operational conditions have deteriorated

• The Shift Manager/Emergency Director has declared a GENERAL EMERGENCY per FB1.L, RB1.L and CB1.L, "Loss of the Fuel Clad Barrier, Loss of Reactor Coolant System Barrier and Loss Of Containment Barrier" (13 PTS)

• R44A/B (Containment High Range Area Monitors) are reading 1000 R/hr

At time 08:55

• The Shift Manager/Emergency Director has completed the Initial Contact Message Form (ICMF) and the form has been verified by the STA

At time 09:50

• A wind shift has occurred and the SM is evaluating the need for a possible Protective Action Recommendation (PAR) Upgrade

Which ONE of the following completes the statements below?

At 08:55, the correct INITIAL PAR on the ICMF should be the _(1)_ PAR.

Based on the wind shift at 09:50, the SM will declare _(2)_ PAR Upgrade.

choices.label: A
choices.html: (1) Default (2) NO

choices.label: B
choices.html: (1) Default (2) an RPSA

choices.label: C
choices.html: (1) RPSA (2) an RPSA

choices.label: D
choices.html: (1) RPSA (2) NO

questions.title: 2020 Q1 — Reactor Trip Status Panel
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is at 60% Power.

The following sequence of events occurs:

• A Main Turbine trip occurs.

• OHA F-36, TURB TRIP & P-9, is illuminated.

• One of the Turbine Stop Valve Closed indicating lights on 2RP4 is flashing.

The flashing light on 2RP4 indicates that …
questions.questionSource: Bank – Salem Vision Database, modification made to stem only

choices.label: A
choices.text: the valve has left its open seat, but has NOT fully closed.
choices.html: the valve has left its open seat, but has NOT fully closed.

choices.label: B
choices.text: SSPS Train "A" and SSPS Train "B" disagree as to the position of the valve.
choices.html: SSPS Train "A" and SSPS Train "B" disagree as to the position of the valve.

choices.label: C
choices.text: EHC fluid pressure to the valve operator remains above 45 psig.
choices.html: EHC fluid pressure to the valve operator remains above 45 psig.

choices.label: D
choices.text: the solenoid-operated dump valve in the EHC fluid supply line has NOT de-energized.
choices.html: the solenoid-operated dump valve in the EHC fluid supply line has NOT de-energized.

questions.title: 2020 Q2 — PORV Discharge Temperature
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is in MODE 3.

• RCS Pressure is 2235 psig

• PZR Power Operated Relief Valve (PORV) 2PR1 is leaking.

• Pressurizer Relief Tank (PRT) pressure is 5 psig.

• PORV discharge temperature has stabilized at 230 °F.

Which ONE of the following will directly cause the indicated PORV discharge temperature to LOWER?

choices.label: A
choices.text: PRT rupture disk develops a leak.
choices.html: PRT rupture disk develops a leak.

choices.label: B
choices.text: PRT pressure is allowed to rise to 10 psig.
choices.html: PRT pressure is allowed to rise to 10 psig.

choices.label: C
choices.text: RCS pressure is reduced to 2000 psig.
choices.html: RCS pressure is reduced to 2000 psig.

choices.label: D
choices.html: PORV leakrate rises by 5 gpm.

questions.title: 2020 Q3 — SBLOCA Subcooling / Steam Tables
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 has experienced a Small Break LOCA with a Loss of Off-Site Power.

• A fault developed on the 2A 4KV Bus and the Bus is de-energized.

• All available ECCS Pumps are currently running.

• Containment Pressure is currently 4.3 psig.

• RCS Pressure is currently 1035 psig.

• The crew has transitioned to 2-EOP-LOCA-2, Post LOCA Cooldown and Depressurization.

• The crew is implementing "Charging Pump Reduction" major action of EOP-LOCA-2.

• Based on operability concerns with the Subcooling Margin Monitor, the CRS has directed the RO to determine subcooling requirements using Steam Tables.

Which ONE of the following choices is the MAXIMUM RCS (Core Exit) temperature that will allow stopping one Charging Pump in accordance with step 21 of 2-EOP-LOCA-2?

[REFERENCES PROVIDED]

questions.title: 2020 Q4 — RCP Motor Winding Temperature
questions.stemText:
Given:

Which ONE of the following would be an indication of the potential for damage to a Reactor Coolant Pump and require tripping the Reactor Coolant Pump in accordance with S2.OP-AB.RCP-0001, Reactor Coolant Pump Abnormality, Attachment 2, Stopping Reactor Coolant Pumps.
questions.questionSource: Modified – Vision Database & Salem 2004 NRC Exam

choices.label: A
choices.text: Closure of CC-131, RCP Thermal Barrier Valve for > 2 minutes.
choices.html: Closure of CC-131, RCP Thermal Barrier Valve for > 2 minutes.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Reactor Coolant Pump Motor Bearing Temperature of 170°F.
choices.html: Reactor Coolant Pump Motor Bearing Temperature of 170°F.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Reactor Coolant Pump Motor Flange Vibration of 3 mils.
choices.html: Reactor Coolant Pump Motor Flange Vibration of 3 mils.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Reactor Coolant Pump Motor Winding Temperature of 320°F.
choices.html: Reactor Coolant Pump Motor Winding Temperature of 320°F.

questions.title: 2020 Q5 — Excess Letdown / SI Isolation
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is at 100% Power.

• Normal Letdown has been isolated due to a problem with the Letdown Pressure Control Valve, 2CV18.

• Excess Letdown has been established to the VCT in accordance with S2.OP-SO.CVC-0003, Excess Letdown Flow.

Immediately following a Safety Injection (SI) signal, Excess Letdown will __(1)__.

Note: 2CV278, Excess Letdown Isolation Valve

   2CV131, Excess Letdown Isolation Valve

   2CV284, Seal Return Isolation Valve

   2CV116, Seal Return Isolation Valve

choices.label: A
choices.text: Continue to flow to the RCDT due to seal return relief valve CV115 cycling following the automatic closure of CV116 and CV284.
choices.html: Continue to flow to the RCDT due to seal return relief valve CV115 cycling following the automatic closure of CV116 and CV284.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Continue to flow to the PRT due to seal return relief valve CV115 cycling following the automatic closure of CV278 and CV131.
choices.html: Continue to flow to the PRT due to seal return relief valve CV115 cycling following the automatic closure of CV278 and CV131.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Continue to flow to the RCDT due to seal return relief valve CV115 cycling following the automatic closure of CV278 and CV131.
choices.html: Continue to flow to the RCDT due to seal return relief valve CV115 cycling following the automatic closure of CV278 and CV131.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Continue to flow to the PRT due to seal return relief valve CV115 cycling following the automatic closure of CV116 and CV284.
choices.html: Continue to flow to the PRT due to seal return relief valve CV115 cycling following the automatic closure of CV116 and CV284.

questions.title: 2020 Q6 — Shutdown LOCA
questions.stemText:
Given:

• 21 RHR Pump is in service aligned for Shutdown Cooling.

• RCS Temperature is 325 °F.

• RCS Pressure is 300 psig and lowering.

• PZR Level is 10% and lowering rapidly.

• Containment Pressure is 0.1 psig.

• 2R41D, Plant Vent, radiation monitor is rising.

Which ONE of the following identifies the required procedure and action to mitigate this event?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Enter S2.OP-AB.RHR-0001, Loss of RHR, Isolate letdown and Start Safety Injection and Charging Pumps as required to Control Pressurizer Level between 5% and 50%.
choices.html: Enter S2.OP-AB.RHR-0001, Loss of RHR, Isolate letdown and Start Safety Injection and Charging Pumps as required to Control Pressurizer Level between 5% and 50%.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Enter S2.OP-AB.RC-0001, Reactor Coolant System Leak, Isolate Letdown and Start one Charging or Safety Injection Pump to maintain Pressurizer Level between 11% and 50%.
choices.html: Enter S2.OP-AB.RC-0001, Reactor Coolant System Leak, Isolate Letdown and Start one Charging or Safety Injection Pump to maintain Pressurizer Level between 11% and 50%.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Enter S2.OP-AB.LOCA-0001, Shutdown LOCA, Stop the operating RHR Pump aligned for Shutdown Cooling and Close 2RH1 and 2RH2 (RHR Common Suction).
choices.html: Enter S2.OP-AB.LOCA-0001, Shutdown LOCA, Stop the operating RHR Pump aligned for Shutdown Cooling and Close 2RH1 and 2RH2 (RHR Common Suction).

choices.label: D
choices.text: Enter S2.OP-AB.RC-0001, Reactor Coolant System Leak, Stop the operating RHR Pump aligned for Shutdown Cooling and close 21SJ49 (RHR Discharge to Cold Legs).
choices.html: Enter S2.OP-AB.RC-0001, Reactor Coolant System Leak, Stop the operating RHR Pump aligned for Shutdown Cooling and close 21SJ49 (RHR Discharge to Cold Legs).

questions.title: 2020 Q7 — PZR PORV Fails Open
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is at 100% Power.

• 2PR1, Pressurizer PORV, fails opens while in Automatic and cannot be closed in Manual from the control console in accordance with S2.OP-AB.PZR-0001, Pressurizer Pressure Malfunction.

In accordance with S2.OP-AB.PZR-0001, which ONE of the following identifies the NEXT required action, and if pressure continues to lower, what subsequent action is taken and why?

choices.label: A
choices.text: OPEN the associated control power breaker. IF AT ANY TIME RCS pressure drops to 2000 psig and continues to drop, THEN: TRIP the Reactor. Manually tripping the reactor at 2000 psig and decreasing, to prevent an automatic trip from OTΔT.
choices.html: OPEN the associated control power breaker. IF AT ANY TIME RCS pressure drops to 2000 psig and continues to drop, THEN: TRIP the Reactor. Manually tripping the reactor at 2000 psig and decreasing, to prevent an automatic trip from OTΔT.

choices.label: B
choices.text: CLOSE the associated block valve. IF AT ANY TIME RCS pressure drops to 2000 psig and continues to drop, THEN: TRIP the Reactor. Manually tripping the reactor at 2000 psig and decreasing, to prevent an automatic trip from OTΔT.
choices.html: CLOSE the associated block valve. IF AT ANY TIME RCS pressure drops to 2000 psig and continues to drop, THEN: TRIP the Reactor. Manually tripping the reactor at 2000 psig and decreasing, to prevent an automatic trip from OTΔT.

choices.label: C
choices.text: CLOSE the associated block valve. IF AT ANY TIME RCS pressure drops to 1900 psig and continues to drop, THEN: TRIP the Reactor. Manually tripping the reactor at 1900 psig and decreasing, to prevent an automatic trip from Low RCS Pressure.
choices.html: CLOSE the associated block valve. IF AT ANY TIME RCS pressure drops to 1900 psig and continues to drop, THEN: TRIP the Reactor. Manually tripping the reactor at 1900 psig and decreasing, to prevent an automatic trip from Low RCS Pressure.

choices.label: D
choices.text: OPEN the associated control power breaker. IF AT ANY TIME RCS pressure drops to 1900 psig and continues to drop, THEN: TRIP the Reactor. Manually tripping the reactor at 1900 psig and decreasing, to prevent an automatic trip from Low RCS Pressure.
choices.html: OPEN the associated control power breaker. IF AT ANY TIME RCS pressure drops to 1900 psig and continues to drop, THEN: TRIP the Reactor. Manually tripping the reactor at 1900 psig and decreasing, to prevent an automatic trip from Low RCS Pressure.

questions.title: 2020 Q8 — ATWS Turbine Trip (POTENTIALLY OUTDATED)
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 was at 100% Power when a trip of both Steam Generator Feed Pumps occurred.

The following sequence of events occurs:

• ALL SG NR LO-LO level setpoints are reached, but the reactor failed to trip.

• ALL attempts to trip the reactor from the control room were unsuccessful and the reactor trip is NOT confirmed.

• The crew enters 2-EOP-TRIP-1, Reactor Trip or Safety Injection.

Complete the following statements:

In accordance with 2-EOP-TRIP-1, which one of the following is the NEXT required action and why?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Trip the Turbine to maintain steam generator inventory.
choices.html: Trip the Turbine to maintain steam generator inventory.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Start 21 and 22 AFW Pumps to maintain steam generator inventory.
choices.html: Start 21 and 22 AFW Pumps to maintain steam generator inventory.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Initiate rod insertion to insert negative reactivity.
choices.html: Initiate rod insertion to insert negative reactivity.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Initiate rapid boration to insert negative reactivity.
choices.html: Initiate rapid boration to insert negative reactivity.

questions.title: 2020 Q9 — MSLB Containment Cooling
questions.stemText:
Given:

• A design bases Main Steam Line Break coincident with a Loss of Off-Site Power has occurred on Unit 2.

The PO reviewing the OHAs and Control Console Bezel Alarms recognizes and announces the following event relevant alarms:

• J-3, 2C 4KV VTL BUS DIFF PROT

• C-6, CNTMT PRESS HI-HI

• C-5, 21 CFCU WTRFLO TRBL

• BEZEL 1-2, 21 CFCU AIR FLOW LO

The PO verifies 21SW58 (21 CFCU Inlet) and 21SW72 (21 CFCU Outlet) service water valves are OPEN.

Which ONE of the following completes the statements below?

Containment cooling design bases configuration __(1)__ met. This is because ____(2)___.

choices.label: A
choices.text:
(1) is
(2) 21, 23, 25 CFCUs and 21 Containment Spray Pump are operating.
choices.html:
(1) is
(2) 21, 23, 25 CFCUs and 21 Containment Spray Pump are operating.

choices.label: B
choices.text:
(1) is NOT
(2) 23, 25 CFCUs and 22 Containment Spray Pump are operating.
choices.html:
(1) is NOT
(2) 23, 25 CFCUs and 22 Containment Spray Pump are operating.

choices.label: C
choices.text:
(1) is NOT
(2) 22, 24 CFCUs and 21 Containment Spray Pump are operating.
choices.html:
(1) is NOT
(2) 22, 24 CFCUs and 21 Containment Spray Pump are operating.

choices.label: D
choices.text:
(1) is
(2) 22, 24 CFCUs and both 21 & 22 Containment Spray Pumps are operating.
choices.html:
(1) is
(2) 22, 24 CFCUs and both 21 & 22 Containment Spray Pumps are operating.

questions.title: 2020 Q10 — Loss of MFW / Dry SG Feed Restriction
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 was at 100% Power when a Loss of all Main Feedwater occurred.

• Operators have not been able to initiate Auxiliary Feedwater (AFW) and have transitioned to 2-EOP-FRHS-1, Response to Loss of Secondary Heat Sink.

• Bleed and Feed has been initiated.

• Core Exit Temperatures (CETs) are now LOWERING.

• All SG Wide Range (WR) Levels are 7 % and stable.

Subsequently, feed flow capability has just been restored and the crew has returned to the major action step for "SECONDARY HEAT SINK RESTORATION".

Based on the above conditions and In accordance with 2-EOP-FRHS-1, complete the statement below concerning the limitation when restoring feed flow and why?

Feed one SG…

choices.label: A
choices.text: at maximum flowrate to prevent lifting a PZR Safety Relief Valve.
choices.html: at maximum flowrate to prevent lifting a PZR Safety Relief Valve.

choices.label: B
choices.text: at maximum flowrate to prevent a severe challenge to the Core Cooling CFST.
choices.html: at maximum flowrate to prevent a severe challenge to the Core Cooling CFST.

choices.label: C
choices.text: between 1E04 and 5E04 lbm/hr to prevent thermal shocking SG tubes.
choices.html: between 1E04 and 5E04 lbm/hr to prevent thermal shocking SG tubes.

choices.label: D
choices.text: between 1E04 and 5E04 lbm/hr to prevent thermal shock of the reactor pressure vessel.
choices.html: between 1E04 and 5E04 lbm/hr to prevent thermal shock of the reactor pressure vessel.

questions.title: 2020 Q11 — EDG Loading Limits for PZR Heaters
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 was at 100% Power when a Loss of Off-Site Power occurred.

• Operators have transitioned to 2-EOP-TRIP-2, Reactor Trip Response.

• All Emergency Diesel Generators are loaded onto their respective 4KV Vital Buses.

The CRS has directed you to energize the 21 PZR Backup Heaters from the 2C Vital Bus in accordance with the station operating procedure.

A procedural caution states the following; "Aligning pressurizer heaters to vital bus adds approximately 210 KW to bus load".

The 2C diesel is currently loaded to the 2000 hour limit. In order to add the PZR heater load of 210 KW and NOT exceed the 2000 hour limit, the current 2C diesel loading must be adjusted to no greater than ______.

questions.title: 2020 Q12 — Loss of VIB VCT Level and Overpressure Protection
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 was at 100% Power when a Loss of the 2A 115V Vital Instrument Bus occurred.

• The CRS has entered S2.OP-AB.115-0001, Loss of 2A Vital Instrument Bus.

The following Volume Control Tank (VCT) components and indications have been affected:

• LT-112, VCT Level - Loss of indication and alarms.

• PI-139, VCT Pressure - Loss of indication.

• The VCT makeup system will be unavailable for normal makeup.

The crew has restored normal letdown in accordance with the procedure and has selected 2CV35 to MANUAL FLOW TO VCT.

Complete the following statements:

The 2LT-114 VCT level can be monitored in the control room using the _(1)_. If VCT level continues to rise above 77%, over-pressurization protection of the VCT is provided by _(2)_.

choices.label: A
choices.text:
_(1)_ plant computer
_(2)_ LT-114 automatically diverting the 2CV35 to the CVCS HUT
choices.html:
_(1)_ plant computer
_(2)_ LT-114 automatically diverting the 2CV35 to the CVCS HUT

choices.label: B
choices.text:
_(1)_ plant computer
_(2)_ 2CV241, VCT relief valve, relieving to the CVCS HUT.
choices.html:
_(1)_ plant computer
_(2)_ 2CV241, VCT relief valve, relieving to the CVCS HUT.

choices.label: C
choices.text:
_(1)_ control console
_(2)_ LT-114 automatically diverting the 2CV35 to the CVCS HUT.
choices.html:
_(1)_ control console
_(2)_ LT-114 automatically diverting the 2CV35 to the CVCS HUT.

choices.label: D
choices.text:
_(1)_ control console
_(2)_ 2CV241, VCT relief valve, relieving to the CVCS HUT.
choices.html:
_(1)_ control console
_(2)_ 2CV241, VCT relief valve, relieving to the CVCS HUT.

questions.title: 2020 Q13 — Loss of 2A 125 VDC Bus Equipment Impact
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is at 100% Reactor Power

At time 16:00

• OHA B-2, "2A 125 VDC CNTRL BUS VOLT LO" actuates

• 2A Vital 125 VDC Bus voltage is reading 0 VDC on 2RP9

Considering ONLY the following equipment malfunctions below:

1. #1 SGFP Emergency Oil Pump loses power

2. Main Turbine Emergency Oil Pump loses power

3. 2A EDG is NOT available for start

Which ONE of the following completes the statement below?

With a confirmed 2A Vital 125 VDC Bus voltage of 0 VDC, Unit 2 will experience equipment malfunction(s) ______.

choices.label: A
choices.html: 3 ONLY

choices.label: B
choices.html: 2 ONLY

choices.label: C
choices.html: 1 and 2 ONLY

choices.label: D
choices.html: 2 and 3 ONLY

questions.title: 2020 Q14 — Service Water Bay Leak and Tech Spec
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is at 100% Power.

• 24 Service Water Pump is Cleared & Tagged for a silt inspection.

• 21, 25, and 26 Service Water Pumps are in service.

• 23 Service Water Pump is in AUTO.

The following OHA alarms are received:

• B-13, 21 SW HDR PRESS LO

• B-14, 22 SW HDR PRESS LO

• B-29, 21-23 SW PUMP SMP AREA LVL HI

Which Abnormal Operating Procedure will isolate the identified service water leak, and once the leak is isolated, what Technical Specification LCO is applicable?

choices.label: A
choices.text: S2.OP-AB.SW-0001, Loss of Service Water Header Pressure; enter Tech Spec 3.7.4 for one INOPERABLE SW loop.
choices.html: S2.OP-AB.SW-0001, Loss of Service Water Header Pressure; enter Tech Spec 3.7.4 for one INOPERABLE SW loop.

choices.label: B
choices.text: S2.OP-AB.SW-0003, Service Water Bay Leak; enter Tech Spec 3.0.3 for two INOPERABLE SW loops.
choices.html: S2.OP-AB.SW-0003, Service Water Bay Leak; enter Tech Spec 3.0.3 for two INOPERABLE SW loops.

choices.label: C
choices.text: S2.OP-AB.SW-0003, Service Water Bay Leak; enter Tech Spec 3.7.4 for one INOPERABLE SW loop.
choices.html: S2.OP-AB.SW-0003, Service Water Bay Leak; enter Tech Spec 3.7.4 for one INOPERABLE SW loop.

choices.label: D
choices.text: S2.OP-AB.SW-0001, Loss of Service Water Header Pressure; enter Tech Spec 3.0.3 for two INOPERABLE SW loops.
choices.html: S2.OP-AB.SW-0001, Loss of Service Water Header Pressure; enter Tech Spec 3.0.3 for two INOPERABLE SW loops.

questions.title: 2020 Q15 — Loss of Control Air ECAC Actions
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is at 100% Power.

• The crew recognizes degrading Control Air Header pressures.

• The CRS enters S2.OP-AB.CA-0001, Loss of Control Air.

• 2A Control Air Header pressure is 93 psig.

• 2B Control Air Header pressure is 88 psig.

Note: ECAC = Emergency Control Air Compressor

In accordance with S2.OP-AB.CA-0001, what action is required, and if control air (CA) header pressure continues to LOWER, what subsequent action will be required?
questions.questionSource: Modified Bank - Salem 16-01 NRC Exam - Q61

choices.label: A
choices.text: Start #2 ECAC and if BOTH CA headers lowers to < 80 psig then Trip the Reactor.
choices.html: Start #2 ECAC and if BOTH CA headers lowers to < 80 psig then Trip the Reactor.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Notify Unit 1 to start #1 ECAC and if EITHER CA headers lowers to < 80 psig then Trip the Reactor.
choices.html: Notify Unit 1 to start #1 ECAC and if EITHER CA headers lowers to < 80 psig then Trip the Reactor.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Start #2 ECAC and if EITHER CA headers lowers to < 80 psig then Trip the Reactor.
choices.html: Start #2 ECAC and if EITHER CA headers lowers to < 80 psig then Trip the Reactor.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Notify Unit 1 to start #1 ECAC and if BOTH CA headers lowers to < 80 psig then Trip the Reactor.
choices.html: Notify Unit 1 to start #1 ECAC and if BOTH CA headers lowers to < 80 psig then Trip the Reactor.

questions.title: 2020 Q16 — LOCA Outside Containment Indication
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 has experienced a LOCA.

• RCS Pressure is 1300 psig.

• Total ECCS Injection Flow is 400 gpm.

• The CRS has transitioned from 2-EOP-TRIP-1, Reactor Trip or Safety Injection, to 2-EOP-LOCA-6, LOCA Outside Containment.

In accordance with 2-EOP-LOCA-6, what indication is specifically checked to ensure successful leak isolation and allow exit from the procedure?

choices.label: A
choices.html: RCS Pressure rising.

choices.label: B
choices.html: PZR Level rising.

choices.label: C
choices.html: Dynamic Range RVLIS Level rising.

choices.label: D
choices.html: RCS Subcooling > 0 °F.

questions.title: 2020 Q17 — Loss of Emergency Recirculation Mitigation Strategies
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 has experienced a design bases Large Break LOCA coincident with a Loss of Off-Site Power.

• 2B Emergency Diesel Generator tripped.

• 21 RHR Pump tripped.

• The CRS has transitioned to 2-EOP-LOCA-5, Loss of Emergency Recirculation.

In accordance with 2-EOP-LOCA-5, which of the following mitigation strategies are applicable based on CURRENT plant conditions and will NEED to be implemented?

1. Run All CFCUs in High Speed.

2. Minimize SI Flow to minimum for adequate decay heat removal.

3. Depressurize the RCS to minimize RCS subcooling.

4. Make up to the RWST.

choices.label: A
choices.html: 1, 2, 3, and 4 Only.

choices.label: B
choices.html: 2, 3, and 4 Only.

choices.label: C
choices.html: 1 and 4 Only.

choices.label: D
choices.html: 2 and 4 Only.

questions.title: 2020 Q18 — FRHS-1 RCS Pressure vs SG Pressure Check
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 has experienced a Reactor Trip from 100% Power.

• Safety Injection has immediately actuated on low RCS Pressure.

• ALL Auxiliary Feed Pumps have been lost and can't be recovered.

• The CRS has transitioned to 2-EOP-FRHS-1, Response to Loss of Secondary Heat Sink at step 20 of 2-EOP-TRIP-1, Reactor Trip or Safety Injection.

2-EOP-FRHS-1 Step 3 (Step 1 in current EOP rev) asks; "IS RCS PRESSURE GREATER THAN ANY INTACT OR RUPTURED SG PRESSURE?"

Which ONE of the following actions is required if the operator answers NO and why?
questions.questionSource: Modified Bank - Salem 2008 NRC Exam, Q23

choices.label: A
choices.text: Check if RCS T-Hots are < 350 °F and Place RHR in Service. If RCS temperature is low enough to place the RHR System in service, then the RHR System is an alternate heat sink to the secondary system.
choices.html: Check if RCS T-Hots are < 350 °F and Place RHR in Service. If RCS temperature is low enough to place the RHR System in service, then the RHR System is an alternate heat sink to the secondary system.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Return to Procedure in Effect. When the RCS depressurizes below the intact SG pressures, for larger LOCA break sizes, the secondary heat sink is not required and actions to restore secondary heat sink are not necessary.
choices.html: Return to Procedure in Effect. When the RCS depressurizes below the intact SG pressures, for larger LOCA break sizes, the secondary heat sink is not required and actions to restore secondary heat sink are not necessary.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Continue attempts to Restore Auxiliary Feed Water flow and if 3/4 SG WR Levels are less than 20% (25% Adverse), initiate Bleed and Feed. There is no decay heat removal through the Steam Generators and the RED Path requiring transition to 2-EOP-FRHS-1 is still valid.
choices.html: Continue attempts to Restore Auxiliary Feed Water flow and if 3/4 SG WR Levels are less than 20% (25% Adverse), initiate Bleed and Feed. There is no decay heat removal through the Steam Generators and the RED Path requiring transition to 2-EOP-FRHS-1 is still valid.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Trip all RCPs and immediately transition to 2-EOP-LOCA-1, Loss of Reactor Coolant. To prevent further loss of reactor coolant through the LOCA, since a LOOP later in the event could cause a more severe loss of coolant or two-phase RCS flow.
choices.html: Trip all RCPs and immediately transition to 2-EOP-LOCA-1, Loss of Reactor Coolant. To prevent further loss of reactor coolant through the LOCA, since a LOOP later in the event could cause a more severe loss of coolant or two-phase RCS flow.

questions.title: 2020 Q19 — Dropped Rod Step Counter Reset
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 reactor power was reduced to 74% Power.

• A dropped Control Bank D Group 1 rod is being recovered in accordance with S2.OP-AB.ROD-0002, Dropped Rod.

Shortly before the crew starts withdrawing the dropped rod, S2.OP-AB.ROD-0002 directs the crew to reset the control bank D group 1 step counter to zero.

Why does the affected group step counter need to be reset to zero?

choices.label: A
choices.text: This prevents OHA E-8 RIL LO and E-16 RIL LO-LO alarms from coming in during rod recovery.
choices.html: This prevents OHA E-8 RIL LO and E-16 RIL LO-LO alarms from coming in during rod recovery.

choices.label: B
choices.text: This prevents a OHA E-40 ROD BANK URGENT FAILURE alarm from coming in during rod recovery.
choices.html: This prevents a OHA E-40 ROD BANK URGENT FAILURE alarm from coming in during rod recovery.

choices.label: C
choices.text: This ensures that the step counter matches actual rod position and the rod is withdrawn to the proper height.
choices.html: This ensures that the step counter matches actual rod position and the rod is withdrawn to the proper height.

choices.label: D
choices.text: This ensures that the P/A converter will send the proper rod height data to the RIL circuitry.
choices.html: This ensures that the P/A converter will send the proper rod height data to the RIL circuitry.

questions.title: 2020 Q20 — Misaligned Rods Required Action
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 was initially at 100% Power.

Subsequently, the following sequence of events occurs:

• Automatic Turbine runback occurs due to an issue with the stator water cooling system.

• During the load reduction, the RO reports that two (2) Control Bank D rods have stopped moving at 215 steps.

• The CRS enters S2.OP-AB.ROD-0001, Immovable / Misaligned Control Rods.

• The RO has placed the Rod Bank Selector Switch in MANUAL.

• Control Bank D Group Demand counters are presently at 185 steps.

• The runback terminates with reactor power at 80 %.

• Tavg is being maintained within +/- 1.5 °F of program.

In accordance with S2.OP-AB.ROD-0001, and assuming the two control rods will not be restored to operable status, which ONE of the following describes a required action that the crew will take.

choices.label: A
choices.text: Place the Unit in Hot Shutdown.
choices.html: Place the Unit in Hot Shutdown.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Reduce power to < 50 % rated thermal power.
choices.html: Reduce power to < 50 % rated thermal power.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Place the Unit in Hot Standby.
choices.html: Place the Unit in Hot Standby.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Reduce power to < 75 % rated thermal power.
choices.html: Reduce power to < 75 % rated thermal power.

questions.title: 2020 Q21 — Rapid Boration per EOP-TRIP-2
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Following a Unit 2 Reactor Trip, the crew has transitioned to 2-EOP-TRIP-2, Reactor Trip Response.

• The crew is performing the Control Rod Insertion Step.

• Two Control Rods have failed to fully insert.

• 2CV175, RAPID BORATE STOP VALVE will NOT open.

Which of the following describes the valve manipulations required in accordance with 2-EOP-TRIP-2, Reactor Trip Response, to establish rapid boration.

choices.label: A
choices.text: OPEN RWST to CHARGING SUCTION VALVES 2SJ1 and 2SJ2, then CLOSE VCT to CHARGING SUCTION VALVES 2CV40 and 2CV41.
choices.html: OPEN RWST to CHARGING SUCTION VALVES 2SJ1 and 2SJ2, then CLOSE VCT to CHARGING SUCTION VALVES 2CV40 and 2CV41.

choices.label: B
choices.text: OPEN the BLENDER BYP VALVE 2CV174 locally, then OPEN the BA FLOW CONTROL TO BLENDER 2CV172.
choices.html: OPEN the BLENDER BYP VALVE 2CV174 locally, then OPEN the BA FLOW CONTROL TO BLENDER 2CV172.

choices.label: C
choices.text: OPEN BA FLOW CONTROL TO BLENDER 2CV172, then OPEN MAKE UP FROM BLENDER TO CHG PUMP SUCTION LINE 2CV185.
choices.html: OPEN BA FLOW CONTROL TO BLENDER 2CV172, then OPEN MAKE UP FROM BLENDER TO CHG PUMP SUCTION LINE 2CV185.

choices.label: D
choices.text: OPEN BA FLOW CONTROL TO BLENDER 2CV172, then OPEN MAKE UP FROM BLENDER TO VCT 2CV181.
choices.html: OPEN BA FLOW CONTROL TO BLENDER 2CV172, then OPEN MAKE UP FROM BLENDER TO VCT 2CV181.

questions.title: 2020 Q22 — IR Channel Bypass Alarm
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is at 100% Power.

• The Intermediate Range (IR) Channel N35 has just failed high.

• The crew has entered S2.OP-AB.NIS-0001(Q), Nuclear Instrumentation System Malfunction.

• The PO is removing N35 from service in accordance with S2.OP-SO.RPS-0001(Q), Nuclear Instrumentation Channel Trip / Restoration and OHA E-29, SR & IR TRIP BYP, annunciates.

Which of the following identifies the cause of the alarm?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Control Power Fuses have been removed.
choices.html: Control Power Fuses have been removed.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Instrument Power Fuses have been removed.
choices.html: Instrument Power Fuses have been removed.

choices.label: C
choices.text: LEVEL TRIP switch has been placed in bypass.
choices.html: LEVEL TRIP switch has been placed in bypass.

choices.label: D
choices.text: POWER MISMATCH BYPASS switch has been placed in bypass.
choices.html: POWER MISMATCH BYPASS switch has been placed in bypass.

questions.title: 2020 Q23 — Area Rad Monitor Crane Interlock
questions.stemText: Which of the following interlocks are activated when Area Radiation Monitor 2R32A, Fuel Handling Crane fails to its' alarm setpoint?

choices.label: A
choices.text: ALL crane hoist operation is prevented, but the hoist may be lowered after pressing the BYP INT pushbutton.
choices.html: ALL crane hoist operation is prevented, but the hoist may be lowered after pressing the BYP INT pushbutton.

choices.label: B
choices.text: FHB exhaust shifts to 22 HEPA plus Charcoal.
choices.html: FHB exhaust shifts to 22 HEPA plus Charcoal.

choices.label: C
choices.text: ALL Crane trolley operation is prevented.
choices.html: ALL Crane trolley operation is prevented.

choices.label: D
choices.text: ONLY Crane hoist-up operation is prevented
choices.html: ONLY Crane hoist-up operation is prevented

questions.title: 2020 Q24 — Core Cooling Red Path Validation
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 has experienced a LOCA.

• The crew is implementing the Critical Function Status Trees.

• The RO has identified that the SPDS Overview Screen indicates a Red Path for Core Cooling.

• RCS Subcooling is < 0 °F.

• No RCPs are running.

Which ONE of the following RCS Parameters would validate a Core Cooling Red Path?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Three (3) hottest CETs reading 751 °F – 755 °F, with RVLIS Upper Range reading 38 %.
choices.html: Three (3) hottest CETs reading 751 °F – 755 °F, with RVLIS Upper Range reading 38 %.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Five (5) hottest CETs reading 701 °F – 705 °F, with RVLIS Full Range reading 39 %.
choices.html: Five (5) hottest CETs reading 701 °F – 705 °F, with RVLIS Full Range reading 39 %.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Three (3) hottest CETs reading 1200 °F, with RVLIS Upper Range reading 44 %.
choices.html: Three (3) hottest CETs reading 1200 °F, with RVLIS Upper Range reading 44 %.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Five (5) hottest CETs reading 750 °F – 800 °F with RVLIS Full Range reading 43 %.
choices.html: Five (5) hottest CETs reading 750 °F – 800 °F with RVLIS Full Range reading 43 %.

questions.title: 2020 Q25 — LOCA Outside Containment from TRIP-3
questions.stemText:
Given

• Unit 2 has experienced a Small Break LOCA.

• The CRS has transitioned to 2-EOP-TRIP-3, SI Termination.

• 21 Charging Pump has been stopped and a normal charging alignment has been established.

• 21 and 22 Safety Injection Pumps have been stopped.

• 21 and 22 RHR Pumps have been stopped.

Prior to attempting to establish normal letdown, a number of overhead annunciators alarm and or reflash. The RO reports the following indications and OHA annunciators:

• A-6, RMS HI RAD OR TRBL, due to 2R41D, Plant Vent Effluent

• A-41, AUX ALM SYS PRINTER, due to 23 and 24 RHR Sump Pump starts.

• C-34 22 RHR SUMP OVRFLO

• D-40, SUBCLG CH A MARGIN LO, due to subcooling at 9 °F

• D-48, SUBCLG CH B MARGIN LO, due to subcooling at 9 °F

• E-36 PZR HTR OFF LVL LO, due to Pressurizer Level off scale low and unable to be recovered.

What procedure will be used to mitigate this event?

choices.label: A
choices.html: 2-EOP-LOCA-6, LOCA Outside Containment.

choices.label: B
choices.html: 2-EOP-LOCA-5, Loss of Emergency Recirculation.

choices.label: C
choices.html: 2-EOP-LOCA-2, Post LOCA Cooldown and Depressurization.

choices.label: D
choices.html: 2-EOP-TRIP-3, SI Termination.

questions.title: 2020 Q26 — SMM Adverse Containment
questions.stemText:
Given:

Unit 2 has experienced a LOCA.

At T+0:

• Containment Pressure has peaked at 10 psig.

• 2R44A, Containment High Range Monitor is reading 5E05 R/HR.

• 2R44B, Containment High Range Monitor is reading 7E05 R/HR.

At T+3 hours:

• Containment Pressure is currently reading 2.5 psig.

• 2R44A, Containment High Range Monitor is reading 2E04 R/HR.

• 2R44B, Containment High Range Monitor is reading 1E04 R/HR.

Complete the following statements concerning operation of the Subcooling Margin Monitor (SMM):

At T+0 the SMM is operating in _(1)_ Mode, and at T+3 hours the SMM will _(2)_ reset to Normal Mode.

choices.label: A
choices.html: NORMAL; automatically

choices.label: B
choices.html: ADVERSE; automatically

choices.label: C
choices.html: NORMAL; require manual action to

choices.label: D
choices.html: ADVERSE; require manual action to

questions.title: 2020 Q27 — LOCA Voiding Indication
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 has experienced a Small Break LOCA.

• An automatic Reactor Trip and SI have occurred.

• All RCPs have been stopped.

• The crew has transitioned to 2-EOP-LOCA-2, Post LOCA Cooldown and Depressurization.

In accordance with 2-EOP-LOCA-2, which ONE of the following operational indications describes how voiding can be identified in the RCS?
questions.questionSource: Bank - 2012 Harris NRC Exam / 2019 BV2 NRC Exam

choices.label: A
choices.html: Rapidly rising Pressurizer Level.

choices.label: B
choices.html: Lowering Safety Injection Flow.

choices.label: C
choices.html: Rising RCS Pressure.

choices.label: D
choices.html: Rapidly lowering RCS Subcooling.

questions.title: 2020 Q28 — RCP Standpipe Level Hi
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is at 100% Power.

• Control Console Bezel Alarm for 21 RCP "STANDPIPE LEVEL HI" annunciates.

• The RO reports 21 RCP Seal Leak-off Flow recorder indication has LOWERED.

The "STANDPIPE LEVEL HI" alarm is an indication of excessive leakage from the RCP _(1)_.

choices.label: A
choices.html: #3 seal.

choices.label: B
choices.html: #2 seal.

choices.label: C
choices.html: #2 and #3 seals.

choices.label: D
choices.html: #1 seal.

questions.title: 2020 Q29 — Charging Pump Power Supplies
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is at 100% Power

• 2C Bus Differential Protection relay actuates and results in the 2C 4KV Bus de-energized.

Which ONE of the following lists the available Charging Pumps following the loss of the 2C 4KV Vital Bus?

choices.label: A
choices.html: 21 Charging and 23 Charging Pumps

choices.label: B
choices.html: 21 Charging Pump ONLY

choices.label: C
choices.html: 21 Charging and 22 Charging Pumps

choices.label: D
choices.html: 22 Charging Pump ONLY

questions.title: 2020 Q30 — CVCS CV71 Post-SI
questions.stemText:
Given:

• A Unit 2 Pressurizer Safety Valve has failed open.

• A Reactor Trip and Safety Injection (SI) have automatically actuated.

The Reactor Operator is throttling closed on 2CV71, Seal Pressure Control Valve.

What is the effect on Charging Pump discharge pressure and charging flow to the RCS?

Charging Pump discharge pressure will...

choices.label: A
choices.text: rise and total Charging flow will remain constant.
choices.html: rise and total Charging flow will remain constant.

choices.label: B
choices.text: rise and total Charging flow will lower.
choices.html: rise and total Charging flow will lower.

choices.label: C
choices.text: remain constant and RCP Seal Injection flow will rise.
choices.html: remain constant and RCP Seal Injection flow will rise.

choices.label: D
choices.text: remain constant and Charging flow will remain constant.
choices.html: remain constant and Charging flow will remain constant.

questions.title: 2020 Q31 — RHR HX Cooldown Rate Control
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is in MODE 5.

• 21 RHR HX Loop is providing Shutdown Cooling with a cooldown rate of 20 °F/hr.

• 21RH18, RHR HX Flow Control Valve, is throttled at 40 % to maintain total flow at 2500 gpm.

• 2RH20, RHR HX Bypass Valve, is throttled at 30 % open.

Subsequently the following occurs:

• Foreign material dropped in the Refueling Cavity is introduced into the RHR system causing partial blockage on the 21 RHR HX tubes.

• Cooldown rate has lowered to 5 °F/hr.

Based on the above conditions, in order to maintain a 20 °F/hr cooldown rate, the operator will be required to _______. (Assume decay heat load remains constant)

choices.label: A
choices.text: Lower the demand on the 21RH18 and Lower the demand on the 2RH20.
choices.html: Lower the demand on the 21RH18 and Lower the demand on the 2RH20.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Lower the demand on the 21RH18 and Raise the demand on the 2RH20.
choices.html: Lower the demand on the 21RH18 and Raise the demand on the 2RH20.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Raise the demand on the 21RH18 and Raise the demand on the 2RH20.
choices.html: Raise the demand on the 21RH18 and Raise the demand on the 2RH20.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Raise the demand on the 21RH18 and Lower the demand on the 2RH20.
choices.html: Raise the demand on the 21RH18 and Lower the demand on the 2RH20.

questions.title: 2020 Q32 — EOP-FRTS-1 PTS Mitigation Basis
questions.stemText:
Given:

The Unit 2 crew has entered 2-EOP-FRTS-1, Response to Imminent Pressurized Thermal Shock.

The procedure directs various mitigating actions including termination of SI and the starting of an RCP. What are the basis for these two mitigating actions?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The soak required by EOP-FRTS-1 requires SI to be secured. Starting an RCP provides the ability to utilize normal spray to depressurize the RCS.
choices.html: The soak required by EOP-FRTS-1 requires SI to be secured. Starting an RCP provides the ability to utilize normal spray to depressurize the RCS.

choices.label: B
choices.text: ECCS flow is a contributor to the RCS cooldown and can prevent subsequent reduction in RCS pressure. Starting an RCP provides mixing of cold ECCS and warm RCS.
choices.html: ECCS flow is a contributor to the RCS cooldown and can prevent subsequent reduction in RCS pressure. Starting an RCP provides mixing of cold ECCS and warm RCS.

choices.label: C
choices.text: ECCS flow is a contributor to the RCS cooldown and can prevent subsequent reduction in RCS pressure. Starting an RCP minimizes the temperature gradient across the S/G tube sheets.
choices.html: ECCS flow is a contributor to the RCS cooldown and can prevent subsequent reduction in RCS pressure. Starting an RCP minimizes the temperature gradient across the S/G tube sheets.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The soak required by EOP-FRTS-1 requires SI to be secured. Starting an RCP is used to equalize boron concentration throughout the RCS to ensure proper shutdown margin as the RCS cools.
choices.html: The soak required by EOP-FRTS-1 requires SI to be secured. Starting an RCP is used to equalize boron concentration throughout the RCS to ensure proper shutdown margin as the RCS cools.

questions.title: 2020 Q33 — Containment TS Pressure and Temperature Limits
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is at 100% Power.

• Containment pressure is 0 psig.

• Containment temperature is 99 °F.

Subsequently, the following sequence of events occurs:

• A load rejection results in a reactor trip.

• Following the trip, a Pressurizer Safety Valve opens, and does not completely reseat.

• The PRT rupture disk has relieved to the containment.

• Containment pressure is rising at 0.1 psig every 5 minutes.

• Containment temperature is rising at 1 °F every 5 minutes.

Assuming containment pressure and temperature trends remain constant, which ONE of the following Containment Technical Specification LCO(s), if any, will NOT BE MET ONE HOUR from now?

choices.label: A
choices.text: BOTH LCO 3.6.1.4, Containment Internal Pressure, and LCO 3.6.1.5, Containment Air Temperature will be exceeded.
choices.html: BOTH LCO 3.6.1.4, Containment Internal Pressure, and LCO 3.6.1.5, Containment Air Temperature will be exceeded.

choices.label: B
choices.text: ONLY LCO 3.6.1.4, Containment Internal Pressure, will be exceeded.
choices.html: ONLY LCO 3.6.1.4, Containment Internal Pressure, will be exceeded.

choices.label: C
choices.text: ONLY LCO 3.6.1.5, Containment Air Temperature, will be exceeded.
choices.html: ONLY LCO 3.6.1.5, Containment Air Temperature, will be exceeded.

choices.label: D
choices.text: NEITHER LCO 3.6.1.4, Containment Internal Pressure, and LCO 3.6.1.5, Containment Air Temperature will be exceeded.
choices.html: NEITHER LCO 3.6.1.4, Containment Internal Pressure, and LCO 3.6.1.5, Containment Air Temperature will be exceeded.

questions.title: 2020 Q34 — CCW Thermal Barrier Leak Indications
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is at 100% Power.

The RO reports the following console alarms on 2CC1:

• CC HDR ACTIVITY HI.

• SURGE TANK LEVEL HI-LO.

• DISCHARGE FLOW LO.

The crew has entered S2.OP-AB.CC-0001, Component Cooling Abnormality. Based on just the above plant conditions, which ONE of the following is the source of leakage into the Component Cooling System?

choices.label: A
choices.html: RHR Heat Exchanger.

choices.label: B
choices.html: Letdown Heat Exchanger.

choices.label: C
choices.html: Thermal Barrier Heat Exchanger.

choices.label: D
choices.html: Seal Water Heat Exchanger.

questions.title: 2020 Q35 — CCW Pump Status After SEC Mode 3 Loading
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is at 100% power.

• 21 and 22 CCW Pumps are running.

• 23 CCW Pump is in AUTO.

Subsequently;

• The Unit experiences a valid Safety Injection actuation coincident with a Loss of Off-Site Power.

Which of the following describes the status of the CCW Pumps following successful SEC loading?
questions.questionSource: Bank, Salem ILT 16-01 NRC Exam, Q7

choices.label: A
choices.text: All CCW pumps are running, all CCW pumps are in Manual.
choices.html: All CCW pumps are running, all CCW pumps are in Manual.

choices.label: B
choices.text: All CCW pumps are stopped, 23 CCW pump remains in AUTO.
choices.html: All CCW pumps are stopped, 23 CCW pump remains in AUTO.

choices.label: C
choices.text: All CCW pumps are stopped, all CCW pumps are in Manual.
choices.html: All CCW pumps are stopped, all CCW pumps are in Manual.

choices.label: D
choices.text: All CCW pumps are running, 23 CCW pump remains in AUTO.
choices.html: All CCW pumps are running, 23 CCW pump remains in AUTO.

questions.title: 2020 Q36 — PZR Pressure Channel Fails Low
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is in MODE 3.

• Pressurizer (PZR) Pressure is 2235 psig.

• RCS Temperature is 547 °F.

• PZR Pressure Channel I (2PT-455) is selected for Control.

• PZR Pressure Channel II (2PT-456) is selected for Alarm.

If PZR Pressure Channel I fails LOW and with NO operator action, PZR pressure will rise until ______.

choices.label: A
choices.html: PZR PORV 2PR1 opens.

choices.label: B
choices.html: BOTH PZR PORVs, 2PR1 and 2PR2 open.

choices.label: C
choices.text: BOTH PZR Spray Valves, 2PS1 and 2PS3 open.
choices.html: BOTH PZR Spray Valves, 2PS1 and 2PS3 open.

choices.label: D
choices.html: PZR PORV 2PR2 opens.

questions.title: 2020 Q37 — OT Delta-T Inputs
questions.stemText: Which ONE of the following lists the correct operational plant parameters used as input to the OTΔT reactor protection setpoint calculation?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Tavg, Reactor Power, and ΔT only.
choices.html: Tavg, Reactor Power, and ΔT only.

choices.label: B
choices.html: Tavg and ΔT only.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Tavg, Pressurizer Pressure, and ΔI only.
choices.html: Tavg, Pressurizer Pressure, and ΔI only.

choices.label: D
choices.html: Tavg and Pressurizer Pressure only.

questions.title: 2020 Q38 — Loss of Tripping Capability RTB
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is at 100% Power

• Control Console Bezel Alarm "LOSS OF TRIPPING CAPABILITY" is received for Reactor Trip Breaker (RTB) "A"

Which ONE of the following describes the effect on Reactor Trip Breaker (RTB) "A" from this alarm condition?

choices.label: A
choices.text: RTB 'A' will NOT trip open on ANY Reactor Trip signals.
choices.html: RTB 'A' will NOT trip open on ANY Reactor Trip signals.

choices.label: B
choices.text: RTB 'A' will open when the Reactor Trip Breaker 'A' pushbutton on 2CC2 is depressed.
choices.html: RTB 'A' will open when the Reactor Trip Breaker 'A' pushbutton on 2CC2 is depressed.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The UV trip coil will NOT be capable of opening RTB "A".
choices.html: The UV trip coil will NOT be capable of opening RTB "A".

choices.label: D
choices.text: The shunt trip coil will NOT be capable of opening RTB 'A".
choices.html: The shunt trip coil will NOT be capable of opening RTB 'A".

questions.title: 2020 Q39 — ESFAS SI Block / Containment High Pressure
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is in MODE 3.

• The crew is implementing S2.OP-IO.ZZ-0006, Hot Standby to Cold Shutdown.

• The crew has commenced a cooldown and depressurization to Cold Shutdown to comply with a Technical Specification action requirement.

• RCS Temperature is 535°F

• RCS Pressure is 1890 psig

• All actions for current plant conditions have been completed in accordance with S2.OP-IO.ZZ-0006.

Multiple failures have just occurred resulting in rapid depressurization of ALL Steam Generators (SGs) INSIDE containment causing a Safety Injection actuation.

Which of the following ESFAS initiation signals and logic caused the Safety Injection actuation to occur?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Containment High Pressure - 2/3 Containment Pressure Channels
choices.html: Containment High Pressure - 2/3 Containment Pressure Channels

choices.label: B
choices.text: Pressurizer Pressure Low - 2/3 Pressurizer Pressure Channels
choices.html: Pressurizer Pressure Low - 2/3 Pressurizer Pressure Channels

choices.label: C
choices.text: Pressurizer Pressure Low – 2/4 Pressurizer Pressure Channels
choices.html: Pressurizer Pressure Low – 2/4 Pressurizer Pressure Channels

choices.label: D
choices.text: Containment High Pressure – 2/4 Containment Pressure Channels
choices.html: Containment High Pressure – 2/4 Containment Pressure Channels

questions.title: 2020 Q40 — CFCU Containment Temperature Rise
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is at 100 % Power.

• Four (4) CFCUs are in service in HIGH speed.

• Containment temperature is 95 °F.

• Containment pressure is 0.10 psig.

Consider the below conditions and its impact to Containment Fan Coil Unit performance during normal plant operation.

Which of the following conditions could result in a RISE in containment temperature?

1. Running CFCUs in LOW speed.

2. Starting additional Service Water Pumps.

3. Increase in service water temperature.

4. Erosion of the flow orifice upstream of the SW223.

choices.label: A
choices.html: 1 and 3 Only.

choices.label: B
choices.html: 2 and 4 Only.

choices.label: C
choices.html: 2, 3, and 4 Only.

questions.title: 2020 Q41 — ECCS Semi-Automatic Switchover
questions.stemText:
Given:

- Unit 2 has experienced a Reactor Trip and Safety Injection due to a Large Break LOCA.

- RWST Level has lowered to less than 15.2 feet on 2/4 RWST Level Channels.

With NO operator action, which choice describes AUTOMATIC actions that will occur based on the above RWST Level?

choices.label: A
choices.text: 21 & 22 SJ44s, RHR Pump Sump Suction Valves OPEN.
choices.html: 21 & 22 SJ44s, RHR Pump Sump Suction Valves OPEN.

choices.label: B
choices.text: 21 & 22 SJ113s, SI to Charging Pump Crossover Valves OPEN.
choices.html: 21 & 22 SJ113s, SI to Charging Pump Crossover Valves OPEN.

choices.label: C
choices.text: 21 & 22 RH4s, RHR Pump Suction Valves CLOSE.
choices.html: 21 & 22 RH4s, RHR Pump Suction Valves CLOSE.

choices.label: D
choices.text: 21 & 22 CS36s, RHR Discharge to Containment Spray Header OPEN.
choices.html: 21 & 22 CS36s, RHR Discharge to Containment Spray Header OPEN.

questions.title: 2020 Q42 — EOP-LOCA-5 Table C CS Pumps Required
questions.stemText:
Given:

- Unit 2 has experienced a design bases Large Break LOCA.

- The 2A Vital Bus is de-energized due to a Differential Protection Fault on the bus.

- The 22 RHR Pump has tripped.

At T+0:

- The crew enters 2-EOP-LOCA-5, Loss of Emergency Recirculation

- RO reports RWST Level is 20 feet.

At T+6 minutes:

- The crew is performing Step 9 of EOP-LOCA-5 to determine the required number of Containment Spray Pumps using Table C.

- RO reports Containment pressure is 18 psig.

At T+6 minutes, determine the relative RWST Level and the number of Containment Spray Pumps required in accordance with EOP-LOCA-5, Table C?

[REFERENCES PROVIDED]

choices.label: A
choices.text: RWST Level is < 15.24 ft., 0 Containment Spray Pumps are required.
choices.html: RWST Level is < 15.24 ft., 0 Containment Spray Pumps are required.

choices.label: B
choices.text: RWST Level is > 15.24 ft., 0 Containment Spray Pumps are required.
choices.html: RWST Level is > 15.24 ft., 0 Containment Spray Pumps are required.

choices.label: C
choices.text: RWST Level is > 15.24 ft., 1 Containment Spray Pump is required.
choices.html: RWST Level is > 15.24 ft., 1 Containment Spray Pump is required.

choices.label: D
choices.text: RWST Level is < 15.24 ft., 1 Containment Spray Pump is required.
choices.html: RWST Level is < 15.24 ft., 1 Containment Spray Pump is required.

questions.title: 2020 Q43 — MS10 Atmospheric Relief Valve Flow and AB.STM-0001
questions.stemText:
Given:

- Unit 2 is at 100% Power when the 21MS10, 21 SG Atmospheric Relief Valve, fails open.

- The operator is unable to close the valve using the control console valve controller.

- The crew enters S2.OP-AB.STM-0001, Excessive Steam Flow.

Complete the following statements:

1) The rated design flow through the 21MS10 is _(1)_ steam flow.

2) After performing the actions of the Continuous Action Statement of S2.OP-AB.STM-0001 to trip the reactor and initiate a Main Steam Line Isolation, a Safety Injection (SI) _(2)_ required.

questions.title: 2020 Q44 — AFW Pump Auto Start on Both SGFP Trip
questions.stemText:
Given:

- Unit 2 is performing a Technical Specifications required shutdown in accordance with S2.OP-IO.ZZ-0004, Power Operation.

- 22 SGFP has been removed from service in accordance with S2.OP-SO.CN-0002, Steam Generator Feed Pump Operation.

- Reactor Power is now 24% and the 21 SGFP has just tripped.

With NO operator action, what will be the status of the Auxiliary Feed Pumps?

choices.label: A
choices.text: ONLY the MDAFW pumps start immediately upon the trip of 21 SGFP.
choices.html: ONLY the MDAFW pumps start immediately upon the trip of 21 SGFP.

choices.label: B
choices.text: ONLY the TDAFW pump starts immediately upon the trip of 21 SGFP.
choices.html: ONLY the TDAFW pump starts immediately upon the trip of 21 SGFP.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The MDAFW pumps AND the TDAFW pump start immediately upon the trip of 21 SGFP.
choices.html: The MDAFW pumps AND the TDAFW pump start immediately upon the trip of 21 SGFP.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The MDAFW pumps AND the TDAFW pump start when NR level in 1/4 SGs lowers to 14%.
choices.html: The MDAFW pumps AND the TDAFW pump start when NR level in 1/4 SGs lowers to 14%.

questions.title: 2020 Q45 — Loss of Control Air BF19 Closure
questions.stemText:
Given:

- Unit 2 is at 100% Reactor Power.

- A complete loss of ALL Station Air has occurred.

- The crew has entered S2.OP-AB.CA-0001, Loss of Control Air.

- BOTH Control Air Header Pressures are 95 psig and LOWERING.

What is the expected impact, if any, to the Main Feedwater Regulating Valves (21-24BF19) and what action will the crew take, if control air pressure continues to lower, in accordance with S2.OP-AB.CA-0001?

choices.label: A
choices.text: No impact to the 21-24BF19s since redundant air panel will swap to Unit 1 control air; the procedural action will be to ensure the #2 ECAC has automatically started.
