choices.html: No impact to the 21-24BF19s since redundant air panel will swap to Unit 1 control air; the procedural action will be to ensure the #2 ECAC has automatically started.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The 21-24BF19s will start to close at 85 psig control air header pressure; procedural action will be to monitor BF19s and trip the reactor if both control air header pressure drops to less than 85 psig.
choices.html: The 21-24BF19s will start to close at 85 psig control air header pressure; procedural action will be to monitor BF19s and trip the reactor if both control air header pressure drops to less than 85 psig.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The 21-24BF19s will start to close at 80 psig control air header pressure; procedural action will be to monitor BF19s and trip the reactor if both control air header pressure drops to less than 80 psig.
choices.html: The 21-24BF19s will start to close at 80 psig control air header pressure; procedural action will be to monitor BF19s and trip the reactor if both control air header pressure drops to less than 80 psig.

choices.label: D
choices.text: No impact to the 21-24BF19s since #2 ECAC supplies backup control air, the procedural action will be to ensure the #1 ECAC has automatically started and monitor BF19s.
choices.html: No impact to the 21-24BF19s since #2 ECAC supplies backup control air, the procedural action will be to ensure the #1 ECAC has automatically started and monitor BF19s.

questions.title: 2020 Q46 — AFW Pressure Override / SG Levels
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is at 100% Reactor Power.

• The Unit experiences a Reactor Trip coincident with a Loss of Off-Site Power.

• 21 AFW Pump PRESSURE OVERRIDE console bezel is illuminated and the associated AF21's remain closed.

With NO operator action, which ONE of the following indications will exist regarding SG level control?

choices.label: A
choices.text: AFW flow indication reading 0 gpm for 21 and 22 SGs.
choices.html: AFW flow indication reading 0 gpm for 21 and 22 SGs.

choices.label: B
choices.text: AFW flow indication reading 0 gpm for 23 and 24 SGs.
choices.html: AFW flow indication reading 0 gpm for 23 and 24 SGs.

choices.label: C
choices.text: 21 and 22 SG levels rising SLOWER than 23 and 24 SG levels.
choices.html: 21 and 22 SG levels rising SLOWER than 23 and 24 SG levels.

choices.label: D
choices.text: 23 and 24 SG levels rising SLOWER than 21 and 22 SG levels.
choices.html: 23 and 24 SG levels rising SLOWER than 21 and 22 SG levels.

questions.title: 2020 Q47 — 4KV Degraded Voltage / SEC Mode
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is responding to a valid Safety Injection (SI) signal.

• Safety Injection has been RESET.

• ALL SECs have been RESET.

• ALL EDGs are running unloaded.

• 4KV Buses 2A and 2C are aligned to 23 Station Power Transformer (SPT).

• 4KV Bus 2B is aligned to 24 SPT.

Subsequently, 24 SPT experiences a failure causing its secondary voltage to drop and stabilize at 3600 volts.

Complete the following statement concerning the effect of this failure on the 2B 4KV Vital Bus:

The 2B 4KV Vital Bus will...

choices.label: A
choices.html: fast transfer to 23 SPT.

choices.label: B
choices.html: remain loaded onto 24 SPT.

choices.label: C
choices.text: be energized by its EDG in MODE IV (SI & Single Bus Degraded Voltage).
choices.html: be energized by its EDG in MODE IV (SI & Single Bus Degraded Voltage).

choices.label: D
choices.text: be energized by its EDG in MODE II* (Single Bus Degraded Voltage).
choices.html: be energized by its EDG in MODE II* (Single Bus Degraded Voltage).

questions.title: 2020 Q48 — EDG KVAR Loading / Voltage Control
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is at 100% Power.

• The 2A EDG is paralleled to the 2A 4KV Bus for Surveillance Testing in accordance with S2.OP-ST.DG-0001, 2A Diesel Generator Surveillance Test.

• Reactive load is 1200 KVAR OUT.

In accordance with S2.OP-ST.DG-0001, what action is needed to RAISE reactive load to 1400-1500 KVAR OUT?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Raise on the Speed Control Switch.
choices.html: Raise on the Speed Control Switch.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Lower on the Speed Control Switch.
choices.html: Lower on the Speed Control Switch.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Raise on the Voltage Control Switch.
choices.html: Raise on the Voltage Control Switch.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Lower on the Voltage Control Switch.
choices.html: Lower on the Voltage Control Switch.

questions.title: 2020 Q49 — 125VDC Battery Discharge Indication
questions.stemText:
Given:

• The Unit 2 125 VDC Vital Bus Batteries are aligned to "float charge" mode.

• A Loss of All AC Power event has occurred.

What 125 VDC Battery indications in the control room will indicate that the batteries are discharging? How many hours of operation by design are the batteries sized for after a loss of all AC power?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Battery voltage indication will rise greater than 0 volts on 2RP9 panel volt meters. 2 Hours.
choices.html: Battery voltage indication will rise greater than 0 volts on 2RP9 panel volt meters. 2 Hours.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Battery amp indication will rise greater than 0 amps on 2RP9 panel amp meters. 2 Hours.
choices.html: Battery amp indication will rise greater than 0 amps on 2RP9 panel amp meters. 2 Hours.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Battery amp indication will rise greater than 0 amps on 2RP9 panel amp meters. 4 Hours.
choices.html: Battery amp indication will rise greater than 0 amps on 2RP9 panel amp meters. 4 Hours.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Battery voltage indication will rise greater than 0 volts on 2RP9 panel volt meters. 4 Hours.
choices.html: Battery voltage indication will rise greater than 0 volts on 2RP9 panel volt meters. 4 Hours.

questions.title: 2020 Q50 — EDG Starting Air Receiver Capability
questions.stemText:
Given:

• The Unit 2 is at 100% Power.

• 2A Emergency Diesel Generator (EDG) Starting Air Receiver 21A is isolated and cleared tagged for maintenance.

• A Loss of Off-Site Power has just occurred.

Complete the following statement regarding the 2A Emergency Diesel Generator (EDG) starting air system capability and response.

Following an SEC start signal, the 2A EDG __(1)__ start and achieve rated speed of 900 RPM in ≤ 13 seconds. The 21B starting air receiver is aligned to supply __(2)__ air start motors.

choices.label: A
choices.html:
(1) will
(2) four

choices.label: B
choices.html:
(1) will NOT
(2) two

choices.label: C
choices.html:
(1) will
(2) two

choices.label: D
choices.html:
(1) will NOT
(2) four

questions.title: 2020 Q51 — SGBD Rad Monitor Warning Setpoint
questions.stemText: Which ONE of the following describes the AUTOMATIC actuations, if any, that will occur if a R19, Steam Generator Blowdown Radiation Monitor reaches its WARNING setpoint?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Causes NO automatic actuations on either unit. The R19 warning setpoint is an early warning function only.
choices.html: Causes NO automatic actuations on either unit. The R19 warning setpoint is an early warning function only.

choices.label: B
choices.text: On Unit 1, causes NO automatic actions. On Unit 2, will automatically close ALL GB10s, GB185s, and 2GB50.
choices.html: On Unit 1, causes NO automatic actions. On Unit 2, will automatically close ALL GB10s, GB185s, and 2GB50.

choices.label: C
choices.text: On Unit 2, causes NO automatic actions. On Unit 1, will automatically close ALL GB10s, GB185s, and 1GB50.
choices.html: On Unit 2, causes NO automatic actions. On Unit 1, will automatically close ALL GB10s, GB185s, and 1GB50.

choices.label: D
choices.text: On Unit 1, closes ALL GB4s, GB8s, GB10s, GB185s, and 1GB50. On Unit 2, isolates blowdown from the affected SGs by closing the associated GB4.
choices.html: On Unit 1, closes ALL GB4s, GB8s, GB10s, GB185s, and 1GB50. On Unit 2, isolates blowdown from the affected SGs by closing the associated GB4.

questions.title: 2020 Q52 — SW Nuclear Header Leak EDG/CFCU Supply
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is at 100% power.

• The crew is implementing S2.OP-AB.SW-0001, Loss of Service Water Header Pressure.

• The problem has been identified as a large Service Water leak in the Auxiliary Building just downstream of the 21SW22, Nuclear Header Inlet Valve.

• 21SW22 has been closed to isolate the leak.

• 21SW23 and 22SW23 (Header tie valves) remain closed.

Given the above conditions, which of the following describes Service Water Cooling supplies to the Emergency Diesel Generators (EDGs) and the Containment Fan Coil Units (CFCUs) if a Design Basis LOCA was to occur?

choices.label: A
choices.text: All 3 EDGs are supplied by 21 and 22 Service Water Headers and 3 CFCUs are supplied by 22 Service Water Header.
choices.html: All 3 EDGs are supplied by 21 and 22 Service Water Headers and 3 CFCUs are supplied by 22 Service Water Header.

choices.label: B
choices.text: All 3 EDGs are supplied by 21 and 22 Service Water Headers and 5 CFCUs are supplied by 22 Service Water Header.
choices.html: All 3 EDGs are supplied by 21 and 22 Service Water Headers and 5 CFCUs are supplied by 22 Service Water Header.

choices.label: C
choices.text: All 3 EDGs and 3 CFCUs are supplied by only the 22 Service Water Header.
choices.html: All 3 EDGs and 3 CFCUs are supplied by only the 22 Service Water Header.

choices.label: D
choices.text: All 3 EDGs and 5 CFCUs are supplied by either 21 or 22 Service Water Header.
choices.html: All 3 EDGs and 5 CFCUs are supplied by either 21 or 22 Service Water Header.

questions.title: 2020 Q53 — SW Pump Trip / 1A 4KV Bus Differential
questions.stemText:
Given:

Initial Conditions:

• Unit 1 is at 100% Power.

• #3 Service Water Bay is cleared and tagged (isolated) due to a leak on the 15SW3, 15 SW Pump Discharge Valve.

• The 1A EDG is running paralleled to the bus for a normally scheduled monthly surveillance.

• 11 and 13 SW Pumps are in service.

• 12 SW Pump is in AUTO.

Current Conditions:

• 13 SW Pump trips

• One minute later the 1A EDG output breaker opens due to 1A 4KV Vital Bus Differential.

Which ONE of the following describes the required mitigating actions for the event?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Enter S1.OP-AB.4KV-0001, Loss of 1A 4KV Vital Bus, and verify that 12 SW Pump has started and SW header pressure has returned to normal.
choices.html: Enter S1.OP-AB.4KV-0001, Loss of 1A 4KV Vital Bus, and verify that 12 SW Pump has started and SW header pressure has returned to normal.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Enter S1.OP-AB.SW-0005, Loss of All Service Water, trip the Rx, confirm the trip, and stop the RCPs to limit heat input to the RCS.
choices.html: Enter S1.OP-AB.SW-0005, Loss of All Service Water, trip the Rx, confirm the trip, and stop the RCPs to limit heat input to the RCS.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Enter S1.OP-AB.SW-0005, Loss of All Service Water, trip the Rx, confirm the trip, and stop the RCPs to limit heat input to the CCW System.
choices.html: Enter S1.OP-AB.SW-0005, Loss of All Service Water, trip the Rx, confirm the trip, and stop the RCPs to limit heat input to the CCW System.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Enter S1.OP-AB.SW-0004, Loss of Service Water During Service Water Header Outage, and verify that 12 SW Pump has started and SW header pressure has returned to normal.
choices.html: Enter S1.OP-AB.SW-0004, Loss of Service Water During Service Water Header Outage, and verify that 12 SW Pump has started and SW header pressure has returned to normal.

questions.title: 2020 Q54 — Emergency Control Air Compressor Power Supply
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 1 is at 100% Power.

• The #1 Emergency Control Air Compressor (ECAC) is running for testing in accordance with S1.OP-PT.CA-0001, Emergency Control Air Compressor Test.

Which ONE of the following would result in the loss of the #1 ECAC?

choices.label: A
choices.html: 1A 4KV to 460V breaker 1A4D trips.

choices.label: B
choices.html: 1C 4KV to 460V breaker 1C4D trips.

choices.label: C
choices.html: 1E 4KV to 460V breaker 1E6D trips.

choices.label: D
choices.html: 1H 4KV to 460V breaker 1H5D trips.

questions.title: 2020 Q55 — Phase A Containment Isolation Valves
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 1 Operators are responding to a Reactor Trip and Safety Injection.

• The crew is verifying valves in their Safeguards positions in accordance with 1-EOP-TRIP-1, Reactor Trip or Safety Injection.

Which of the following valves receive a Phase A Isolation signal to close?

choices.label: A
choices.html: 1CV2, 1CV277 (Letdown)

choices.label: B
choices.html: 1CC131, 1CC190 (RCP Thermal Barrier)

choices.label: C
choices.html: 11-14BF13 (Feedwater)

choices.label: D
choices.html: 1CC113, 1CC215 (Excess Letdown)

questions.title: 2020 Q56 — Rod Drive MG Set Power Supplies
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 1 is operating at 100% Power.

• The 1E 460 volt bus is de-energized following a trip of its feeder breaker.

• Tagging is in progress to allow troubleshooting of 1E 460 volt bus.

The operator mistakenly opens the 1F 460 volt bus feeder breaker, de-energizing the 1F 460 volt bus.

What is the consequence, if any, of this action?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The Reactor will trip due to the loss of BOTH Rod Drive Motor Generators.
choices.html: The Reactor will trip due to the loss of BOTH Rod Drive Motor Generators.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The Reactor will trip due to the loss of a SINGLE Rod Drive Motor Generator.
choices.html: The Reactor will trip due to the loss of a SINGLE Rod Drive Motor Generator.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The Reactor will NOT trip because BOTH Rod Drive Motor Generators are still in service.
choices.html: The Reactor will NOT trip because BOTH Rod Drive Motor Generators are still in service.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The Reactor will NOT trip because ONE Rod Drive Motor Generator is sufficient to maintain power to the Rod Control System.
choices.html: The Reactor will NOT trip because ONE Rod Drive Motor Generator is sufficient to maintain power to the Rod Control System.

questions.title: 2020 Q57 — PZR Level Channel I Fails High
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 1 is operating at 100% Power.

• All systems are operating in AUTO.

• PZR Level Control System selected to Channel I for Control.

• PZR Level Control System selected to Channel II for Alarm.

A failure causes the following sequential plant events. Assume NO operator actions are taken.

• Charging Flow reduces to minimum.

• Actual PZR level drops slowly.

• Letdown isolates and PZR heaters turn off.

• A Reactor Trip eventually occurs on high PZR level.

Which ONE of the following failures would cause the above sequential events?

choices.label: A
choices.html: Auctioneered Tavg failed high.

choices.label: B
choices.text: PZR Level Channel I failed low.
choices.html: PZR Level Channel I failed low.

choices.label: C
choices.text: PZR Level Channel I failed high.
choices.html: PZR Level Channel I failed high.

choices.label: D
choices.text: PZR Level Channel II failed low.
choices.html: PZR Level Channel II failed low.

questions.title: 2020 Q58 — Subcooling Margin Monitor Inputs
questions.stemText: Which ONE of the following lists the parameters used by the Subcooling Margin Monitor to calculate subcooling margin?

choices.label: A
choices.text: RCS pressure and RCS hot leg temperatures.
choices.html: RCS pressure and RCS hot leg temperatures.

choices.label: B
choices.html: PZR pressure and CET temperatures.

choices.label: C
choices.html: RCS pressure and CET temperatures.

choices.label: D
choices.text: PZR pressure and RCS hot leg temperatures.
choices.html: PZR pressure and RCS hot leg temperatures.

questions.title: 2020 Q59 — Containment Ventilation Isolation
questions.stemText:
1. MANUALLY initiating which of the following from the Control Room Console will also result in a Containment Ventilation Isolation signal?

2. Which valves receive a closed demand from the Containment Ventilation Isolation signal?

choices.label: A
choices.text:
_(1)_ Phase B and Spray Actuation
_(2)_ VC1, 4 Only
choices.html:
_(1)_ Phase B and Spray Actuation
_(2)_ VC1, 4 Only

choices.label: B
choices.html:
_(1)_ Phase A Isolation
_(2)_ VC1, 4 Only

choices.label: C
choices.html:
_(1)_ Phase A Isolation
_(2)_ VC1, 4, 5, and 6

choices.label: D
choices.text:
_(1)_ Phase B and Spray Actuation
_(2)_ VC1, 4, 5, and 6
choices.html:
_(1)_ Phase B and Spray Actuation
_(2)_ VC1, 4, 5, and 6

questions.title: 2020 Q60 — ADFCS SG Level Channel Failure
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is at 100% Power.

• Steam Generator level control and SGFPs are in AUTO.

• 21 Steam Generator NR Level Channel I failed high due to a sensor malfunction.

• No corrective actions have been initiated yet.

Complete the following statement concerning the response of the feedwater control system if 21 Steam Generator NR Level Channel II fails LOW?

The Ovation Advanced Digital Feedwater System will initiate OHA G-7, ADFCS ALTERNATE ACTION, and ....

choices.label: A
choices.html: ONLY 21BF19 will shift to MANUAL

choices.label: B
choices.text: BOTH 21BF19 and 21BF40 will shift to MANUAL
choices.html: BOTH 21BF19 and 21BF40 will shift to MANUAL

choices.label: C
choices.text: ALL BF19's and BF40's remain in AUTOMATIC
choices.html: ALL BF19's and BF40's remain in AUTOMATIC

choices.label: D
choices.text: 21BF19, 21BF40, and BOTH SGFPs shift to MANUAL
choices.html: 21BF19, 21BF40, and BOTH SGFPs shift to MANUAL

questions.title: 2020 Q61 — Steam Dump PT-507 Fails High
questions.stemText:
Given:

Unit 2 is operating at 100% Power.
The Steam Dumps are in Main Steam Pressure Control Mode - AUTO due to a previous failure of 2PT-506, Main Turbine Steam Line Inlet Pressure Channel II.

A Reactor Trip and a malfunction causing 2PT-507, Main Steam Header Pressure, to fail high have just occurred.

With 2PT-507 failed HIGH, which ONE of the following describes how the Steam Dump System and the plant will respond, assuming no operator action is taken?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Steam Dumps remain CLOSED. RCS temperature rises until the MS10s, Atmospheric Dump valves, open to control RCS temperature.
choices.html: Steam Dumps remain CLOSED.  RCS temperature rises until the MS10s, Atmospheric Dump valves, open to control RCS temperature.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Steam Dumps remain CLOSED. RCS temperature rises until Main Steam Safety Valves open to control RCS temperature.
choices.html: Steam Dumps remain CLOSED.  RCS temperature rises until Main Steam Safety Valves open to control RCS temperature.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Steam Dumps OPEN. RCS temperature rapidly lowers until 543°F when Main Steam Line Isolation (MSLI) and Safety Injection (SI) actuate.
choices.html: Steam Dumps OPEN.  RCS temperature rapidly lowers until 543°F when Main Steam Line Isolation (MSLI) and Safety Injection (SI) actuate.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Steam Dumps OPEN. RCS temperature rapidly lowers until steam dumps close at 543°F. Steam Dumps will then cycle and maintain RCS temperature at 547°F.
choices.html: Steam Dumps OPEN.  RCS temperature rapidly lowers until steam dumps close at 543°F.  Steam Dumps will then cycle and maintain RCS temperature at 547°F.

questions.title: 2020 Q62 — SFP Anti-Siphon Design
questions.stemText:
Design features of the Spent Fuel Cooling and Purification System ensure the fuel stored in the Spent Fuel Pool will not become uncovered as a result of any postulated loss of system integrity.

Complete the following statement that best describes this design feature.

The Spent Fuel Pool is designed with…
questions.questionSource: Modified Bank – 2015 Indian Point 2 NRC, Q59

choices.label: A
choices.text: a suction pipe inlet located above the top of fuel, and an anti-siphon hole on the return line that discharges above the top of the fuel.
choices.html: a suction pipe inlet located above the top of fuel, and an anti-siphon hole on the return line that discharges above the top of the fuel.

choices.label: B
choices.text: an anti-siphon hole on the suction pipe, and a low level cut-off switch that automatically trips the Spent Fuel Cooling Pump.
choices.html: an anti-siphon hole on the suction pipe, and a low level cut-off switch that automatically trips the Spent Fuel Cooling Pump.

choices.label: C
choices.text: an anti-siphon hole on the suction pipe, and the return line that discharges above the top of the fuel assemblies.
choices.html: an anti-siphon hole on the suction pipe, and the return line that discharges above the top of the fuel assemblies.

choices.label: D
choices.text: no drains on the pool, and double check valves on the pump discharge line to prevent backflow.
choices.html: no drains on the pool, and double check valves on the pump discharge line to prevent backflow.

questions.title: 2020 Q63 — Condenser Air Removal AR25 Stuck Open
questions.stemText:
Given:

Unit 2 is at 100% Power.
The Plant Operator (PO) is swapping Condenser Vacuum Pumps in accordance with S2.OP-SO.AR-0001, Condenser Air Removal System Operation.
The PO has started 24 Vacuum Pump successfully and has just initiated a stop on 22 Vacuum Pump.
The 22AR25, Air Ejector Suction Isolation Valve, remains OPEN.

Which ONE of the following describes the status of main condenser vacuum and what procedural action the crew will take to mitigate the event?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Condenser backpressure is rising. The PO restarts 22 Vacuum Pump.
choices.html: Condenser backpressure is rising.  The PO restarts 22 Vacuum Pump.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Condenser backpressure is lowering. The PO restarts 22 Vacuum Pump.
choices.html: Condenser backpressure is lowering.  The PO restarts 22 Vacuum Pump.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Condenser backpressure is rising. The Secondary Field Operator ensures the 22AR27, Air Ejector Bypass Valve, is opened.
choices.html: Condenser backpressure is rising.  The Secondary Field Operator ensures the 22AR27, Air Ejector Bypass Valve, is opened.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Condenser backpressure is lowering. The Secondary Field Operator ensures the 22AR27, Air Ejector Bypass Valve, is opened.
choices.html: Condenser backpressure is lowering.  The Secondary Field Operator ensures the 22AR27, Air Ejector Bypass Valve, is opened.

questions.title: 2020 Q64 — CW Malfunction / Rapid Load Reduction [OUTDATED]
questions.stemText:
DISCLAIMER: This question is outdated. AB.CW no longer specifies an exact power level reduction to 83%. It only states to perform a rapid load reduction to prevent flashing in the Condensate System.

Q64 — CW Malfunction / Rapid Load Reduction [OUTDATED]
075000K2.02 (2.5)

choices.label: A
choices.text: Stop 21 Condensate Pump and reduce reactor power to ≤ 85% in accordance with S2.OP-AB.CN-0001.
choices.html: Stop 21 Condensate Pump and reduce reactor power to ≤ 85% in accordance with S2.OP-AB.CN-0001.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Stop 21 Condensate Pump and reduce reactor power to ≤ 83% in accordance with S2.OP-AB.LOAD-0001, to prevent flashing in Condensate System.
choices.html: Stop 21 Condensate Pump and reduce reactor power to ≤ 83% in accordance with S2.OP-AB.LOAD-0001, to prevent flashing in Condensate System.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Terminate the liquid release, stop 21 Condensate Pump, and reduce reactor power to ≤ 85% in accordance with S2.OP-AB.CN-0001.
choices.html: Terminate the liquid release, stop 21 Condensate Pump, and reduce reactor power to ≤ 85% in accordance with S2.OP-AB.CN-0001.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Terminate the liquid release, stop 21 Condensate Pump, and reduce reactor power to ≤ 83% in accordance with S2.OP-AB.LOAD-0001, to prevent flashing in Condensate System.
choices.html: Terminate the liquid release, stop 21 Condensate Pump, and reduce reactor power to ≤ 83% in accordance with S2.OP-AB.LOAD-0001, to prevent flashing in Condensate System.

questions.title: 2020 Q65 — Fire Pump Start on Loss of AC
questions.stemText:
Given:

Unit 2 is at 100% Power.

The following sequence of events occurs:

OHA A-15, FIRE PMP 1/2 RUN in alarm
OHA A-23, FIRE PUMP 1/2 TRBL in alarm.
BOTH Fire Pumps are running.
NO other Fire Protection System alarms are present.
Fire protection reports NO fire system actuations.
Fire main header pressure is 135 psig and stable.

Which ONE of the following describes the cause for the above conditions?

choices.label: A
choices.html: Fire Protection Jockey Pump tripped.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Major Fire Protection System pipe rupture.
choices.html: Major Fire Protection System pipe rupture.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Loss of Normal AC power to BOTH Fire Pump Battery Chargers.
choices.html: Loss of Normal AC power to BOTH Fire Pump Battery Chargers.

choices.label: D
choices.text: A momentary (1 second) drop in Fire Protection header pressure to 70 psig.
choices.html: A momentary (1 second) drop in Fire Protection header pressure to 70 psig.

questions.title: 2020 Q66 — Operator Burdens / Annunciator Marking
questions.stemText:
During shift turnover, the on-coming RO notices an OHA window with reflash capability (multiple inputs) with a single strand of red tape diagonally across the annunciator window.

In accordance with OP-AA-102-103-1001, Operator Burdens Program, which ONE of the following describes the status of the annunciator window?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Reflash alarm function is defeated and NO new alarms will be annunciated.
choices.html: Reflash alarm function is defeated and NO new alarms will be annunciated.

choices.label: B
choices.text: One or more inputs into this annunciator window are inoperable or unreliable.
choices.html: One or more inputs into this annunciator window are inoperable or unreliable.

choices.label: C
choices.text: All inputs into this annunciator window are inoperable or unreliable.
choices.html: All inputs into this annunciator window are inoperable or unreliable.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Operator flagging that Maintenance is performing functional testing which will result in a valid alarm on this window.
choices.html: Operator flagging that Maintenance is performing functional testing which will result in a valid alarm on this window.

questions.title: 2020 Q67 — Shift Turnover Pre/Post Relief Actions
questions.stemText: Which ONE of the following describes Reactor Operator pre and post-shift relief actions that should be implemented by the oncoming operator in accordance with OP-AA-112-101, Shift Turnover and Relief?
questions.questionSource: Modified Bank – Hope Creek 2105 NRC Exam – Q66

choices.label: A
choices.text:
PRIOR to relief, read the Control Room logs through the last previous date on shift, or the preceding four (4) days logs, whichever is less.
POST relief, confer with the Control Room Supervisor to determine the scope of planned shift activities and their responsibilities for that shift.
choices.html:
PRIOR to relief, read the Control Room logs through the last previous date on shift, or the preceding four (4) days logs, whichever is less.
POST relief, confer with the Control Room Supervisor to determine the scope of planned shift activities and their responsibilities for that shift.

choices.label: B
choices.text:
PRIOR to relief, read the Control Room logs through the last previous date on shift, or the preceding four (4) days logs, whichever is less.
POST relief, tour the main control board back panels.
choices.html:
PRIOR to relief, read the Control Room logs through the last previous date on shift, or the preceding four (4) days logs, whichever is less.
POST relief, tour the main control board back panels.

choices.label: C
choices.text:
PRIOR to relief, read the Control Room logs through the last previous date on shift, or the preceding seven (7) days logs, whichever is less.
POST relief, tour the main control board back panels.
choices.html:
PRIOR to relief, read the Control Room logs through the last previous date on shift, or the preceding seven (7) days logs, whichever is less.
POST relief, tour the main control board back panels.

choices.label: D
choices.text:
PRIOR to relief, read the Control Room logs through the last previous date on shift, or the preceding seven (7) days logs, whichever is less.
POST relief, confer with the Control Room Supervisor to determine the scope of planned shift activities and their responsibilities for that shift.
choices.html:
PRIOR to relief, read the Control Room logs through the last previous date on shift, or the preceding seven (7) days logs, whichever is less.
POST relief, confer with the Control Room Supervisor to determine the scope of planned shift activities and their responsibilities for that shift.

questions.title: 2020 Q68 — Control Rod Movement Requirements
questions.stemText: During non-transient conditions, what is the MINIMUM information that the reactor operator shall state just prior to manually manipulating control rods in accordance with OP-AP-300-1001, PWR Control Rod Movement Requirements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The selected control bank initial position, target control rod position, and the direction of movement.
choices.html: The selected control bank initial position, target control rod position, and the direction of movement.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The initial TAVG, target control rod position, the final expected TAVG.
choices.html: The initial T<sub>AVG</sub>, target control rod position, the final expected T<sub>AVG</sub>.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The initial TAVG, target TAVG, rod direction, and the expected number of steps required to achieve the target TAVG.
choices.html: The initial T<sub>AVG</sub>, target T<sub>AVG</sub>, rod direction, and the expected number of steps required to achieve the target T<sub>AVG</sub>.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The initial NIS indicated Power Level, target control rod position, and the final expected NIS Power Level.
choices.html: The initial NIS indicated Power Level, target control rod position, and the final expected NIS Power Level.

questions.title: 2020 Q69 — Component Configuration Control / TSAS Positioning
questions.stemText:
A one (1) hour or less Technical Specification Action Statement (TSAS) requires a component to be positioned to the CLOSED position.

In accordance with OP-AA-108-101-1002, Component Configuration Control, the component will be positioned by _(1)_ and then the INDEPENDENT VERIFICATION (IV) will be performed _(2)_.

choices.label: A
choices.text:
_(1)_ Abnormal Component Position Sheet (ACPS)
_(2)_ As soon as practicable after the TSAS was entered
choices.html:
_(1)_ Abnormal Component Position Sheet (ACPS)
_(2)_ As soon as practicable after the TSAS was entered

choices.label: B
choices.text:
_(1)_ Tagout
_(2)_ Within the one (1) hour action time
choices.html:
_(1)_ Tagout
_(2)_ Within the one (1) hour action time

choices.label: C
choices.text:
_(1)_ Abnormal Component Position Sheet (ACPS)
_(2)_ Within the one (1) hour action time
choices.html:
_(1)_ Abnormal Component Position Sheet (ACPS)
_(2)_ Within the one (1) hour action time

choices.label: D
choices.text:
_(1)_ Tagout
_(2)_ As soon as practicable after the TSAS was entered
choices.html:
_(1)_ Tagout
_(2)_ As soon as practicable after the TSAS was entered

questions.title: 2020 Q70 — Breaker Tagging / Removal from Energized Bus
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is in MODE 5 during a scheduled maintenance outage.

• A Red Blocking Tag (RBT) is hung on a 460V breaker racking mechanism.

• The 460V Bus associated with the breaker is energized.

• The breaker is tagged in the "Disconnect" (DI) position.

Which ONE of the following describes the required tagging sequence when removing the breaker from its cubicle to facilitate maintenance?

[REFERENCE PROVIDED]

choices.label: A
choices.text: The RBT will be removed from the breaker but kept active and maintained in the physical possession of Operations while the breaker is out of the cubicle.
choices.html: The RBT will be removed from the breaker but kept active and maintained in the physical possession of Operations while the breaker is out of the cubicle.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The RBT will remain on the breaker racking mechanism, the breaker removed from the cubicle and an additional RBT installed on the cubicle door.
choices.html: The RBT will remain on the breaker racking mechanism, the breaker removed from the cubicle and an additional RBT installed on the cubicle door.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The RBT will remain on the breaker racking mechanism, the breaker removed from the cubicle and a White Caution Tag installed on the cubicle door and red danger rope will be hung across the cubicle opening.
choices.html: The RBT will remain on the breaker racking mechanism, the breaker removed from the cubicle and a White Caution Tag installed on the cubicle door and red danger rope will be hung across the cubicle opening.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The RBT will be removed from the breaker racking mechanism, the breaker removed from the cubicle, a red danger rope, tape or FME device will be hung across the cubicle opening and the same RBT installed on the rope.
choices.html: The RBT will be removed from the breaker racking mechanism, the breaker removed from the cubicle, a red danger rope, tape or FME device will be hung across the cubicle opening and the same RBT installed on the rope.

questions.title: 2020 Q71 — Surveillance Dollar Sign ($) Significance
questions.stemText:
When performing a Surveillance procedure, the operator encounters a step which has a dollar sign ( $ ) under the line where the operator initials completion of that step.

What is the significance of the dollar sign ( $ )?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It identifies an item required to meet Salem UFSAR acceptance criteria, which if not satisfactorily completed should be brought to the attention of the SM/CRS upon completion of the surveillance.
choices.html: It identifies an item required to meet Salem UFSAR acceptance criteria, which if not satisfactorily completed should be brought to the attention of the SM/CRS upon completion of the surveillance.

choices.label: B
choices.text: It identifies an item required to meet Technical Specification acceptance criteria, which if not satisfactorily completed should be brought to the immediate attention of the SM/CRS.
choices.html: It identifies an item required to meet Technical Specification acceptance criteria, which if not satisfactorily completed should be brought to the immediate attention of the SM/CRS.

choices.label: C
choices.text: It identifies a step which requires Independent Verification of its completion PRIOR to continuing to the next step.
choices.html: It identifies a step which requires Independent Verification of its completion PRIOR to continuing to the next step.

choices.label: D
choices.text: It identifies a step which requires direct oversight by an assigned Reactivity Management SRO.
choices.html: It identifies a step which requires direct oversight by an assigned Reactivity Management SRO.

questions.title: 2020 Q72 — Radwaste Release RP Support Requirement
questions.stemText: Which ONE of the following evolutions performed by Operations Department personnel, in accordance with station procedures, will require Radiation Protection support due to potential radiation/contamination hazards?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Placing 22 Hydrogen Recombiner in service after a LOCA event.
choices.html: Placing 22 Hydrogen Recombiner in service after a LOCA event.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Performing a 21 Waste Gas Decay Tank release.
choices.html: Performing a 21 Waste Gas Decay Tank release.

choices.label: C
choices.html: Performing a Containment pressure relief.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Rotating a spectacle flange to support a direct release of 21 CVCS Monitor Tank to the Circulating Water System.
choices.html: Rotating a spectacle flange to support a direct release of 21 CVCS Monitor Tank to the Circulating Water System.

questions.title: 2020 Q73 — Emergency Dose Extension at ALERT
questions.stemText: Which ONE of the following is the LOWEST Emergency Classification that results in an automatic extension of annual dose (TEDE) limits for Emergency Response Organization (ERO) Personnel that have an NRC Form-4 on file and what is the dose limit extended to?

choices.label: A
choices.html: SITE AREA EMERGENCY, 5000 mrem.

choices.label: B
choices.html: SITE AREA EMERGENCY, 4500 mrem.

choices.label: C
choices.html: ALERT, 5000 mrem.

choices.label: D
choices.html: ALERT, 4500 mrem.

questions.title: 2020 Q74 — EOP Continuous Action Verbs
questions.stemText:
In accordance with OP-AA-101-111-1003, Use of Procedures, Salem EOPs' Continuous Action Steps are either surrounded by a shaded box or contain which of the following continuous action verbs?

1. ADJUST

2. CONTROL

3. MAINTAIN

4. MODIFY

5. MONITOR

6. VERIFY

choices.label: A
choices.html: 1, 2, 4 ONLY.

choices.label: B
choices.html: 2, 3, 5 ONLY.

choices.label: C
choices.html: 3, 4, 6 ONLY.

choices.label: D
choices.html: 1, 5, 6 ONLY.

questions.title: 2020 Q75 — Containment Fire Response 2FP147
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is operating at 100 % Power.

• OHA A-7, FIRE PROT FIRE, annunciates.

• Panel 2RP5 is checked and indicates a Fire in Containment.

 ˆ Zone 59 — Air and Water Deluge, Containment El. 100 Panel 335 is lit.

 ˆ Zone 74 — Smoke and Fire Detector, Containment El. 100 Panel 335 is lit.

• The crew enters S2.OP-AB.FIRE-0001, Control Room Fire Response.

• The crew Trips the Reactor, Turbine, and all RCPs.

• 2-EOP-TRIP-1 is entered while continuing with S2.OP-AB.FIRE-0001, Control Room Fire Response.

Which ONE of the following describes the NEXT required action for the above conditions in accordance with S2.OP-AB.FIRE-0001?

choices.label: A
choices.text: OPEN the 2FP147 from the control room
choices.html: OPEN the 2FP147 from the control room

choices.label: B
choices.text: Dispatch an NEO to OPEN the associated deluge valves.
choices.html: Dispatch an NEO to OPEN the associated deluge valves.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Dispatch an NEO to place both PORV BLOCK Valve breaker key switches in EMER CLOSE.
choices.html: Dispatch an NEO to place both PORV BLOCK Valve breaker key switches in EMER CLOSE.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Verify OHA A-15, FIRE PUMP 1/2 RUN, is in alarm indicating that a Diesel Fire Pump has started and is supplying fire protection water to the associated deluge valves in containment.
choices.html: Verify OHA A-15, FIRE PUMP 1/2 RUN, is in alarm indicating that a Diesel Fire Pump has started and is supplying fire protection water to the associated deluge valves in containment.

questions.title: 2020 Q76 — CCW Restoration After LBLOCA + LOOP
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is at 100% Power.

• 21 Auxiliary Feedwater Pump is cleared and tagged for motor replacement.

• 22 CFCU is cleared and tagged for bearing replacement.

• A Large Break LOCA occurs concurrently with a Loss of Off-Site Power.

• The crew is currently implementing step 17, "CCW Pump Operation Evaluation", in accordance with 2-EOP-TRIP-1, Reactor Trip or Safety Injection.

Complete the following statement concerning component cooling water (CCW) system operation based on the above conditions:

The CRS will direct implementation of _(1)_ and the crew will start _(2)_.

choices.label: A
choices.text:
_(1)_ 2-EOP-APPX-1, Component Cooling Water Restoration
_(2)_ 22 CCW Pump
choices.html:
_(1)_ 2-EOP-APPX-1, Component Cooling Water Restoration
_(2)_ 22 CCW Pump

choices.label: B
choices.text:
_(1)_ 2-EOP-APPX-1, Component Cooling Water Restoration
_(2)_ 21 CCW Pump
choices.html:
_(1)_ 2-EOP-APPX-1, Component Cooling Water Restoration
_(2)_ 21 CCW Pump

choices.label: C
choices.text:
_(1)_ S2.OP-SO.CC-0001, Component Cooling System Operation
_(2)_ 22 CCW Pump
choices.html:

questions.title: 2020 Q77 — Loss of Charging Pump Tech Spec Entries
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is at 100% Power.

• 21 Charging Pump is in service.

• 22 and 23 Charging Pumps are Operable.

Subsequently, the following sequence of events occurs:

• 21 Charging Pump trips due to a breaker malfunction.

• The CRS enters S2.OP-AB.CVC-0001, Loss of Charging, and starts 23 Charging Pump.

Based on the above conditions complete the following statements:

(1) The CRS will enter Technical Specification LCO(s) _(1)_.

(2) Assuming that 21 Charging Pump remains INOPERABLE for the next four (4) days, the CRS will place the Unit in _(2)_.

Note: 3.1.2.2, Reactivity Control Systems – Boration Flow Paths

   3.1.2.4, Reactivity Control Systems – Charging Pumps

   3.5.2, ECCS Subsystems
questions.questionSource: Bank – Salem 2015 NRC Exam – SRO Q2

choices.label: A
choices.text:
_(1)_ 3.1.2.2 for not having the required boration flowpath operable, 3.1.2.4 for not having the required Charging Pumps operable, and 3.5.2 for not having the required ECCS subsystems operable.
_(2)_ MODE 3 and borated to at least 1 % delta k/k within 78 hours from the pump trip.
choices.html:
_(1)_ 3.1.2.2 for not having the required boration flowpath operable, 3.1.2.4 for not having the required Charging Pumps operable, and 3.5.2 for not having the required ECCS subsystems operable.
_(2)_ MODE 3 and borated to at least 1 % delta k/k within 78 hours from the pump trip.

choices.label: B
choices.text:
_(1)_ 3.1.2.2 for not having the required boration flowpath operable, 3.1.2.4 for not having the required Charging Pumps operable, and 3.5.2 for not having the required ECCS subsystems operable.
_(2)_ MODE 4 within 84 hours from the pump trip.
choices.html:
_(1)_ 3.1.2.2 for not having the required boration flowpath operable, 3.1.2.4 for not having the required Charging Pumps operable, and 3.5.2 for not having the required ECCS subsystems operable.
_(2)_ MODE 4 within 84 hours from the pump trip.

choices.label: C
choices.text:
_(1)_ 3.5.2 ONLY for not having the required ECCS subsystems operable.
_(2)_ MODE 4 within 84 hours from the pump trip.
choices.html:
_(1)_ 3.5.2 ONLY for not having the required ECCS subsystems operable.
_(2)_ MODE 4 within 84 hours from the pump trip.

choices.label: D
choices.text:
_(1)_ 3.5.2 ONLY for not having the required ECCS subsystems operable.
_(2)_ MODE 3 and borated to at least 1 % delta k/k within 78 hours from the pump trip.
choices.html:
_(1)_ 3.5.2 ONLY for not having the required ECCS subsystems operable.
_(2)_ MODE 3 and borated to at least 1 % delta k/k within 78 hours from the pump trip.

questions.title: 2020 Q78 — CCW Abnormality RCP Bearing Temperature Time Limit
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is at 100% Power.

• 22 Component Cooling Water Pump is cleared and tagged for maintenance.

At 1000:

• Bezel Alarm, SURGE TANK LEVEL HI-LO, is received.

• CCW Surge Tank Level is 35 % and lowering.

• Bezel Alarm, 21 (22) CC HDR PRESSURE LO, is received.

• OHA D-37, RCP BRG CLG HDR TEMP HI, is illuminated

• The crew has entered S2.OP-AB.CC-0001, Component Cooling Abnormality.

• The highest RCP Motor Bearing temperature is 140 °F and rising at 5 °F/minute

At 1005:

• Makeup to the CCW Surge Tank has been initiated and level is being maintained at approximately 40 %.

• OHAs D-20 through D-23; 21-24 RCP BRG CLG WTR FLO LO illuminate.

In accordance with S2.OP-AB.CC-0001, the EARLIEST time that the CRS will need to take action to prevent damage to the RCPs will be at ______.

questions.title: 2020 Q79 — SGTR-1 Subcooling Loss Transition to SGTR-3
questions.stemText:
Given:

• The crew is performing 2-EOP-SGTR-1, Steam Generator Tube Rupture.

• Safety Injection has been terminated.

• The crew has just restored normal charging alignment per 2-EOP-SGTR-1, step 30 when RCS subcooling lowers to 0 °F.

In accordance with 2-EOP-SGTR-1, which ONE of the following completes the statement below:

The crew will start ECCS pumps as necessary to restore subcooling and ___________.
questions.questionSource: Bank – Salem ILT 17-01 Audit Exam, SRO Q4

choices.label: A
choices.text: remain in 2-EOP-SGTR-1, Steam Generator Tube Rupture.
choices.html: remain in 2-EOP-SGTR-1, Steam Generator Tube Rupture.

choices.label: B
choices.html: go to 2-EOP-SGTR-2, Post SGTR Cooldown.

choices.label: C
choices.text: go to 2-EOP-SGTR-3, SGTR with LOCA – Subcooled Recovery.
choices.html: go to 2-EOP-SGTR-3, SGTR with LOCA – Subcooled Recovery.

choices.label: D
choices.text: go to 2-EOP-SGTR-4, SGTR with LOCA – Saturated Recovery.
choices.html: go to 2-EOP-SGTR-4, SGTR with LOCA – Saturated Recovery.

questions.title: 2020 Q82 — Fuel Handling Incident Mast Tube
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is in MODE 6.

• The Containment Equipment Hatch is closed.

• Core Reload is in progress.

• The transfer cart is in the Fuel Handling Building.

• A fuel assembly in the mast tube is in transit approaching the core.

• Gas bubbles are observed in the vicinity of the fuel assembly last placed in the core.

• The Refueling SRO has ordered all fuel transfers in progress stopped and all non-essential personnel evacuated from the containment.

Based on the above conditions, complete the statement concerning the NEXT action taken in accordance with S2.OP-AB.FUEL-0001, Fuel Handling Incident:

The CRS will direct placing the fuel assembly in the mast tube_________.

choices.label: A
choices.text: in the upender and lower the upender to the horizontal position.
choices.html: in the upender and lower the upender to the horizontal position.

choices.label: B
choices.text: into the core in any location that takes the least amount of time.
choices.html: into the core in any location that takes the least amount of time.

choices.label: C
choices.text: into the core in the emergency location X-3.
choices.html: into the core in the emergency location X-3.

choices.label: D
choices.text: into the core in its designated location or the emergency location P-10, whichever is closer.
choices.html: into the core in its designated location or the emergency location P-10, whichever is closer.

At 2RP2 Panel, the crew will SELECT "FIRE ____(1)_____ CONTROL AREA".

Based on the location of the fire, the crew ____(2)_____ required to DISPATCH an Operator to align the PORV Block Valve circuits to EMERG CLOSE per S2.OP-AB.FIRE-0001 Attachment 15, "PORV – EMERG CLOSE/NORMAL ALIGNMENT".

choices.label: C
choices.html: (1) OUTSIDE    (2) is NOT

         2-EOP-TRIP-6, Natural Circulation Rapid Cooldown without RVLIS

questions.title: 2020 Q85 — FRTS-1 Entry RCS Pressure Below 300
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is in MODE 3.

• RCS Temperature is 547°F

• RCS Pressure is 2235 psig.

Subsequently a LOCA occurs and the crew has transitioned from 2-EOP-TRIP-1, Reactor Trip or Safety Injection;

• RCS Pressure is 125 psig.

• RCS CETs read 380°F.

• RCS Cold Leg temperatures are 250°F.

• The RCS has cooled down > 100°F in the last 30 minutes.

• 22 RHR Pump failed to start.

• 21 RHR Pump is running providing 1150 gpm cold leg injection flow.

choices.label: A
choices.text: made but NO actions are implemented before returning to procedure in effect.
choices.html: made but NO actions are implemented before returning to procedure in effect.

choices.label: B
choices.text: NOT required since RCS pressure is below 350 psig.
choices.html: NOT required since RCS pressure is below 350 psig.

choices.label: C
choices.text: made and a RCS temperature soak for a ONE hour period will be completed.
choices.html: made and a RCS temperature soak for a ONE hour period will be completed.

choices.label: D
choices.text: NOT required since S2.OP-AB.LOCA-0001, SHUTDOWN LOCA, will address any thermal shock concerns.
choices.html: NOT required since S2.OP-AB.LOCA-0001, SHUTDOWN LOCA, will address any thermal shock concerns.

questions.title: 2020 Q86 — CR Evacuation Seal Injection
questions.stemText:
Given:

• A Control Room Evacuation has been initiated on Unit 2 in accordance with S2.OP-AB.CR-0001, Control Room Evacuation.

In accordance with S2.OP-AB.CR-0001, which ONE of the following describes the procedural actions taken outside the control room to maintain seal injection flows to each Reactor Coolant Pump?

Note: 2CV71, Charging Header Pressure Control Valve

         2CV73, Seal Injection Pressure Control Bypass Valve

         2CV55, Charging Flow Control Valve

choices.label: B

choices.html: Take local control of 2CV71 and 23 Charging Pump speed controller.

choices.label: C

choices.html: Manually adjust 2CV73 and take local control of 23 Charging Pump speed controller.

choices.label: D

choices.html: Manually adjust 2CV73 and take local control of 2CV55.

questions.title: 2020 Q87 — LOCA Sump Blockage / ECCS Flow
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 was initially at 100% Power.

At T+0:

• A Large Break LOCA occurs.

• Automatic Rx Trip, Safety Injection, and Containment Spray have actuated.

• The CRS has transitioned to 2-EOP-LOCA-3, Transfer to Cold Leg Recirculation, from 2-EOP-TRIP-1, Reactor Trip or Safety Injection, based on RWST levels reaching 15.2 feet.

• RO reports following status of 2-EOP-LOCA-3 actions:

    ◦ Containment Sump Levels are > 62 %

    ◦ 21 and 22 SJ44 (Containment Sump Valves) are open.

    ◦ 21 and 22 RHR Pumps are running.

    ◦ 2SJ69 (Common Suction) is stroking closed.

At T+16 minutes:

• The 2SJ69 valve is closed and the following indications are reported by the Reactor Operator:

    ◦ 21 and 22 RHR Pump amps are oscillating.

    ◦ 21 and 22 RHR Pump flows are oscillating.

    ◦ 21 and 22 RHR Pump discharge pressures are oscillating.

Note: 2-EOP-APPX-7, Containment Sump Blockage Guideline

         2-EOP-LOCA-5, Loss of Emergency Recirculation

Based on the above conditions, what procedure will the CRS enter NEXT to address this event and what is the MINIMUM ECCS Flow Rate required for decay heat removal at T+16 minutes?

[REFERENCES PROVIDED]

choices.label: A
choices.html: 2-EOP-APPX-7; 500 gpm.

choices.label: B
choices.html: 2-EOP-LOCA-5; 500 gpm.

choices.label: C
choices.html: 2-EOP-APPX-7; 550 gpm.

choices.label: D
choices.html: 2-EOP-LOCA-5; 550 gpm.

(1) Prior to any operator action taken, the current Containment Pressure channel logic (coincidence) for the remaining channels to cause a Phase B actuation is __(1)__.

(2) In accordance with Technical Specification 3.3.2.1, ESFAS Instrumentation, the CRS will direct maintenance to remove the inoperable channel from service by placing the Containment Pressure HI-HI Bistable in the __(2)__ condition.

choices.label: A
choices.html:
(1) 2/3
(2) Tripped

choices.label: B
choices.html:
(1) 2/3
(2) Bypassed

choices.label: C
choices.html:
(1) 1/3
(2) Tripped

choices.label: D
choices.html:
(1) 1/3
(2) Bypassed

questions.title: 2020 Q89 — SW CFCU Isolation / Water Hammer
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is in MODE 4.

• Personnel performing work in Containment notify the Control Room of a large Service Water Cooler leak on 22 CFCU.

• The crew has entered S2.OP-AB.SW-0001, Loss of Service Water Header Pressure.

In accordance with S2.OP-AB.SW-0001, complete the following statements concerning isolating a service water leak on a CFCU:

(1) The CRS will direct stopping 22 CFCU and isolating the SW leak by closing the ____(1)___.

(2) The basis for this action is to ___(2)___.

choices.label: A
choices.text:
(1) SW58 (Inlet Water Valve) first and then the SW72 (Outlet Water Valve).
(2) allow closing the SW54 (CFCU SW Inlet) and SW76 (CFCU SW Outlet) in the field with a lower differential pressure across the valves.
choices.html:
(1) SW58 (Inlet Water Valve) first and then the SW72 (Outlet Water Valve).
(2) allow closing the SW54 (CFCU SW Inlet) and SW76 (CFCU SW Outlet) in the field with a lower differential pressure across the valves.

choices.label: B
choices.text:
(1) SW72 (Outlet Water Valve) first and then the SW58 (Inlet Water Valve).
(2) allow closing the SW54 (CFCU SW Inlet) and SW76 (CFCU SW Outlet) in the field with a lower differential pressure across the valves.
choices.html:
(1) SW72 (Outlet Water Valve) first and then the SW58 (Inlet Water Valve).
(2) allow closing the SW54 (CFCU SW Inlet) and SW76 (CFCU SW Outlet) in the field with a lower differential pressure across the valves.

choices.label: C
choices.text:
(1) SW58 (Inlet Water Valve) first and then the SW72 (Outlet Water Valve).
(2) minimize the possibility of water hammer following restoration.
choices.html:
(1) SW58 (Inlet Water Valve) first and then the SW72 (Outlet Water Valve).
(2) minimize the possibility of water hammer following restoration.

choices.label: D
choices.text:
(1) SW72 (Outlet Water Valve) first and then the SW58 (Inlet Water Valve).
(2) minimize the possibility of water hammer following restoration.
choices.html:
(1) SW72 (Outlet Water Valve) first and then the SW58 (Inlet Water Valve).
(2) minimize the possibility of water hammer following restoration.

questions.title: 2020 Q94 — Refueling De-tensioning Requirements
questions.stemText:
Given:

Select from the choices below that contains ONLY actions directed to be performed in accordance with S2.OP-IO.ZZ-0007, Cold Shutdown to Refueling, BEFORE Reactor Head De-tensioning would be initiated for a refueling outage starting on November 1st.

1. The Reactor shall be subcritical for at least 168 hours.

2. Verify each valve that isolates unborated water sources is secured in the closed position.

3. Any two of the Source Range and/or Gamma-Metrics neutron monitors are Operable.

4. Continuous communications between the control room and refuel floor is established.

choices.html: 1 and 4 Only.

questions.title: 2020 Q95 — Abnormal Secondary Chemistry
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is at 100% Power.

• Chemistry has removed the Condensate Polisher due to emergent issue and is NOT available for service.

Subsequently, the following alarms and indications are received in the Control Room:

• Bezel alarm CONDENSATE PUMP DISCH SODIUM HI.

• Bezel alarm HOTWELL OUTLET CONDUCTIVITY HI.

• Bezel alarm CONDENSATE PUMP DISCH CONDUCTIVITY HI.

• Condensate Pump Discharge sodium is 15 ppb.

• 21A Hotwell Cation Conductivity is 0.5 µS/cm and rising.

• Chemistry has validated a tube leak exists in the 21A Waterbox.

Assume the Chemistry parameters above continue over the next hour.

Which ONE of the following describes the actions required in accordance with S2.OP-AB.CHEM-0001, Abnormal Secondary Plant Chemistry?

[REFERENCE PROVIDED]

choices.label: A
choices.text: Maximize SGBD flow, Emergency Trip 21A Circulator, and reduce power to ≤ 50 % power within 24 hours.
choices.html: Maximize SGBD flow, Emergency Trip 21A Circulator, and reduce power to ≤ 50 % power within 24 hours.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Reduce SGBD flow to minimum, Emergency Trip 21A Circulator, and commence a plant shutdown as quickly as possible.
choices.html: Reduce SGBD flow to minimum, Emergency Trip 21A Circulator, and commence a plant shutdown as quickly as possible.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Adjust SGBD Flow per Chemistry, continue to monitor secondary chemistry, 21A Circulator can remain in service.
choices.html: Adjust SGBD Flow per Chemistry, continue to monitor secondary chemistry, 21A Circulator can remain in service.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Stop 21A Circulator, transfer SGBD to 22 Condenser, reduce power to ≤ 83 %, and adjust SGBD Flow per Chemistry.
choices.html: Stop 21A Circulator, transfer SGBD to 22 Condenser, reduce power to ≤ 83 %, and adjust SGBD Flow per Chemistry.

questions.title: 2020 Q96 — Special Test or Evolution Screening
questions.stemText:
Given:

Unit 2 is in MODE 5.
A surveillance test is planned that involves a significant risk to Decay Heat Removal, if the test is not performed correctly a loss of Decay Heat Removal could occur.
A Special Test or Evolution Coordinator has been assigned to determine if there is a need to implement special administrative and management controls in accordance with OP-AA-108-110, Evaluation of Special Tests or Evolutions.

Based on the above information, complete the following statements concerning special test or evolution conducted in accordance with OP-AA-108-110.

(1) The procedure or evolution is classified as a special test or evolution when the activity is complex __(1)__ infrequently performed.

(2) The __(2)__ approves IMPLEMENTATION of a special test or evolution.

choices.label: A
choices.html:
(1) AND
(2) Operations Shift Management

choices.label: B
choices.html:
(1) OR
(2) Operations Shift Management

choices.label: C
choices.html:
(1) AND
(2) Responsible Senior Line Manager

choices.label: D
choices.html:
(1) OR
(2) Responsible Senior Line Manager

questions.title: 2020 Q97 — Protected Equipment Program
questions.stemText:
Given:

Unit 1 is at 100% Power.
12 Charging Pump is scheduled to be cleared and tagged for Preventative Maintenance.
The Preventative Maintenance Tasks are expected to take 24 hours to complete.

Complete the following statements concerning the implementation of OP-AA-108-116, Protected Equipment Program.

(1) The __(1)__ has overall authority of the protected equipment program.

(2) Prior to tagging 12 Charging Pump, redundant equipment must be protected when it's unavailability or manipulation could cause ___(2)___.

choices.label: A
choices.text:
(1) Shift Manager
(2) entry into Tech Spec 3.0.3 or the unit to be in Hot Shutdown in 12 hours or less.
choices.html:
(1) Shift Manager
(2) entry into Tech Spec 3.0.3 or the unit to be in Hot Shutdown in 12 hours or less.

choices.label: B
choices.text:
(1) Work Control Supervisor
(2) entry into Tech Spec 3.0.3 or the unit to be in Hot Shutdown in 12 hours or less.
choices.html:
(1) Work Control Supervisor
(2) entry into Tech Spec 3.0.3 or the unit to be in Hot Shutdown in 12 hours or less.

choices.label: C
choices.text:
(1) Work Control Supervisor
(2) overall online risk assessment to change to Orange.
choices.html:
(1) Work Control Supervisor
(2) overall online risk assessment to change to Orange.

choices.label: D
choices.text:
(1) Shift Manager
(2) overall online risk assessment to change to Orange.
choices.html:
(1) Shift Manager
(2) overall online risk assessment to change to Orange.

questions.title: 2020 Q98 — Waste Gas Release Restrictions
questions.stemText:
Given:

Unit 1 is shutdown during a refueling outage.
A normal release of 14 Waste Gas Decay Tank to the plant vent is scheduled to be performed on day shift in accordance with S1.OP-SO.WG-0011, Discharge of 14 Gas Decay Tank (WGDT).

When reviewing the schedule, which of the following activities is procedurally allowed to be scheduled during the period 14 WGDT is being released?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Aligning Unit 2 Vent Header to Unit 1 Waste Gas Compressor suction.
choices.html: Aligning Unit 2 Vent Header to Unit 1 Waste Gas Compressor suction.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Transfer of gas between 12 and 13 WGDTs.
choices.html: Transfer of gas between 12 and 13 WGDTs.

choices.label: C
choices.html: Release of 11 WGDT.

choices.label: D
choices.html: Initiation of Unit 1 VCT Purge.

questions.title: 2020 Q99 — Emergency Coordinator Escalation
questions.stemText:
Given:

An event has occurred on Salem Unit 1:

The TSC is ACTIVATED.
The EOF is MANNED and NOT ACTIVATED.

In accordance with NC.EP-EP.ZZ-0102, Emergency Coordinator Response, which ONE of the following describes the individual responsible for escalating an emergency event level from a Site Area Emergency (SAE) to a General Emergency (GE)?
questions.questionSource: Bank – Hope Creek 2015 NRC Exam – Q97

choices.label: A
choices.html: The Shift Manager.

choices.label: B
choices.html: The Emergency Duty Officer.

choices.label: C
choices.html: The Emergency Response Manager.

choices.label: D
choices.html: The Site Vice President.

questions.title: 2020 Q100 — EOP-TRIP-1 FRHS-1 Transition
questions.stemText:
Given:

Unit 2 was initially operating at 100% Power when the following sequence of events occurs;

The reactor trips.
A valid demand for Safety Injection occurs.
The crew enters 2-EOP-TRIP-1, Reactor Trip or Safety Injection.

Subsequently, the following occurs;

23 AFW Pump tripped on overspeed and cannot be reset due to trip throttle valve damage.
21 AFW Pump is tagged out for impeller replacement.
22 AFW Pump has tripped on motor overcurrent.
All Steam Generator NR Levels are < 9%.
All Steam Generator Pressures are stable.

Which ONE of the following indicates the proper procedural usage of the Emergency Operating Procedures (EOPs)?

Note: 2-EOP-FRHS-1, Loss of Secondary Heat Sink

         CFST, Critical Safety Function Status Trees

choices.label: A
choices.html: Immediately transition to 2-EOP-FRHS-1.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Complete all immediate actions of 2-EOP-TRIP-1, then transition to 2-EOP-FRHS-1.
choices.html: Complete all immediate actions of 2-EOP-TRIP-1, then transition to 2-EOP-FRHS-1.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Continue to perform 2-EOP-TRIP-1 until directed to transition to 2-EOP-FRHS-1.
choices.html: Continue to perform 2-EOP-TRIP-1 until directed to transition to 2-EOP-FRHS-1.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Continue to perform 2-EOP-TRIP-1 until CFST monitoring is directed, then transition to 2-EOP-FRHS-1.
choices.html: Continue to perform 2-EOP-TRIP-1 until CFST monitoring is directed, then transition to 2-EOP-FRHS-1.

questions.title: 2022 Q1 — Reactor Trip Breakers / SSPS Train Failure
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is at 100% Reactor Power.

• Reactor Trip Breaker testing is in progress.

• Reactor Trip Breakers (RTB) 'A' and 'B' are closed.

• Operators have just closed Reactor Trip Bypass Breaker (BYB) 'A'.

Subsequently the following occurs:

• A valid Reactor Trip demand signal is actuated.

• SSPS Train A actuates as designed.

• SSPS Train B fails to actuate.

Which ONE of the following describes how the RTB 'B' and BYB 'A' will respond?

choices.label: A
choices.html: RTB 'B': OPEN; BYB 'A': OPEN

choices.label: B
choices.html: RTB 'B': CLOSED; BYB 'A': OPEN

choices.label: C
choices.html: RTB 'B': CLOSED; BYB 'A': CLOSED

choices.label: D
choices.html: RTB 'B': OPEN; BYB 'A': CLOSED

questions.title: 2022 Q2 — PZR Safety Valve Tailpipe Temperature
questions.stemText:
Given:

• A manual reactor trip and SI have been actuated on Unit 1.

• A PZR safety valve has been stuck slightly open for ten minutes.

• RCS pressure is 1900 psig and slowly lowering.

• PZR vapor space temperature is 630 °F and stable.

• PRT level is 80% and slowly rising.

• PRT pressure is 35 psig and slowly rising.

• Containment pressure is 4 psig and slowly rising.

Currently, safety valve tailpipe temperature indication should be reading approximately:

questions.title: 2022 Q3 — Phase A Safeguards Indication
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 reactor tripped and Safety Injection actuated due to a small break LOCA.

• The crew has entered 2-EOP-TRIP-1, Reactor Trip or Safety Injection.

• The crew is performing step 12, Safeguard Valve Alignment.

• The RO notices on 2CC1 SAFEGUARDS ACTUATION Bezels that the "ØA ISOLATION OPERATE" Red light is lit for one train and is out for the other train.

Which ONE of the following completes both statements?

1. The train of Phase A (ØA) with the Red light __(1)__ failed to actuate.

2. In accordance with 2-EOP-TRIP-1, control room operators will attempt to reposition the components associated with the lights on 2RP4 that are __(2)__ on the train that failed to actuate.

choices.label: A
choices.html:
1) lit
2) lit

choices.label: B
choices.html:
1) lit
2) out

choices.label: C
choices.html:
1) out
2) lit

choices.label: D
choices.html:
1) out
2) out

questions.title: 2022 Q4 — EOP-APPX-7 Reflux Cooling
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 1 is performing 1-EOP-APPX-7, CONTAINMENT SUMP BLOCKAGE.

• ALL RCPs are stopped.

Attempts to establish containment sump recirculation have failed.

1-EOP-APPX-7, Step 43, MAINTAINING RCS HEAT REMOVAL, is in progress.

• The crew is dumping steam to the condensers from the intact SGs.

In accordance with 1-EOP-APPX-7, which ONE of the following is the reason for dumping steam from the intact SGs?

choices.label: A
choices.text: To prevent a SG tube rupture.
choices.html: To prevent a SG tube rupture.

choices.label: B
choices.text: To allow time to make-up to the RWST.
choices.html: To allow time to make-up to the RWST.

choices.label: C
choices.text: To raise injection flow provided by ECCS.
choices.html: To raise injection flow provided by ECCS.

choices.label: D
choices.html: To promote Core Reflux Cooling.

questions.title: 2022 Q5 — CCW Thermal Barrier / RCP Impact
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 1 is in MODE 3 at normal operating temperature and pressure.

• While performing troubleshooting activities, the 1CC190, CCW Thermal Barrier Isolation Valve, closes.

Which ONE of the following completes the statement concerning the impact this failure will have on the RCPs?

Thermal Barrier CCW return flow has been lost from ...

choices.label: A
choices.text: ALL RCPs. RCP operation may continue.
choices.html: ALL RCPs. RCP operation may continue.

choices.label: B
choices.text: ONLY 1 RCP. RCP operation may continue.
choices.html: ONLY 1 RCP. RCP operation may continue.

choices.label: C
choices.text: ALL RCPs. All RCPs must be stopped within 2 minutes due to loss of cooling.
choices.html: ALL RCPs. All RCPs must be stopped within 2 minutes due to loss of cooling.

choices.label: D
choices.text: ONLY 1 RCP. The affected RCP must be stopped within 2 minutes due to loss of cooling
choices.html: ONLY 1 RCP. The affected RCP must be stopped within 2 minutes due to loss of cooling

2) take manual control of 23 Charging Pump Speed Controller

1) 0
2) take manual control of 23 Charging Pump Speed Controller

choices.label: D

1) 0
2) take manual control of 2CV55, Charging Flow Control Valve
choices.html:
1) 0
2) take manual control of 2CV55, Charging Flow Control Valve

questions.title: 2022 Q7 — RHR Suction Isolation Interlocks
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is in MODE 4 with RCS heat up in progress IAW S2.OP-IO.ZZ-0002, Cold Shutdown to Hot Standby.

• 2RH1 and 2RH2, RHR Suction Isolation Valves, are OPEN.

• Pressurizer Overpressure Protection System (POPS) Channels are ARMED.

• 21 RHR Pump is in shutdown cooling mode.

• 22 RHR Pump is aligned for ECCS injection.

Subsequently, the following events occurs;

• 21 RHR Pump trips.

• 22 RHR Pump fails to start.

• RCS pressure is 350 psig and rising.

Which ONE of the following completes both statements?

1. 2RH1 and 2RH2 _(1)_ close automatically when RCS pressure exceeds 375 psig.

2. Closing the 2RH1 and 2RH2 is required to prevent exceeding the design pressure of the RHR system _(2)_.

choices.label: A
choices.html:
(1) will
(2) suction piping ONLY

choices.label: B
choices.html:
(1) will
(2) suction AND discharge piping

choices.label: C
choices.html:
(1) will NOT
(2) suction piping ONLY

choices.label: D
choices.text:
(1) will NOT
(2) suction AND discahrge piping
choices.html:
(1) will NOT
(2) suction AND discahrge piping

questions.title: 2022 Q8 — CCW / RCP Motor Bearing Temp
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 is at 100% Power.

• The RO reports the following:

  - CCW Surge Tank Levels are lowering slowly.

  - OHA A-41, AUX ALM SYS PRINTER, annunciates for Containment Sump Pump start then stops 5 seconds later.

  - The RO reports 23 RCP motor bearing temperatures are 160 °F and rising at 0.5 °F per minute.

• The crew enters S2.OP-AB.CC-0001, Component Cooling Abnormality.

Based on current plant conditions and in accordance with S2.OP-AB.CC-0001, which ONE of the following is the MAXIMUM time allowed before tripping the reactor and stopping the RCP?

choices.label: A
choices.html: 15 minutes

choices.label: B
choices.html: 20 minutes

choices.label: C
choices.html: 25 minutes

choices.label: D
choices.html: 35 minutes

Given:

• ALL systems are in AUTO.

In accordance with S1.OP-AB.PZR-0001, Pressurizer Pressure Malfunction, which ONE of the following completes both statements?

choices.label: B

2. Low PZR pressure

choices.label: C

2. Low PZR pressure

questions.title: 2022 Q10 — EOP-FRSM-1 / ATWS Actions
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 experienced a valid reactor trip demand but the reactor failed to trip.

• The RO attempted a MANUAL reactor trip by operating both reactor trip switches (pistol grip) and depressing the reactor trip breaker pushbuttons, but was unsuccessful in tripping the reactor.

In accordance with 2-EOP-FRSM-1, Response to Nuclear Power Generation – ATWS, which ONE of the following is the NEXT required action?

choices.label: A
choices.html: INITIATE Emergency Boration.

choices.label: B
choices.text: INSERT control rods at maximum rate using MANUAL and/or AUTO rod control.
choices.html: INSERT control rods at maximum rate using MANUAL and/or AUTO rod control.

choices.label: C
choices.html: TRIP the Main Turbine.

choices.label: D
choices.text: OPEN both Rod Drive M-G Set breakers 2E6D and 2G6D.
choices.html: OPEN both Rod Drive M-G Set breakers 2E6D and 2G6D.

2. If natural circulation is NOT occurring, then the Plant Operator will adjust the __(2)__ on the intact steam generators to raise steam flow.

choices.label: B

2) Steam Dumps

choices.label: C
choices.html:

2) Atmospheric Relief Valves

choices.label: D
choices.html:
1) 552 °F
2) Steam Dumps

Given:

  - RCS Pressure is 1950 psig and lowering

Main Steam Line Isolation actuated due to...

choices.label: A

2) more

2) more

questions.title: 2022 Q19 — Dropped Rod / NIS Indication
questions.stemText:
Given:
