choices.html: SI flows 200 gpm, close 21/22 SJ49

choices.label: C
choices.html: SI pressure 1700 psig, close 21/22 SJ49

choices.label: D
choices.html: SI pressure 1700 psig, close 2SJ135

questions.title: 2023 Q17 — Loss of Emergency Recirc / Upper Head Voiding
questions.stemText:
Given:

• LOCA, all RCPs stopped

• Loss of emergency recirc

• Crew in EOP-LOCA-5

• RCS depressurization in progress

Is upper head voiding expected and what should be monitored?

choices.label: A
choices.html: Voiding IS expected, monitor CETs

choices.label: B
choices.text: Voiding IS expected, monitor PZR level
choices.html: Voiding IS expected, monitor PZR level

choices.label: C
choices.text: Voiding is NOT expected, monitor PZR level
choices.html: Voiding is NOT expected, monitor PZR level

choices.label: D
choices.text: Voiding is NOT expected, monitor CETs
choices.html: Voiding is NOT expected, monitor CETs

questions.title: 2023 Q18 — Loss of Secondary Heat Sink / MSLI on High Steam Flow
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 lost all feedwater, performing EOP-FRHS-1

• SI manually actuated and reset

• 24 SG selected for depressurization

• MSLI on all SGs except 24

• 22MS167 failed to close

• Depressurization rate set too HIGH

What happens and how does the crew continue depressurization?

choices.label: A
choices.text: No effect, MSLI signals blocked when SI reset
choices.html: No effect, MSLI signals blocked when SI reset

choices.label: B
choices.text: Hi steam line diff pressure causes MSLI, use atmospheric dumps
choices.html: Hi steam line diff pressure causes MSLI, use atmospheric dumps

choices.label: C
choices.text: Hi steam flow with Lo-Lo Tavg causes MSLI, use atmospheric dumps
choices.html: Hi steam flow with Lo-Lo Tavg causes MSLI, use atmospheric dumps

choices.label: D
choices.text: Lo-Lo Tavg (P-12) closes steam dumps, bypass Tavg pushbuttons to continue
choices.html: Lo-Lo Tavg (P-12) closes steam dumps, bypass Tavg pushbuttons to continue

questions.title: 2023 Q19 — EOP-TRIP-2 / Inadequate Rapid Boration Flow
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 reactor trip, crew in EOP-TRIP-2

• 3 control rods not fully inserted

• Rapid boration via BAT pump

• 2CV175 fully open

• Flowmeter reads 20 gpm

What is the required action?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Continue rapid boration until required boron achieved
choices.html: Continue rapid boration until required boron achieved

choices.label: B
choices.text: Stop BAT pump, realign charging suction to RWST, maintain charging >87 gpm
choices.html: Stop BAT pump, realign charging suction to RWST, maintain charging >87 gpm

choices.label: C
choices.text: Establish rapid boration via cold leg injection from RWST and BIT valves
choices.html: Establish rapid boration via cold leg injection from RWST and BIT valves

choices.label: D
choices.text: Locally open 2CV174 to bypass blender, open 2CV172 for max boron, maintain charging >87 gpm
choices.html: Locally open 2CV174 to bypass blender, open 2CV172 for max boron, maintain charging >87 gpm

questions.title: 2023 Q20 — Condenser Backpressure / Effect on Turbine
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 1 at 100%

• All circulators in service

• Condenser backpressure rising

What is the confirming indication and operational concern?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Rising condenser diff temp, damage to LP turbine
choices.html: Rising condenser diff temp, damage to LP turbine

choices.label: B
choices.text: Rising condenser diff temp, condensate pump cavitation
choices.html: Rising condenser diff temp, condensate pump cavitation

choices.label: C
choices.text: Lowering generator MWe, condensate pump cavitation
choices.html: Lowering generator MWe, condensate pump cavitation

choices.label: D
choices.text: Lowering generator MWe, damage to LP turbine
choices.html: Lowering generator MWe, damage to LP turbine

questions.title: 2023 Q21 — Containment Spray Logic / Channel Removed from Service
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 at 100%

• Containment Pressure Channel III out of service (properly removed)

• Then trip + SI due to LOCA

• Containment pressures: Ch I 15.2, Ch II 14.7, Ch III 0.0, Ch IV 14.5 psig

Are CS/Phase B setpoints met, and what opens the CS2 valves?

choices.label: A
choices.text: CS/Phase B NOT met, CS2s open on CS signal
choices.html: CS/Phase B NOT met, CS2s open on CS signal

choices.label: B
choices.text: CS/Phase B NOT met, CS2s open on Phase B signal
choices.html: CS/Phase B NOT met, CS2s open on Phase B signal

choices.label: C
choices.text: CS/Phase B ARE met, CS2s open on Phase B signal
choices.html: CS/Phase B ARE met, CS2s open on Phase B signal

choices.label: D
choices.text: CS/Phase B ARE met, CS2s open on CS signal
choices.html: CS/Phase B ARE met, CS2s open on CS signal

questions.title: 2023 Q22 — High RCS Activity / Maximize Letdown Flow
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 at 100%

• High RCS activity

• Performing AB.RC-0002

• Chemistry confirms elevated activity below TS limit

What is the required action?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Reduce letdown flow to minimize aux building exposure
choices.html: Reduce letdown flow to minimize aux building exposure

choices.label: B
choices.html: Perform hourly RCS sampling

choices.label: C
choices.text: Maximize letdown flow to accelerate cleanup through demineralizers
choices.html: Maximize letdown flow to accelerate cleanup through demineralizers

choices.label: D
choices.text: Shutdown within 6 hours to minimize SGTR release potential
choices.html: Shutdown within 6 hours to minimize SGTR release potential

questions.title: 2023 Q23 — EOP Flowchart Symbols / Concurrent Step
questions.stemText:
When proceeding through 2-EOP-TRIP-3, SI Termination, you come across the following symbol at Step 1:

What does the symbol denote?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Action must be completed before continuing (sequential branching)
choices.html: Action must be completed before continuing (sequential branching)

choices.label: B
choices.text: Initiate action while continuing existing flow path (concurrent step)
choices.html: Initiate action while continuing existing flow path (concurrent step)

choices.label: C
choices.text: Execute if/when conditions specified occur (conditional step)
choices.html: Execute if/when conditions specified occur (conditional step)

choices.label: D
choices.text: Wait for conditions before proceeding (wait step)
choices.html: Wait for conditions before proceeding (wait step)

questions.title: 2023 Q24 — Containment Flooding / SW Not Isolated by Phase A/B
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 1 LOCA

• Containment pressure peaked 20 psig now 13 psig lowering

• Successful cold leg recirc transfer

• Entered EOP-FRCE-2 for high containment water level

Which system is the source of water into containment that is NOT isolated by Phase A or B?

choices.label: A
choices.html: Fire Protection

choices.label: B
choices.html: Service Water

choices.label: C
choices.html: Component Cooling Water

choices.label: D
choices.html: Primary Water

questions.title: 2023 Q25 — High Containment Radiation / CFCU Low Speed HEPA
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 reactor trip from 100%

• STA reports valid CFST YELLOW path for Containment Environment (high radiation)

• CRS enters EOP-FRCE-3

What speed and filter alignment is required for CFCUs?

choices.label: A
choices.html: High speed, roughing filters

choices.label: B
choices.html: High speed, HEPA filters

choices.label: C
choices.html: Low speed, HEPA filters

choices.label: D
choices.html: Low speed, roughing filters

questions.title: 2023 Q26 — EOP-TRIP-4 Natural Circulation / RCP Restart Priority
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 experienced a LOOP

• Crew is performing EOP-TRIP-4, Natural Circulation

• Power has been restored to 4KV Group Buses

How many RCPs are started and why?

choices.label: A
choices.text: One RCP; prevents two phase flow in the RCS.
choices.html: One RCP; prevents two phase flow in the RCS.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Two RCPs; prevents two phase flow in the RCS.
choices.html: Two RCPs; prevents two phase flow in the RCS.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Two RCPs; permits faster cooldown with less potential for upper head voiding.
choices.html: Two RCPs; permits faster cooldown with less potential for upper head voiding.

choices.label: D
choices.text: One RCP; permits faster cooldown with less potential for upper head voiding.
choices.html: One RCP; permits faster cooldown with less potential for upper head voiding.

questions.title: 2023 Q27 — AB.RCP-0001 / CCW Loss Time Limit and Bearing Temp
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Salem Unit 2 is at 100% power

Then:

• OHA D-20, 21 RCP BRG CLG WTR FLO LO, is Alarming

• RO reports that all RCP motor bearing temperatures are rising

Which of the following completes the statement?

Per S2.OP-AB.RCP-0001, RCP Abnormality, the crew has a MAXIMUM of (1) minutes to restore CCW flow OR is required to trip the reactor and stop the RCP anytime RCP motor bearing temperatures exceeds (2).

choices.label: A
choices.html: 2 minutes; 175°F

choices.label: B
choices.html: 2 minutes; 165°F

choices.label: C
choices.html: 5 minutes; 165°F

choices.label: D
choices.html: 5 minutes; 175°F

questions.title: 2023 Q28 — CVCS Letdown HX Temp Detector Fails Low / Boron Release
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Salem Unit 2 is at 100% power

• 2CC71, Letdown HX Temp Control Valve, is in Auto

Then:

• 2TE-130, temperature detector for 2CC71, fails LOW

Which of the following describes the impact of this failure?

1. Letdown temperature will (1) and the RCS T-avg temperature will (2).

choices.label: A
choices.text: Letdown temperature rises; RCS Tavg lowers.
choices.html: Letdown temperature rises; RCS Tavg lowers.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Letdown temperature rises; RCS Tavg rises.
choices.html: Letdown temperature rises; RCS Tavg rises.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Letdown temperature lowers; RCS Tavg rises.
choices.html: Letdown temperature lowers; RCS Tavg rises.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Letdown temperature lowers; RCS Tavg lowers.
choices.html: Letdown temperature lowers; RCS Tavg lowers.

questions.title: 2023 Q29 — CVCS VCT Level Transmitter Fails High / Auto Swapover
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Salem Unit 2 is at 100% power

• 21 Charging Pump is in service

• 2LT-112, VCT level, transmitter fails HIGH

Which of the following describes the operational impact to the CVCS system and the effect on the running charging pump, if any (assume no operator action taken)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: No automatic make-up, VCT level lowers, automatic swapover to RWST occurs.
choices.html: No automatic make-up, VCT level lowers, automatic swapover to RWST occurs.

choices.label: B
choices.text: No automatic make-up, VCT level lowers, no automatic swapover to RWST, charging pump damage.
choices.html: No automatic make-up, VCT level lowers, no automatic swapover to RWST, charging pump damage.

choices.label: C
choices.html: Automatic make-up maintains VCT level.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Automatic make-up initiates but cannot maintain VCT level.
choices.html: Automatic make-up initiates but cannot maintain VCT level.

questions.title: 2023 Q30 — RHR Cooldown Rate Control / RH18 and RH20 Valves
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Salem Unit 1 is in MODE 4 performing a shutdown.

• 11 RHR Pump is in service

• 11 RHR Heat Exchanger is in service

• 11RH18 and 12RH18, RHR HX Flow Cont. Valves, are being throttled

• 1RH20, RHR HX Bypass Valve, is being throttled

• The BOP operator has determined that the cooldown is approaching the maximum allowable Technical Specification limit

Which of the following describes the actions that the operator will take to LOWER the cooldown rate while maintaining a constant RHR flow rate?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Lower RH18 demand; Raise RH20 demand.
choices.html: Lower RH18 demand; Raise RH20 demand.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Raise RH18 demand; Lower RH20 demand.
choices.html: Raise RH18 demand; Lower RH20 demand.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Lower RH18 demand; Lower RH20 demand.
choices.html: Lower RH18 demand; Lower RH20 demand.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Raise RH18 demand; Raise RH20 demand.
choices.html: Raise RH18 demand; Raise RH20 demand.

questions.title: 2023 Q31 — SBLOCA Break Location / Highest Peak Clad Temperature
questions.stemText:
Given:

• A small break LOCA has occurred

Which RCS break location results in the highest peak clad temperature during a SBLOCA?

choices.label: A
choices.html: Cold leg

choices.label: B
choices.html: Hot leg

choices.label: C
choices.html: CRDM housing on reactor head

choices.label: D
choices.html: Pressurizer steam space

questions.stemText:
Given:

• Salem Unit 2 is at 100% power

• PRT level is 90%

Which of the following completes the statement?

PRT level will be lowered ...

choices.label: A
choices.text: Via RCDT Pump to Aux Building Sump
choices.html: Via RCDT Pump to Aux Building Sump

choices.label: B
choices.text: Via RCDT Pump to CVCS Hold-Up Tank
choices.html: Via RCDT Pump to CVCS Hold-Up Tank

choices.label: C
choices.text: Gravity drain to CVCS Hold-Up Tank
choices.html: Gravity drain to CVCS Hold-Up Tank

choices.label: D
choices.text: Gravity drain to Aux Building Sump
choices.html: Gravity drain to Aux Building Sump

questions.title: 2023 Q33 — CCW System Response to LOCA + LOOP / SEC Mode 3
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Salem Unit 2 experienced a LOCA and a Loss of Off-site Power

• The crew is implementing 2-EOP-TRIP-1, Reactor Trip or Safety Injection

Which of the following describes the operational impact on the Component Cooling Water System (CCW) and the reason why?

choices.label: A
choices.text: CCW pumps remain running, both HXs available.
choices.html: CCW pumps remain running, both HXs available.

choices.label: B
choices.text: No CCW pumps running; SW to CCW HXs auto-isolated to prevent SW pump runout.
choices.html: No CCW pumps running; SW to CCW HXs auto-isolated to prevent SW pump runout.

choices.label: C
choices.text: No CCW pumps running; SW to CCW HXs must be manually isolated.
choices.html: No CCW pumps running; SW to CCW HXs must be manually isolated.

choices.label: D
choices.text: All CCW pumps running; SW to CCW HXs isolated.
choices.html: All CCW pumps running; SW to CCW HXs isolated.

questions.title: 2023 Q34 — MPC Fails Low / PZR Pressure Response
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Salem Unit 2 is at 100% power

• Master Pressure Controller (MPC) is in AUTO

• PZR Pressure Channel I is selected for Control

• PZR Pressure Channel IV is selected for Alarm

Then:

• A malfunction with the MPC causes the output demand to fail LOW (0%)

Which of the following identifies the effect on RCS pressure and the expected system response, assuming no operator action?

RCS pressure will...

choices.label: A
choices.text: RCS pressure lowers; no backup heaters energize.
choices.html: RCS pressure lowers; no backup heaters energize.

choices.label: B
choices.text: RCS pressure lowers; both backup heater groups energize.
choices.html: RCS pressure lowers; both backup heater groups energize.

choices.label: C
choices.text: RCS pressure rises; no PORVs open.
choices.html: RCS pressure rises; no PORVs open.

choices.label: D
choices.text: RCS pressure rises; BOTH PORVs will open.
choices.html: RCS pressure rises; BOTH PORVs will open.

questions.title: 2023 Q35 — Reactor Trip Logic at 7% Power / Which Trip Active
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 at 7% reactor power

Which of the following conditions DIRECTLY causes a reactor trip?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Three RCS FLOW LO bistables tripped on one loop.
choices.html: Three RCS FLOW LO bistables tripped on one loop.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Three PZR PRESS LO bistables tripped.
choices.html: Three PZR PRESS LO bistables tripped.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Two PZR PRESS HI bistables tripped.
choices.html: Two PZR PRESS HI bistables tripped.

choices.label: D
choices.text: One SG LO LEVEL bistable tripped on two SGs.
choices.html: One SG LO LEVEL bistable tripped on two SGs.

questions.title: 2023 Q36 — RCP Underfrequency Logic / 1/2 Taken Twice
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 2 at 18% power

• 500KV grid transient occurs

• H and E buses: 56.0 Hz / 4100V

• F and G buses: 59.7 Hz / 4200V

What is the plant response?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Trip breakers remain closed; ALL RCPs remain running.
choices.html: Trip breakers remain closed; ALL RCPs remain running.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Trip breakers remain closed; RCPs on H and E buses trip on individual RCP breaker underfrequency.
choices.html: Trip breakers remain closed; RCPs on H and E buses trip on individual RCP breaker underfrequency.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Trip breakers open on RCP underfrequency; ALL RCPs trip.
choices.html: Trip breakers open on RCP underfrequency; ALL RCPs trip.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Trip breakers open on RCP underfrequency; RCPs on H and E buses trip on individual breaker underfrequency.
choices.html: Trip breakers open on RCP underfrequency; RCPs on H and E buses trip on individual breaker underfrequency.

questions.title: 2023 Q37 — LOCA + LOOP After SI/SEC Reset / EDG and Safeguards Response
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Unit 1 LOCA in progress

• Crew is in EOP-LOCA-1

• SI has been reset, SECs have been reset

• All EDGs have been stopped from the Control Room

• A LOOP then occurs

What is the EDG and safeguards load response?

choices.label: A
choices.text: EDGs must be manually started; some safeguard loads must be manually started.
choices.html: EDGs must be manually started; some safeguard loads must be manually started.

choices.label: B
choices.text: EDGs must be manually started; SECs sequence all safeguard loads.
choices.html: EDGs must be manually started; SECs sequence all safeguard loads.

choices.label: C
choices.text: EDGs auto-start; SECs sequence all safeguard loads.
choices.html: EDGs auto-start; SECs sequence all safeguard loads.

choices.label: D
choices.text: EDGs auto-start; some safeguard loads must be manually started.
choices.html: EDGs auto-start; some safeguard loads must be manually started.

questions.title: 2023 Q38 — CFCU Response to Inadvertent SI / SEC Mode 1
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Salem Unit 2 is at 100% power

• 21, 22, 23 and 24 CFCUs are running in HIGH speed with 25 CFCU in standby

• An inadvertent SI actuation occurs

Which of the following completes both statements?

1. Following SI actuation, all CFCUs will start in LOW speed (1).

2. To restore CFCUs to HIGH speed operation, (2) must be RESET.

choices.label: A
choices.html: After 20 seconds; reset SI only.

choices.label: B
choices.text: After 20 seconds; reset SI and SECs.
choices.html: After 20 seconds; reset SI and SECs.

choices.label: C
choices.html: Immediately; reset SI and SECs.

choices.label: D
choices.html: Immediately; reset SI only.

questions.title: 2023 Q39 — CFCU Power Supplies / 4KV Vital Bus Loss
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Salem Unit 2 experienced a reactor trip and safety injection actuation due to a LOCA

• 23 CFCU is CIT for maintenance

• 2B 4KV Vital Bus de-energized on Bus Differential protection

Which of the following identifies the status of the CFCUs?

choices.label: A
choices.html: 22 and 25

choices.label: B
choices.html: 22 and 24

choices.label: C
choices.html: 21 and 24

choices.label: D
choices.html: 21 and 25

questions.title: 2023 Q40 — Containment Spray Pump Sequencing / SEC Not Reset
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Salem Unit 2 experienced a LOCA

• Containment pressure is 10 psig and slowly rising

• ALL available safeguards equipment have started with the exception that 2B SEC failed to actuate

• SECs have NOT been RESET

Based on the conditions above, which of the following completes the statement?

(1) containment spray pump(s) is/are still available from a Class 1E vital power source and (2) automatically start when containment pressure exceeds the containment pressure HI-HI setpoint.

choices.label: A
choices.text: Only one CS pump available; WILL auto-start on Hi-Hi containment pressure.
choices.html: Only one CS pump available; WILL auto-start on Hi-Hi containment pressure.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Only one CS pump available; will NOT auto-start on Hi-Hi containment pressure.
choices.html: Only one CS pump available; will NOT auto-start on Hi-Hi containment pressure.

choices.label: C
choices.text: BOTH CS pumps available; WILL auto-start on Hi-Hi containment pressure.
choices.html: BOTH CS pumps available; WILL auto-start on Hi-Hi containment pressure.

choices.label: D
choices.text: BOTH CS pumps available; will NOT auto-start on Hi-Hi containment pressure.
choices.html: BOTH CS pumps available; will NOT auto-start on Hi-Hi containment pressure.

questions.title: 2023 Q41 — AFW TD Pump Response to Lowering SG Pressure
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Salem Unit 2 reactor tripped and actuation of Main Steam Line Isolation occurred due to a steamline break

• ONLY the 23 AFW Turbine-Driven feedpump is running supplying all SGs

• ALL SG pressures are 900 psig and lowering rapidly

Which of the following completes the statement concerning the effect on the AFW system (assuming no operator action)?

When SG pressures lowers to 700 psig, 23 AFW turbine speed will (1) and feedflow will (2).

choices.label: A
choices.html: (1) lower (2) lower

choices.label: B
choices.html: (1) remain constant (2) rise

choices.label: C
choices.html: (1) remain constant (2) remain constant

choices.label: D
choices.html: (1) lower (2) rise

questions.title: 2023 Q42 — SG High Level Trip and FW Isolation
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Salem Unit 2 is at 100% power

• 21BF19, MFW Regulating Valve, fails fully OPEN

Which of the following completes the statement regarding how the plant will respond?

The reactor will trip and the crew will enter 2-EOP-TRIP-1, Reactor Trip or Safety Injection, when high level on SG NR levels reach (1) followed by a (2).

choices.label: A
choices.html: (1) 67% (2) Feedwater Isolation

choices.label: B
choices.html: (1) 67% (2) Feedwater Interlock ONLY

choices.label: C
choices.html: (1) 92% (2) Feedwater Isolation

choices.label: D
choices.html: (1) 92% (2) Feedwater Interlock ONLY

questions.title: 2023 Q43 — AFW 2MS132 Instrument Air Supply Rupture
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Salem Unit 2 is at 100% power

• Instrument air supply line to 2MS132, 23 AFW Turbine Steam Inlet Valve, ruptures

Which of the following completes both statements?

1. What is the status of 23 AFW Pump?

2. What action should the operator take FIRST?

choices.label: A
choices.text: (1) Running (2) Reduce reactor power to prevent overpower condition per S2.OP-IO.ZZ-0004, Power Operation
choices.html: (1) Running (2) Reduce reactor power to prevent overpower condition per S2.OP-IO.ZZ-0004, Power Operation

choices.label: B
choices.text: (1) Running (2) Take manual control of the Main Feedwater Regulating valves to prevent SG overfill per S2.OP-AB.CA-0001, Loss of Control Air.
choices.html: (1) Running (2) Take manual control of the Main Feedwater Regulating valves to prevent SG overfill per S2.OP-AB.CA-0001, Loss of Control Air.

choices.label: C
choices.text: (1) NOT Running (2) Enter Tech Spec LCO 3.7.1.2, Auxiliary Feedwater System for INOPERABLE AFW Pump.
choices.html: (1) NOT Running (2) Enter Tech Spec LCO 3.7.1.2, Auxiliary Feedwater System for INOPERABLE AFW Pump.

choices.label: D
choices.text: (1) NOT Running (2) Fail open the 2MS132 by using the handwheel on the valve actuator per S2.OP-AB.CA-0001, Loss of Control Air.
choices.html: (1) NOT Running (2) Fail open the 2MS132 by using the handwheel on the valve actuator per S2.OP-AB.CA-0001, Loss of Control Air.

questions.title: 2023 Q44 — AFW 2DR6 Air Leak / Tank Overflow
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Salem Unit 2 is at 100% power

Then:

• The Primary Equipment Operator reports a significant air leak on the air supply line at the connection to the 2DR6, AFW Tank Makeup, valve actuator

Which of the following describes the expected impact to the Auxiliary Feedwater System (assuming the leak is causing a reduction in air pressure to the actuator) and operator response, if any?

AFW storage tank level will ...

choices.label: A
choices.text: rise and overflow; local/manual control is NOT available.
choices.html: rise and overflow; local/manual control is NOT available.

choices.label: B
choices.text: rise and overflow; local/manual control is available.
choices.html: rise and overflow; local/manual control is available.

choices.label: C
choices.text: remain the same; local/manual control is available.
choices.html: remain the same; local/manual control is available.

choices.label: D
choices.text: remain the same; local/manual control is NOT available.
choices.html: remain the same; local/manual control is NOT available.

questions.title: 2023 Q45 — Loss of 2A 460V Bus / Charging Pump
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Salem Unit 2 is at 100% power

• 23 Charging Pump is in service

• 22 PZR Backup Heaters are ON

• A Overcurrent fault on the 2A 460V Motor Control Center (MCC) causes the 4160V feeder breaker 2A4O to trip open, de-energizing the 2A 460V and 230V MCCs

Per S2.OP-AB.460-0001, Loss of 2A 460 V Bus, which of the following describes the effect on plant operations and the required action(s)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Loss of 2B1 125 VDC Battery Charger; place 2B2 125 VDC Battery Charger in service.
choices.html: Loss of 2B1 125 VDC Battery Charger; place 2B2 125 VDC Battery Charger in service.

questions.title: 2023 Q72 — Subcritical SR Counts and SUR After Rod Withdrawal
questions.stemText:
Given:

- Salem Unit 1 is performing a reactor startup with the reactor slightly subcritical.

- 2N31 and 2N32 Source Range count rates are stable.

Then:

- The RO performs a short control rod withdrawal.

Which of the following describes the response on the Source Range (SR) counts and startup rate after the rod withdrawal has stopped (assume the reactor remains subcritical)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: SR counts will slowly increase then gradually increase and stabilize at a higher value; startup rate will rapidly increase then gradually decrease to a slightly positive value.
choices.html: SR counts will slowly increase then gradually increase and stabilize at a higher value; startup rate will rapidly increase then gradually decrease to a slightly positive value.

choices.label: B
choices.text: SR counts will rapidly increase then stabilize at a higher value; startup rate will rapidly increase then gradually decrease and stabilize at zero.
choices.html: SR counts will rapidly increase then stabilize at a higher value; startup rate will rapidly increase then gradually decrease and stabilize at zero.

choices.label: C
choices.text: SR counts will rapidly increase then gradually decrease and stabilize at the previous value; startup rate will rapidly increase then gradually decrease and stabilize at zero.
choices.html: SR counts will rapidly increase then gradually decrease and stabilize at the previous value; startup rate will rapidly increase then gradually decrease and stabilize at zero.

choices.label: D
choices.text: SR counts will rapidly increase then stabilize at a higher value; startup rate will not change until criticality is achieved.
choices.html: SR counts will rapidly increase then stabilize at a higher value; startup rate will not change until criticality is achieved.

questions.title: 2023 Q73 — Steam Tables Subcooling and SG Pressure
questions.stemText:
Given:

- Salem Unit 2 is shutdown.

- RCS pressure is 1805 psig and stable.

- Core decay heat is being removed via the Steam Generators (SGs) using the MS10s, Atmospheric Dump Valves

What is the HIGHEST pressure in the SGs to obtain a MINIMUM 100 degrees F subcooling margin in the reactor coolant leaving the SGs (assume the reactor coolant leaves the SGs at the SG saturation temperature)?
questions.questionSource: Modified Bank - INPO GFES Bank P575

choices.label: A
choices.html: 790 psig

choices.label: B
choices.html: 805 psig

choices.label: C
choices.html: 820 psig

choices.label: D
choices.html: 835 psig

questions.title: 2023 Q74 — Condensate Depression Effects on Efficiency and Cavitation
questions.stemText:
Given:

- Salem Unit 2 is operating at 80% power.

- All Circulators are in service.

Which of the following completes the statement?

If condensate depression decreases from 5 degrees F to 2 degrees F, the steam cycle thermal efficiency will (1) and the condensate pumps will operate (2) cavitation.

choices.label: A
choices.html: (1) lower (2) closer to

choices.label: B
choices.html: (1) lower (2) farther from

choices.label: C
choices.html: (1) rise (2) closer to

choices.label: D
choices.html: (1) rise (2) farther from

questions.title: 2023 Q75 — Axial Peaking Factor and Boron Dilution
questions.stemText:
Given:

- Salem Unit 1 is operating at 80% power, middle of life of the fuel cycle.

- All control rods are withdrawn except D bank is at 190 steps and in manual control.

- Core axial power distribution is peaked below the core midplane.

Which of the following will INCREASE the core maximum axial peaking factor? (Assume no operator action is taken unless stated and that main turbine load and core xenon distribution do not change unless stated)

choices.label: A
choices.text: Turbine load/reactor power is reduced by 10 percent.
choices.html: Turbine load/reactor power is reduced by 10 percent.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The control bank D is withdrawn 4 steps.
choices.html: The control bank D is withdrawn 4 steps.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Reactor coolant system boron concentration is reduced by 15 ppm.
choices.html: Reactor coolant system boron concentration is reduced by 15 ppm.

choices.label: D
choices.text: A fully withdrawn control rod located at the edge of the core drops to the bottom of the core.
choices.html: A fully withdrawn control rod located at the edge of the core drops to the bottom of the core.

questions.title: 2023 Q76 — PZR Vapor Space Leak ECCS Status and Procedure Transition
questions.stemText:
Given:

- Salem Unit 2 experienced an automatic reactor trip and Safety Injection due to lowering RCS pressure

- The crew suspects a PZR vapor space leak

Then:

- The crew is implementing 2-EOP-LOCA-1, Loss of Reactor or Secondary Coolant

- Subcooling is 10 degrees F

- RCS pressure is 1450 psig and stable

- PZR level is 30% and rising

Based on the above conditions, which of the following completes both statements?

1. Which ECCS Pump(s) is/are injecting?

2. Per 2-EOP-LOCA-1, what procedure will the CRS direct NEXT?

choices.label: A
choices.text: (1) Charging and SI (2) 2-EOP-LOCA-2, Post LOCA Cooldown and Depressurization
choices.html: (1) Charging and SI (2) 2-EOP-LOCA-2, Post LOCA Cooldown and Depressurization

choices.label: B
choices.html: (1) Charging ONLY (2) 2-EOP-TRIP-3, SI Termination

choices.label: C
choices.html: (1) Charging and SI (2) 2-EOP-TRIP-3, SI Termination

choices.label: D
choices.text: (1) Charging ONLY (2) 2-EOP-LOCA-2, Post LOCA Cooldown and Depressurization
choices.html: (1) Charging ONLY (2) 2-EOP-LOCA-2, Post LOCA Cooldown and Depressurization

questions.title: 2023 Q77 — LOCA Containment Spray vs FRP Purple Path Priority
questions.stemText:
Given:

- A LOCA is in progress on Salem Unit 2.

- The crew is implementing 2-EOP-LOCA-1, Loss of Reactor or Secondary Coolant, and is currently at Step 7.4 for assessing conditions for stopping containment spray pumps.

- Containment pressure is 23 psig.

Then:

- A valid PURPLE path exists on Thermal Shock

- Containment pressure is below 13 psig.

In accordance with OP-SA-108-101-2002, EOP Users Guide, which of the following is required and why?

Note: FRP = Functional Restoration Procedure(s)

choices.label: A
choices.text: Step 7.4 may be performed if the CRS determines there is no conflict with implementing the FRP.
choices.html: Step 7.4 may be performed if the CRS determines there is no conflict with implementing the FRP.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Immediately perform Step 7.4; a Note step in EOP-LOCA-1 specifically states NO FRPs are to be implemented during performance of EOP-LOCA-1.
choices.html: Immediately perform Step 7.4; a Note step in EOP-LOCA-1 specifically states NO FRPs are to be implemented during performance of EOP-LOCA-1.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Immediately perform Step 7.4; Step 7.4 is a continuous action step and still in effect.
choices.html: Immediately perform Step 7.4; Step 7.4 is a continuous action step and still in effect.

choices.label: D
choices.text: DO NOT perform Step 7.4; transition to FRP for PURPLE path.
choices.html: DO NOT perform Step 7.4; transition to FRP for PURPLE path.

questions.title: 2023 Q78 — RCP Vibration Abnormal Procedure Actions
questions.stemText:
Given:

- Salem Unit 2 is operating at 30% power

- OHA D-36, RCP VIB HI, is LIT

- 21 RCP parameters are:

  - Motor flange vibration is 3 mils and steady

  - Shaft vibration is 16 mils and steady

- Engineering has determined the vibrations are valid

- The crew enters S2.OP-AB.RCP-0001, RCP Abnormality

What action will the CRS direct?

Note: TS 3.4.1.1, Reactor Coolant Loops and Coolant Circulation

2-EOP-TRIP-1, Reactor Trip or Safety Injection

choices.label: A
choices.text: Trip the reactor and stop 21 RCP per Attachment 2, Stopping RCPs, of S2.OP-AB.RCP-0001.
choices.html: Trip the reactor and stop 21 RCP per Attachment 2, Stopping RCPs, of S2.OP-AB.RCP-0001.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Preparations for a controlled Unit shutdown to remove 21 RCP from service.
choices.html: Preparations for a controlled Unit shutdown to remove 21 RCP from service.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Stop 21 RCP within 1 hour per TS 3.4.1.1 and place the Unit in MODE 3 within 6 hours.
choices.html: Stop 21 RCP within 1 hour per TS 3.4.1.1 and place the Unit in MODE 3 within 6 hours.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Trip the reactor and stop 21 RCP per OHA D-36 Alarm Response Procedure and enter 2-EOP-TRIP-1.
choices.html: Trip the reactor and stop 21 RCP per OHA D-36 Alarm Response Procedure and enter 2-EOP-TRIP-1.

questions.title: 2023 Q79 — Loss of RHR Breaker Reclosure Policy
questions.stemText:
Given:

- Salem Unit 2 is in MODE 5

- 21 RHR Pump is in shutdown cooling

- 22 RHR Pump is in standby

Then:

- 21 RHR Pump trips

- The crew enters S2.OP-AB.RHR-0001, Loss of RHR

- Attempt to start 22 RHR Pump results in the breaker tripping free

- STA reports Time to Core Boiling is less than 15 minutes

- Field operator reports no protective relay flags are present and no obvious reason for the 4KV breaker for 22 RHR Pump to trip free, and an acrid smell is observed near the 21 RHR 4KV breaker cubicle

In accordance with OP-SA-108-106-1001, Equipment Control and Large Motor Starting Criteria, complete the following statement concerning breaker re-closure for 22 RHR Pump?

Based on the above conditions, the (1) can authorize one attempt at reclosing the breaker without an investigation ONLY during (2).

choices.label: A
choices.html: (1) Shift Manager (2) emergency conditions

choices.label: B
choices.html: (1) Plant Manager (2) emergency conditions

choices.label: C
choices.text: (1) Shift Manager (2) emergency conditions AND with Shift Technical Advisor concurrence
choices.html: (1) Shift Manager (2) emergency conditions AND with Shift Technical Advisor concurrence

choices.label: D
choices.text: (1) Plant Manager (2) emergency conditions AND with Shift Technical Advisor concurrence
choices.html: (1) Plant Manager (2) emergency conditions AND with Shift Technical Advisor concurrence

questions.title: 2023 Q80 — ATWS EOP-FRSM-1 Turbine Trip Verification
questions.stemText:
Given:

- Salem Unit 2 reactor failed to automatically trip following a valid RPS trip signal

- All attempts to trip the reactor from the main control room have been unsuccessful

- The crew enters 2-EOP-FRSM-1, Response to Nuclear Power Generation, and all immediate actions are complete

In accordance with 2-EOP-FRSM-1, which of the following completes both statements?

1. Which of the following describes how the turbine trip status is checked?

2. What subsequent action will the CRS direct if the turbine trip status check is NOT met following immediate actions of 2-EOP-FRSM-1?

choices.label: A
choices.text: (1) ALL turbine stop valve closed bi-stables LIT on 2RP4. (2) Dispatch an operator to locally trip the turbine at the front standard.
choices.html: (1) ALL turbine stop valve closed bi-stables LIT on 2RP4. (2) Dispatch an operator to locally trip the turbine at the front standard.

choices.label: B
choices.text: (1) ALL turbine stop valve closed bi-stables LIT on 2RP4. (2) Direct the control room operator to stop all EHC pumps.
choices.html: (1) ALL turbine stop valve closed bi-stables LIT on 2RP4. (2) Direct the control room operator to stop all EHC pumps.

choices.label: C
choices.text: (1) 2 out 3 Auto Stop Oil low pressure bi-stables LIT on 2RP4. (2) Direct the control room operator to stop all EHC pumps.
choices.html: (1) 2 out 3 Auto Stop Oil low pressure bi-stables LIT on 2RP4. (2) Direct the control room operator to stop all EHC pumps.

choices.label: D
choices.text: (1) 2 out 3 Auto Stop Oil low pressure bi-stables LIT on 2RP4. (2) Dispatch an operator to locally trip the turbine at the front standard.
choices.html: (1) 2 out 3 Auto Stop Oil low pressure bi-stables LIT on 2RP4. (2) Dispatch an operator to locally trip the turbine at the front standard.

questions.title: 2023 Q81 — SG Tube Leak Action Levels
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Salem Unit 2 is at 100% power

• The crew is performing S2.OP-AB.SG-0001, Steam Generator Tube Leak, due to a confirmed tube leak on one SG

• Chemistry is performing hourly trends of primary to secondary leak rates

Chemistry leak rates are as follows:

TimeSG Leak Rate (gpd)
020040
030060
040090
0500125

Based on the above trends, what is the HIGHEST action that the CRS should direct in accordance with S2.OP-AB.SG-0001?

[REFERENCE PROVIDED]

choices.label: A
choices.text: Be in MODE 3 in less than or equal to 24 hours.
choices.html: Be in MODE 3 in less than or equal to 24 hours.

choices.label: B
choices.html: Be in MODE 3 within 6 hours.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Reduce power to less than or equal to 50% within 1 hour AND be in MODE 3 in the following 2 hours.
choices.html: Reduce power to less than or equal to 50% within 1 hour AND be in MODE 3 in the following 2 hours.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Trip the reactor and actuate Safety Injection.
choices.html: Trip the reactor and actuate Safety Injection.

questions.title: 2023 Q82 — IR NIS P-6 Permissive / TS Bases
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Salem Unit 2 is performing a reactor startup

• A valid P-6 permissive light is now LIT on 2RP4

When the P-6 permissive was LIT, the RO observed the following:

• 2N35, Intermediate Range, NIS channel indicates 1E-5% power

• 2N36, Intermediate Range, NIS channel indicates 1E-8% power

Which of the following completes both statements?

1. The (1) Intermediate Range (IR) NIS channel is not indicating as expected and is INOPERABLE.

2. Per TS 3.3.1.1 Instrumentation Bases, why is the 24 hour action time allowed when one IR NIS channel is INOPERABLE?

choices.label: A
choices.text: (1) 2N35 — (2) Takes into account the low probability of failure of the operable IR channel during this period.
choices.html: (1) 2N35 — (2) Takes into account the low probability of failure of the operable IR channel during this period.

choices.label: B
choices.text: (1) 2N35 — (2) Takes into account the low probability of reactivity events during this period.
choices.html: (1) 2N35 — (2) Takes into account the low probability of reactivity events during this period.

choices.label: C
choices.text: (1) 2N36 — (2) Takes into account the low probability of reactivity events during this period.
choices.html: (1) 2N36 — (2) Takes into account the low probability of reactivity events during this period.

choices.label: D
choices.text: (1) 2N36 — (2) Takes into account the low probability of failure of the operable IR channel during this period.
choices.html: (1) 2N36 — (2) Takes into account the low probability of failure of the operable IR channel during this period.

questions.title: 2023 Q83 — Control Room Evacuation / EOPs and SG Pressure
questions.stemText:
Given:

• BOTH Salem Control Rooms have been evacuated due to toxic gas

Per S2.OP-AB.CR-0001, Control Room Evacuation, which of the following completes both statements?

1. EOPs (1) applicable during Control Room Evacuation.

2. The "Caution" statement in Attachment 3 alerts the CRS to monitor SG pressures at the Hot Shutdown Panel to avoid (2).

choices.label: A
choices.text: (1) are NOT — (2) exceeding Tech Spec RCS cooldown rate of 100 degrees F per hour
choices.html: (1) are NOT — (2) exceeding Tech Spec RCS cooldown rate of 100 degrees F per hour

choices.label: B
choices.text: (1) are NOT — (2) Safety Injection on steam line differential pressure
choices.html: (1) are NOT — (2) Safety Injection on steam line differential pressure

choices.label: C
choices.text: (1) are — (2) exceeding Tech Spec RCS cooldown rate of 100 degrees F per hour
choices.html: (1) are — (2) exceeding Tech Spec RCS cooldown rate of 100 degrees F per hour

choices.label: D
choices.text: (1) are — (2) Safety Injection on steam line differential pressure
choices.html: (1) are — (2) Safety Injection on steam line differential pressure

questions.title: 2023 Q84 — EOP-FRCC-1 SG Depressurization / RCP Start
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Salem Unit 2 is performing 2-EOP-FRCC-1, Response to Inadequate Core Cooling

• All steam generators are intact and at approximately 1040 psig

• MSIVs are open

• The crew is preparing to depressurize the steam generators in order to inject accumulators

• Containment Pressure is 5 psig

Which of the following completes both statements?

1. The operators will depressurize the steam generators using steam dumps at a (1).

2. Following SG depressurization, the SG NR levels are as follows:

21 SG22 SG23 SG24 SG
7%12%16%10%

Per 2-EOP-FRCC-1, step 18, RCP Start for Temporary Core Cooling, what is the MAXIMUM number of RCPs that the CRS will direct to be started (assume CETs remain above 1200 F)?
questions.questionSource: Modified Bank - Diablo Canyon 2019 NRC SRO84

choices.label: A
choices.text: (1) maximum rate while attempting to avoid a Main Steam Isolation — (2) One
choices.html: (1) maximum rate while attempting to avoid a Main Steam Isolation — (2) One

choices.label: B
choices.text: (1) maximum rate while attempting to avoid a Main Steam Isolation — (2) Three
choices.html: (1) maximum rate while attempting to avoid a Main Steam Isolation — (2) Three

choices.label: C
choices.text: (1) rate not to exceed 100 degrees F per hour — (2) Three
choices.html: (1) rate not to exceed 100 degrees F per hour — (2) Three

choices.label: D
choices.text: (1) rate not to exceed 100 degrees F per hour — (2) One
choices.html: (1) rate not to exceed 100 degrees F per hour — (2) One

questions.title: 2023 Q85 — SG Overpressure / Security Event (Redacted)
2023 Q86 — RCP Seal Failure / Standpipe Level
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Salem Unit 2 is at 100% power

Then:

• Console Alarm, STANDPIPE LEVEL-LO, is now LIT

• The RO reports the following for 21 RCP:

  - Seal Leak-off recorder indicates 1.5 gpm and steady

  - Seal Injection Flow indicates 8.5 gpm

Which of the following completes both statements?

1. Which RCP seal failed?

2. What action(s) will the CRS direct the crew?

Note: ARP = Alarm Response Procedure

S2.OP-AB.RCP-0001, Reactor Coolant Pump Abnormality
questions.questionSource: Modified Bank - Diablo Canyon 2019 NRC SRO86

choices.label: A
choices.text: 1) #3 seal — 2) Perform an orderly shutdown and stop 21 RCP within 8 hours per S2.OP-AB.RCP-0001.
choices.html: 1) #3 seal — 2) Perform an orderly shutdown and stop 21 RCP within 8 hours per S2.OP-AB.RCP-0001.

choices.label: B
choices.text: 1) #3 seal — 2) Make-up to the standpipe per the ARP; power operation may continue.
choices.html: 1) #3 seal — 2) Make-up to the standpipe per the ARP; power operation may continue.

choices.label: C
choices.text: 1) #2 seal — 2) Make-up to the standpipe per the ARP; power operation may continue.
choices.html: 1) #2 seal — 2) Make-up to the standpipe per the ARP; power operation may continue.

choices.label: D
choices.text: 1) #2 seal — 2) Perform an orderly shutdown and stop 21 RCP within 8 hours per S2.OP-AB.RCP-0001.
choices.html: 1) #2 seal — 2) Perform an orderly shutdown and stop 21 RCP within 8 hours per S2.OP-AB.RCP-0001.

questions.title: 2023 Q87 — EOP-TRIP-4 Loss of PZR Level / SI Actuation
questions.stemText:
Given:

• A Loss of Offsite Power has occurred on Salem Unit 2

• The crew is performing actions in 2-EOP-TRIP-4, Natural Circulation Cooldown

• 21 Charging Pump is running

• RCS cooldown to establish required subcooling is in progress

• Low PZR Pressure SI signal is BLOCKED

• RCS temperature is 537 degrees F

Then:

• RCS pressure is 1183 psig and lowering

• 2CV55, Charging Flow Control Valve, is fully open

• 2CV71, Seal Injection Backpressure Valve, is fully open

• Pressurizer level is 10% and lowering

Which of the following describes the correct action(s) for these conditions per 2-EOP-TRIP-4?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Stop the RCS cooldown and wait until PZR level and subcooling are restored.
choices.html: Stop the RCS cooldown and wait until PZR level and subcooling are restored.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Align the Charging Pump suction to the RWST, open the BIT isolation valves, and transition to 2-EOP-LOCA-1, Loss of Reactor or Secondary Coolant.
choices.html: Align the Charging Pump suction to the RWST, open the BIT isolation valves, and transition to 2-EOP-LOCA-1, Loss of Reactor or Secondary Coolant.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Operate Pressurizer heaters as necessary to maintain saturated conditions in the pressurizer per 2-EOP-TRIP-4.
choices.html: Operate Pressurizer heaters as necessary to maintain saturated conditions in the pressurizer per 2-EOP-TRIP-4.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Actuate Safety Injection and return to 2-EOP-TRIP-1, Reactor Trip or Safety Injection.
choices.html: Actuate Safety Injection and return to 2-EOP-TRIP-1, Reactor Trip or Safety Injection.

questions.title: 2023 Q88 — Grid Disturbance / Solar Magnetic Disturbance
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Salem Unit 2 is at 100% power

Then:

• The Electric System Operator (ESO) reports a Solar Magnetic Disturbance (SMD) of greater than K-5 intensity

• Salem control room has confirmed an Excess MVAR condition

The CRS will direct the crew to ...

choices.label: A
choices.text: trip the reactor and perform 2-EOP-TRIP-1, Reactor Trip or Safety Injection, and S2.OP-AB.GRID-0001, Abnormal Grid, concurrently.
choices.html: trip the reactor and perform 2-EOP-TRIP-1, Reactor Trip or Safety Injection, and S2.OP-AB.GRID-0001, Abnormal Grid, concurrently.

choices.label: B
choices.text: trip the reactor and perform 2-EOP-TRIP-1, Reactor Trip or Safety Injection, Only.
choices.html: trip the reactor and perform 2-EOP-TRIP-1, Reactor Trip or Safety Injection, Only.

choices.label: C
choices.text: initiate load reduction per S2.OP-AB.GRID-0001 Abnormal Grid, and S2.OP-AB.LOAD-0001, Rapid Load Reduction.
choices.html: initiate load reduction per S2.OP-AB.GRID-0001 Abnormal Grid, and S2.OP-AB.LOAD-0001, Rapid Load Reduction.

choices.label: D
choices.text: initiate load reduction per S2.OP-AB.GRID-0001 Abnormal Grid, Only.
choices.html: initiate load reduction per S2.OP-AB.GRID-0001 Abnormal Grid, Only.

questions.title: 2023 Q89 — EDG Common Mode Failure / Surveillance Scheduling
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Both Salem Units are at 100% power.

• Severe Thunderstorms are expected to arrive onsite in 6 hours

When:

• During the monthly surveillance test on the 1A EDG, the diesel failed to start due to a failed component

• This same component is also installed in all of the other five EDGs

• The CRS enters TS 3.8.1.1 and is making preparations for performing surveillance tests on all of the five EDGs to comply with Tech Spec for common mode failure

Then:

• The Shift Manager instructs the CRSs to schedule the surveillance runs in the most expeditious manner that does NOT violate station procedures and/or commitments

In order to expeditiously complete the EDG surveillance runs prior to the arrival of the severe weather, how should the Shift Manager direct the crews on running the EDGs?

choices.label: A
choices.text: ONE EDG at a time on both units (i.e., 1B and 2B simultaneously).
choices.html: ONE EDG at a time on both units (i.e., 1B and 2B simultaneously).

choices.label: B
choices.text: ONE EDG at a time (i.e., all 5 EDGs in series).
choices.html: ONE EDG at a time (i.e., all 5 EDGs in series).

choices.label: C
choices.text: ALL EDGs at the same time on one unit (i.e., 1B and 1C simultaneously, then 2A, 2B, and 2C simultaneously).
choices.html: ALL EDGs at the same time on one unit (i.e., 1B and 1C simultaneously, then 2A, 2B, and 2C simultaneously).

choices.label: D
choices.text: ALL EDGs at the same time on both units simultaneously.
choices.html: ALL EDGs at the same time on both units simultaneously.

questions.title: 2023 Q90 — Containment Pressure Relief / R12A Failure ODCM
questions.stemText:
Given:

• Salem Unit 2 is at 100% power

• A Containment Pressure Relief is in progress

Then:

• 2R12A, Containment Atmosphere Radiation Monitor, fails due to a power supply failure

• The Containment Pressure Relief is stopped

Based on the above condition, which of the following is ONLY required per the ODCM to recommence the containment pressure relief (assume all other Radiation Monitors are OPERABLE)?

[REFERENCE PROVIDED]

Per the ODCM requirements, the Containment Pressure Relief ...
questions.questionSource: Modified Bank - DC Cook 2018 NRC SRO90

choices.label: A
choices.text: may recommence without any compensatory actions.
choices.html: may recommence without any compensatory actions.

choices.label: B
choices.text: may recommence provided two independent samples of containment are analyzed and two release rate calculations are independently verified.
choices.html: may recommence provided two independent samples of containment are analyzed and two release rate calculations are independently verified.

choices.label: C
choices.text: may recommence provided grab samples are taken at least once per 8 hours and analyzed for principal gaseous emitters.
choices.html: may recommence provided grab samples are taken at least once per 8 hours and analyzed for principal gaseous emitters.

choices.label: D
choices.text: may NOT recommence until 2R12A radiation monitor is restored to OPERABLE status.
choices.html: may NOT recommence until 2R12A radiation monitor is restored to OPERABLE status.

questions.title: 2023 Q91 — RCP Seal Injection Flow / TS 3.5.4
questions.stemText:
Given:

- Salem Unit 2 is at 100% power

- 21 Charging Pump is in service

- Surveillance test S2.OP-ST.RC-0007, Seal Injection Flow, was just completed.

The following test data was recorded:

- Charging Header pressure = 2450 psig

- 2CV55 fully open

- 21 RCP seal injection flow = 10 gpm

- 22 RCP seal injection flow = 10.5 gpm

- 23 RCP seal injection flow = 9.8 gpm

- 24 RCP seal injection flow = 10.2 gpm

Based on the surveillance test data, what action is required?

[REFERENCE PROVIDED]

choices.label: A
choices.text: Restore the inoperable Charging Pump to Operable status within 72 hours per TS 3.5.2 ECCS Subsystems Tavg > 350 °F.
choices.html: Restore the inoperable Charging Pump to Operable status within 72 hours per TS 3.5.2 ECCS Subsystems Tavg > 350 °F.

choices.label: B
choices.text: No action is required, the surveillance test is satisfactory.
choices.html: No action is required, the surveillance test is satisfactory.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Adjust manual seal injection throttle valves to within the limit within 4 hours as required by TS 3.5.4, Seal Injection Flow.
choices.html: Adjust manual seal injection throttle valves to within the limit within 4 hours as required by TS 3.5.4, Seal Injection Flow.

choices.label: D
choices.text: No TS action is required unless the seal injection flows can NOT be adjusted to within the limits within 4 hours.
choices.html: No TS action is required unless the seal injection flows can NOT be adjusted to within the limits within 4 hours.

questions.title: 2023 Q92 — PZR Pressure Channel Failure / NRC Reportability
questions.stemText:
Given:

- Salem Unit 2 is at 100% power

- PZR Pressure Channel I failed and has been removed from service per S2.OP-SO.RPS-0003, Placing Pressurizer Channel in Tripped Condition

Then:

- PZR Pressure Channel III fails LOW

Based on the conditions above, which of the following correctly identifies the MOST limiting time requirement for reportability to the NRC?

[REFERENCE PROVIDED]

choices.label: A
choices.html: 4 hour report under RAL# 11.3.1

choices.label: B
choices.html: 4 hour report under RAL# 11.3.2

choices.label: C
choices.html: 8 hour report under RAL# 11.3.3

choices.label: D
choices.html: 60 day report under RAL# 11.3.4

questions.title: 2023 Q93 — CW Bus Loss / Circulator Trip
questions.stemText:
Given:

- Salem Unit 2 is at 100% power

- 21A CW Pump is C/T for waterbox cleaning

Then:

- The 4KV Infeed breaker to the 24 CW bus fails opens

What action will the CRS direct the crew?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Power operation at 100% may continue since the 24 CW Bus will auto transfer to the 23 CW Bus.
choices.html: Power operation at 100% may continue since the 24 CW Bus will auto transfer to the 23 CW Bus.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Initiate a load reduction to less than 49% power and trip the main turbine per S2.OP-AB.TRB-0001, Turbine Trip Below P-9.
choices.html: Initiate a load reduction to less than 49% power and trip the main turbine per S2.OP-AB.TRB-0001, Turbine Trip Below P-9.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Initiate a load reduction to less than or equal to 83% power per S2.OP-AB.CW-0001, Circulating Water Malfunction.
choices.html: Initiate a load reduction to less than or equal to 83% power per S2.OP-AB.CW-0001, Circulating Water Malfunction.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Trip the reactor and enter 2-EOP-TRIP-1, Reactor Trip or Safety Injection, per S2.OP-AB.CW-0001, Circulating Water Malfunction.
choices.html: Trip the reactor and enter 2-EOP-TRIP-1, Reactor Trip or Safety Injection, per S2.OP-AB.CW-0001, Circulating Water Malfunction.

questions.title: 2023 Q94 — Shift Complement / Unexpected Absence
questions.stemText:
Given:

- Salem Unit 2 is at 100% power

- The crew is at minimum shift complement

Then:

- A shift change is in progress

- An on-duty Unit 2 Reactor Operator reports that his relief is unable to report to work

- There are NO available qualified licensed ROs on-site

In accordance with OP-AA-101-111, Roles and Responsibilities of On-Shift Personnel, which of the following completes both statements?

1. The on-duty Unit 2 RO shift position (1) be unmanned upon shift change.

2. The shift complement may be one less than the minimum requirements for a period not to exceed (2) .

choices.label: A
choices.html: (1) can (2) 2 hours

choices.label: B
choices.html: (1) can (2) 1 hour

choices.label: C
choices.html: (1) can NOT (2) 1 hour

choices.label: D
choices.html: (1) can NOT (2) 2 hours

questions.title: 2023 Q95 — FHB BLDG AIR D/P LO / Fuel Handling
questions.stemText:
Given:

- Salem Unit 2 is in MODE 6

- Conditions are established per S2.OP-IO.ZZ-0010, Spent Fuel Movements, to support fuel handling operations in the Fuel Handling Building (FHB)

- Movement of irradiated fuel in the FHB is in progress

Then:

- FHB console alarm, BLDG AIR D/P LO, is Alarming

- No operator action has been taken

Based on the above condition and per S2.OP-AR.ZZ-0011, 2CC1 Console Alarm, which of the following completes both statements?

1) What is the NEXT required action?

2) If the BLDG AIR D/P LO alarm is INVALID and will NOT clear, then the local indicator (2) be used to continuously monitor FHB D/P.

choices.label: A
choices.text: (1) Validate the alarm using the local indicator. (2) may NOT
choices.html: (1) Validate the alarm using the local indicator. (2) may NOT

choices.label: B
choices.text: (1) Suspend movement of irradiated fuel assemblies. (2) may
choices.html: (1) Suspend movement of irradiated fuel assemblies. (2) may

choices.label: C
choices.text: (1) Suspend movement of irradiated fuel assemblies. (2) may NOT
choices.html: (1) Suspend movement of irradiated fuel assemblies. (2) may NOT

choices.label: D
choices.text: (1) Validate the alarm using the local indicator. (2) may
choices.html: (1) Validate the alarm using the local indicator. (2) may

questions.title: 2023 Q96 — Online Work Management / SM Responsibilities
questions.stemText:
Per WC-AA-101, On-Line Work Management Process, which of the following choices are responsibilities of the Shift Manager in support of online maintenance?

1. Ensures site FLEX capability is maintained.

2. Ensures risk is reassessed when an emergent condition results in a plant configuration not previously assessed.

3. Ensures Protected Equipment barriers are applied per OP-AA-108-116, Protected Equipment Program.

4. Manages special or complex projects during on-line implementation.

choices.label: B
choices.html: 1, 2, and 3 Only

choices.label: C
choices.html: 2 and 3 Only

choices.label: D
choices.html: 1 and 4 Only

questions.title: 2023 Q97 — RCS Leak / CVCS 2CV6 / TS 3.4.7.2
questions.stemText:
Given:

- Salem Unit 2 is at 100% power

At Time 0500:

- The crew enters S2.OP-AB.RC-0001, RCS Leak, for a suspected RCS leak

At Time 0505:

- The estimated leak rate is 12 gpm

- The leak is identified from the 2CV6, Letdown Relief Valve

At Time 0516:

- Letdown is isolated and the leakage has stopped

Which of the following completes both statements?

1. At time 0505, what is the status of TS 3.4.7.2, Operational Leakage?

2. At time 0516, the CRS __ required to make an Emergency Declaration.

[REFERENCE PROVIDED]

choices.label: A
choices.html: (1) NOT Met (2) is NOT

choices.label: B
choices.html: (1) NOT Met (2) is

choices.label: C
choices.html: (1) Met (2) is

choices.label: D
choices.html: (1) Met (2) is NOT

questions.title: 2023 Q98 — CVCS Monitor Tank Release / 2R18 Inoperable
questions.stemText:
Given:

- Salem Unit 2 is at 100% power

- A release of 21 CVCS Monitor Tank needs to be performed

- 2R18, Radioactive Liquid Effluent Monitor, was declared inoperable 3 days ago

Which of the following completes both statements?

1. Before performing the liquid radioactive waste release, the (1) authorizes the discharge.

2. Based on the above condition, the ODCM requires that __(2)__

Note: ODCM = Offsite Dose Calculation Manual

choices.label: A
choices.text: (1) Shift Manager/Control Room Supervisor (2) at least two independent samples of the monitor tank are analyzed prior to the release
choices.html: (1) Shift Manager/Control Room Supervisor (2) at least two independent samples of the monitor tank are analyzed prior to the release

choices.label: B
choices.text: (1) Shift Manager/Control Room Supervisor (2) continuous surveys of the discharge piping are performed during the release
choices.html: (1) Shift Manager/Control Room Supervisor (2) continuous surveys of the discharge piping are performed during the release

choices.label: C
choices.text: (1) Radiation Protection Manager (2) continuous surveys of the discharge piping are performed during the release
choices.html: (1) Radiation Protection Manager (2) continuous surveys of the discharge piping are performed during the release

choices.label: D
choices.text: (1) Radiation Protection Manager (2) at least two independent samples of the monitor tank are analyzed prior to the release
choices.html: (1) Radiation Protection Manager (2) at least two independent samples of the monitor tank are analyzed prior to the release

questions.title: 2023 Q99 — Containment Radiation CFST / FRCE-3
questions.stemText:
Given:

- The STA is monitoring Critical Safety Function Status trees

Which of the following completes the statement?

If Containment Radiation Monitors (1) exceeds 2 R/HR, the CRS (2) 2-EOP-FRCE-3, Response to High Containment Radiation Level.

choices.label: A
choices.html: (1) R10A/B (2) must GO TO

choices.label: B
choices.html: (1) R44A/B (2) must GO TO

choices.label: C
choices.text: (1) R44A/B (2) may elect to GO TO
choices.html: (1) R44A/B (2) may elect to GO TO

choices.label: D
choices.text: (1) R10A/B (2) may elect to GO TO
choices.html: (1) R10A/B (2) may elect to GO TO

questions.title: 2023 Q100 — Fire Pumps Inoperable / Hope Creek Cross-Tie
questions.stemText:
Redacted Due to Security-Related Information

Given:

- A truck accident has rendered BOTH Salem Fire Pumps INOPERABLE

- The crew is implementing S2.OP-AB.FP-0001, Fire Protection System Malfunction

In accordance with S2.OP-AB.FP-0001, which of the following completes both statements?

1. What action will the CRS direct?

2. If the action cannot be established within 24 hours, then the unit must be in ____ within the next 6 hours?

choices.label: A
choices.text: (1) Open the backup supply cross-tie valves from Hope Creek. (2) HOT STANDBY
choices.html: (1) Open the backup supply cross-tie valves from Hope Creek. (2) HOT STANDBY

choices.label: B
choices.text: (1) Open the backup supply cross-tie valves from Hope Creek. (2) HOT SHUTDOWN
choices.html: (1) Open the backup supply cross-tie valves from Hope Creek. (2) HOT SHUTDOWN

choices.label: C
choices.text: (1) Redacted Due to Security-Related Information (2) Redacted Due to Security-Related Information
choices.html: (1) Redacted Due to Security-Related Information (2) Redacted Due to Security-Related Information

choices.label: D
choices.text: (1) Redacted Due to Security-Related Information (2) HOT STANDBY
choices.html: (1) Redacted Due to Security-Related Information (2) HOT STANDBY
